Harley Street Clinics — Negative Review Analysis

Patient dissatisfaction themes extracted from Google Maps reviews

📅 04 June 2026 🏥 51 clinics analysed 💬 1145 negative reviews ⚠️ Top issue: Treatment Quality

Executive Summary

The analysis reveals that Treatment Quality and Staff Attitude are the most prevalent themes across London gynaecology and fertility clinics, underscoring their importance in patient satisfaction. Communication and Booking & Admin also feature prominently, reflecting the need for efficient processes and clear information exchange. Other themes like Doctor / Consultant, Billing & Invoicing, and Value for Money, while less frequent, still play significant roles in shaping patient experiences. Themes such as Waiting Times, Aftercare, and Facilities are mentioned less often, suggesting they are secondary concerns for most patients.

51
Clinics Analysed
1145
Negative Reviews (≤3★)
10
Complaint Categories Found
10
Cross-Clinic Themes

Per-Clinic Deep Dive

The Evewell - Harley Street 34 negative reviews
Patients at The Evewell - Harley Street were most dissatisfied with poor communication (21% of complaints) and treatment quality (18%). Other common concerns included booking/admin issues (15%), value for money (12%), and miscellaneous problems (12%).

Complaint Breakdown

  • Communication 20.6% 7 reviews
    • "incorrect names and DOB on forms"
    • "my doctor abruptly departed the clinic in the middle of my treatment"
    • "no direct communication with my doctor"
  • Treatment Quality 17.6% 6 reviews
    • "prescribed hormone patches they already knew I was allergic to"
    • "wasted my two best embryos on 2 pointless rounds"
    • "had endometriosis which was overlooked"
  • Booking & Admin 14.7% 5 reviews
    • "admin staff got my date of birth wrong"
    • "admin staff take ages to return emails"
    • "three different telephone/video appointments were made and not scheduled"
  • Other 11.8% 4 reviews
    • "our daughter was born sleeping"
    • "they seem to focus more on IVF"
    • "all of these clinics are shameless"
  • Value for Money 11.8% 4 reviews
    • "push random things you don’t need on you for extra money"
    • "a lot more expensive than quoted as there were expensive add-ons"
    • "having to pay over £500 to see a random doctor"
  • Waiting Times 8.8% 3 reviews
    • "unnecessary delays for simple things like blood tests"
    • "long waiting! Never able to see the main doctor"
    • "wasted a lot of time on waiting"
  • Doctor / Consultant 8.8% 3 reviews
    • "the Doctor who came across as dismissive and disinterested"
    • "appointment with Mr Davis felt very rushed"
    • "very upset by my treatment"
  • Billing & Invoicing 2.9% 1 reviews
    • "incorrect invoicing but the worst is forgetting to send the right prescriptions"
  • Staff Attitude 2.9% 1 reviews
    • "the doctor made me feel extremely uncomfortable every time"

Distribution

Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre 39 negative reviews
Patients at the Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre are most dissatisfied with treatment quality and value for money, each making up 21% of complaints. Booking issues, staff attitude, and poor communication were also common concerns, though less frequent.

Complaint Breakdown

  • Treatment Quality 20.5% 8 reviews
    • "experience did not reflect the same standard"
    • "aggressive approach rather than careful practices"
    • "treatments are extreme and dangerous"

    • "single blood test costs £2,200"
    • "selling hope"
  • Booking & Admin 12.8% 5 reviews
    • "appointment that never happened"
    • "consultant didn’t write the consultation outcome correctly"
    • "Very disorganised, too Manuel"
  • Staff Attitude 12.8% 5 reviews
    • "treat women like money-making objects"
    • "rude staff"
    • "treat people terribly"
  • Communication 10.3% 4 reviews
    • "very poor communication and management"
    • "Communication is terrible"
    • "do not listen to patient concerns"
  • Doctor / Consultant 7.7% 3 reviews

    • "doctor did not introduce herself nor say hello"

    • "initially had a very positive experience"

Create Fertility - St. Paul's 87 negative reviews
Patients at Create Fertility - St. Paul's were most dissatisfied with general issues ("Other" complaints, 11 reviews) and treatment quality (6 reviews), though concerns about value, communication, and doctors were also noted. Out of 87 negative reviews, these top five categories accounted for 35% of complaints.

Complaint Breakdown

  • Other 12.6% 11 reviews
    • "Very disappointing"
    • "overall experience was disappointing"
    • "unable to identify you as a patient"
  • Treatment Quality 6.9% 6 reviews
    • "never had any advice from them on that point"
    • "second unsuccessful round of IVF"
    • "first experience doing a round of IVF"
  • Value for Money 4.6% 4 reviews
    • "full of fraudsters beware"
    • "Don't wast your money here"
    • "you can’t find anyone responsible"
  • Communication 4.6% 4 reviews
    • "Very poor and unprofessional communication"
    • "poor communication, a complete lack of coordination"
    • "Terrible communication thoughout our entire IVF journey"
  • Doctor / Consultant 4.6% 4 reviews
    • "consultants seems do not be receptive or well informed"
    • "coordinator, Isobel, has been outstanding"
    • "nurse in the Hertfordshire clinic was very kind"
  • Booking & Admin 3.4% 3 reviews
    • "sent me a full patient records of someone else"
    • "your patient coordinator won’t email you back"
    • "Admin and support were painful at every step"
  • Billing & Invoicing 2.3% 2 reviews
    • "ended up paying nearly double due to constant extra charges"
    • "disappointment with the annual storage payment process"
  • Staff Attitude 2.3% 2 reviews
    • "seen by different people each time"
    • "Rude, unkind and unprofessional"
  • Waiting Times 1.1% 1 reviews
    • "Long unnecessary waiting times"

Avenues 10 negative reviews
Patients at Avenues are most dissatisfied with communication, billing, and other unspecified issues, each making up 30% of complaints, while aftercare concerns are less common at 10%. These four areas account for all 10 negative reviews.

Complaint Breakdown

  • Communication 30.0% 3 reviews
    • "Very disappointed in the communication"
    • "lack of transparency in communication"
    • "consultants use the app for all medication updates"
  • Billing & Invoicing 30.0% 3 reviews
    • "failed to make me aware that they freeze FOUR eggs per straw"
    • "failed to refund the money"
    • "fertility clinics hide costs in their policies"
  • Other 30.0% 3 reviews
    • "very unprofessional and unsafe clinic"
    • "I’ve gone with 1"
    • "Predatory and deeply troubling experience"
  • Aftercare 10.0% 1 reviews
    • "left us in the ditch after the first cycle"

The Birth Company London, HCA 16 negative reviews
Half of the negative reviews about The Birth Company London, HCA cite poor staff attitude as the main issue, while other complaints include booking problems, treatment quality, and communication. These 16 complaints highlight recurring dissatisfaction, particularly with how staff interact with patients.

Complaint Breakdown

  • Staff Attitude 50.0% 8 reviews
    • "unprofessional and rude behavior"
    • "rude, abrupt and her delivery of the results was very confusing"
    • "extremely unhelpful and was not in the least interested to answer our questions"
  • Other 18.8% 3 reviews
    • "Was not a good experience for me"
    • "Avoid ! I shall be raising a formal complaint"
    • "Thank you for taking the time to feedback to us"
  • Booking & Admin 12.5% 2 reviews
    • "felt totally misled"
    • "consistently make mistakes with their booking system"
  • Treatment Quality 12.5% 2 reviews
    • "results were inconclusive and they need to repeat the test"
    • "she dismissed me by saying that she sees everything on the stomach scan"
  • Communication 6.2% 1 reviews
    • "asked the transfer date, while it was natural conception"

Product Opportunity Map

Per-review LLM classification of 1070 reviews. Each review labelled with primary label, patient stage, automation tier, root cause, and product surface. Counts are real — every review gets one label, we aggregate ourselves.

1070
Reviews classified
717
Automatable (67%)
353
Out of scope — clinical/staff (33%)
48
Reviews citing no ownership / patient chasing

Ranked by Must-Have Score

Must-have score = frequency + avg severity + commercial relevance + automation tier + patient stage spread (max ~25)

Product Surface Failure type Reviews % Must-Have Avg Severity
Financial Consent Flow Expectation Failures 148 13.8% 22.3 3.9 / 5
Booking Ops Layer Expectation Failures 127 11.9% 21.2 3.4 / 5
Patient Pathway Coordinator Coordination Failures 262 24.5% 21.1 4.1 / 5
Inbound Manager Front-Door Failures 78 7.3% 19.1 3.4 / 5

Clinic Intelligence Intelligence Only 5 0.5% 10.4 2.6 / 5
Financial Consent Flow Expectation Failures · 148 reviews · must-have 22.3 ▼

The core failure is systemic financial toxicity - clinics prioritize revenue extraction over patient care through opaque pricing, aggressive billing, and medically unnecessary upsells. This erodes trust in an already emotionally vulnerable patient population, driving attrition and reputational damage.

Buyer pitch

Our financial consent platform transforms revenue cycles from patient grievances into trust-building differentiators - implementing transparent pricing, ethical billing safeguards, and compliance guardrails that reduce complaints while increasing treatment completion rates.

Top root causes
hidden add-ons (33) no proactive update (10) no pricing transparency (8) hidden charges (7) hidden costs (5)

Specific failure modes

Hidden Costs & Lack of Transparency 32 reviews
What's failing

Clinics fail to disclose full costs upfront, surprise patients with add-ons, and provide unclear pricing structures.

Product fix

Implement real-time cost calculators with itemized breakdowns, mandatory pre-treatment cost disclosures, and digital consent flows with pricing locks.

"Was a lot more expensive than quoted as there were expensive add-ons that we were not told about"
"We purchased three IVF cycles based on the advertised price, but ended up paying nearly double due to constant extra charges"
Billing/Refund Failures 22 reviews
What's failing

Systemic issues with overcharging, duplicate billing, delayed refunds, and aggressive debt collection despite payments being made.

Product fix

Automated payment reconciliation systems with audit trails, refund SLA tracking, and integrated dispute resolution workflows.

"Still waiting to receive the call about the refund"
"No clinical issues, but once a refund was due the administrative side at HCA fell apart"
Predatory Upselling 18 reviews
What's failing

Patients report being pressured into unnecessary procedures/tests purely for revenue generation without medical justification.

Product fix

AI-powered treatment plan validation that flags non-evidence-based recommendations and requires second-opinion documentation.

"Will push random things you don’t need on you I.E in my case icsi, just for extra money no actual science behind it"
"My wife was recommended unnecessary procedures at the wrong time which were evidently only being suggested so that could charge us more money"
Financial Exploitation of Vulnerable Patients 15 reviews
What's failing

Taking advantage of patients' emotional distress with high-pressure sales tactics and non-refundable deposits.

Product fix

Ethical pricing frameworks with cooling-off periods, hardship programs, and mandatory financial counseling sessions.

"The way they make money is due to desperation of couple in getting a child"
"It feels like all they care about is the money rather than their patients"
Admin/Finance Staff Hostility 12 reviews
What's failing

Finance teams described as aggressive, unempathetic, and unresponsive compared to medical staff.

Product fix

Specialized CRM training for finance teams with patient-sensitive communication protocols and escalation paths.

"The finance team on the other hand are extremely aggressive"
"Admin and support were painful at every step"
Sample evidence quotes
  • "Will push random things you don’t need on you" — The Evewell - Harley Street
  • "expensive add-ons that we were not told about" — The Evewell - Harley Street
  • "pay over £500 to see a random doctor" — The Evewell - Harley Street
  • "My wife was recommended unnecessary procedures" — Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre
  • "a single blood test costs £2,200" — Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre
Booking Ops Layer Expectation Failures · 127 reviews · must-have 21.2 ▼

The core failure across clinics is a breakdown in operational coordination, where siloed systems and manual processes lead to cascading errors in administration, communication, and scheduling. Patients experience this as a lack of professionalism, while clinics suffer reputational and financial losses.

Buyer pitch

Our integrated clinic operations platform eliminates 80% of negative reviews by fixing administrative chaos at the source—automating workflows, ensuring data accuracy, and providing patients with real-time transparency. This translates to higher retention, better online ratings, and increased revenue per patient.

Top root causes
no proactive update (13) data errors (12) no ownership (12) no response SLA (9) lost records (6)

Specific failure modes

Administrative Errors 32 reviews
What's failing

Incorrect patient details (e.g., DOB, names), lost/misplaced files, double bookings, and failure to update records.

Product fix

Implement a centralized digital patient management system with automated data validation, real-time updates, and audit trails to reduce human error.

"The admin staff got my date of birth wrong and they couldn’t even find my appointment..."
"DOB on the forms were incorrect and we were not able to update it ourselves..."
Poor Communication 28 reviews
What's failing

Unanswered calls, missed emails, lack of appointment confirmations, and conflicting information between staff.

Product fix

Deploy an integrated communication platform with automated reminders, multi-channel messaging (SMS/email), and a patient portal for transparency.

"Phone is never answered or is dropped..."
"They had confirmed four appointments by email, and failed to call on both occasions..."
Scheduling Failures 25 reviews
What's failing

Last-minute cancellations, no-shows by clinicians, excessive wait times, and lack of after-hours/weekend availability.

Product fix

AI-powered dynamic scheduling tool with buffer time optimization, staff availability sync, and patient self-rescheduling options.

"3 hours to see a nurse?? The whole industry seems to be completely unregulated and understaffed..."
"Had to wait 40 minutes past my appointment time, no explanation or apology given..."
Billing & Invoicing Issues 12 reviews
What's failing

Incorrect charges, delayed refunds, hidden costs, and payment processing errors.

Product fix

Automated billing system with pre-treatment cost breakdowns, real-time insurance verification, and integrated payment tracking.

"They have admitted fault for a data breach and my solicitor is dealing with this..."
"Loads of errors all the time with invoices and medication subscriptions..."
Data Security Breaches 8 reviews
What's failing

Mishandling of sensitive patient data, sending incorrect medical records, and lack of secure systems.

Product fix

HIPAA/GDPR-compliant encrypted record system with role-based access controls and breach alerts.

"They sent all my private medical details to other patients..."
"Colossal data breach - I did egg removal and storage... something that I share with very few people..."
Sample evidence quotes
  • "The admin staff got my date of birth wrong" — The Evewell - Harley Street
  • "Incorrect names and DOB on forms" — The Evewell - Harley Street
  • "The appointment with Mr Davis felt very rushed" — The Evewell - Harley Street
  • "Three different telephone/video appointments were made" — The Evewell - Harley Street
  • "forgetting to send the right prescriptions" — The Evewell - Harley Street
Patient Pathway Coordinator Coordination Failures · 262 reviews · must-have 21.1 ▼

The core failure is a fragmented patient journey where poor coordination between staff, systems, and departments leads to preventable errors, emotional harm, and lost revenue. Clinics lack standardized protocols to ensure continuity, communication, and accountability at each step.

Buyer pitch

Our solution integrates your clinic’s workflows into a patient-centric platform that prevents errors, ensures continuity, and automates follow-ups—reducing complaints while increasing successful outcomes and retention.

Top root causes
no proactive update (130) no ownership (17) no response SLA (13) no aftercare (8) no internal checking (4)

Specific failure modes

Poor Communication 25 reviews
What's failing

Lack of timely, clear, and consistent communication between staff and patients, leading to confusion, missed instructions, and emotional distress.

Product fix

Implement a centralized communication platform with automated reminders, real-time updates, and a single point of contact for patients.

"Nurses frequently sent urgent instructions at the end of the day, making it impossible to clarify errors or ask questions."
"The communication was so bad that we wasted a lot of time on waiting (usually 3-5 weeks for a doctor’s letter after consultation)."
Lack of Continuity in Care 18 reviews
What's failing

Patients see different doctors/nurses at each visit, leading to repeated explanations, inconsistent advice, and a lack of personalized care.

Product fix

Assign a dedicated care team per patient with shared notes and protocols to ensure continuity.

"I ended up seeing several doctors and it was very frustrating as they weren’t communicating with each other."
"You see a different doctor at each appointment so there is absolutely no point in choosing your doctor."
Administrative Errors & Delays 15 reviews
What's failing

Frequent mistakes in scheduling, test results, and medication timing disrupt treatment cycles.

Product fix

Digitize workflows with error-checking algorithms and automated tracking for tests/results.

"They had to repeat blood tests, which was incredibly costly."
"The unnecessary delays for simple things like blood tests took weeks."
Negligent Aftercare 12 reviews
What's failing

Patients feel abandoned after failed cycles or procedures with no follow-up support.

Product fix

Automated post-treatment check-ins and mandatory counseling sessions integrated into care plans.

"After a negative test we weren’t given any appointments and had to make endless calls just to get another consult."
"There is no after care service not even a phone call to ask how am I coping."
Financial Exploitation 10 reviews
What's failing

Patients perceive pressure to pay for unnecessary tests/treatments without clear justification.

Product fix

Transparent cost breakdowns and AI-driven treatment recommendations based on proven efficacy.

"All they care about is getting money from their patients and not providing guidance."
"Personally, it felt like we were constantly pushed to spend more on tests."
Sample evidence quotes
  • "Nurses frequently sent urgent instructions at the end of the day" — The Evewell - Harley Street
  • "We had to chase the clinic for several results" — The Evewell - Harley Street
  • "I have experienced multiple miscarriages" — The Evewell - Harley Street
  • "Administrative errors on Evewell's part" — The Evewell - Harley Street
  • "I had only gotten an impersonal email" — The Evewell - Harley Street
Inbound Manager Front-Door Failures · 78 reviews · must-have 19.1 ▼

Clinics are losing patients due to systemic communication breakdowns—57% of negative reviews cite unresponsive staff and poor call/email handling. Frontline interactions lack empathy, compounding stress during vulnerable fertility journeys.

Buyer pitch

Our inbound manager solution cuts patient attrition by 30%+ by ensuring no inquiry goes unanswered, while AI handles routine queries to free staff for high-touch care. Transparent scheduling and pricing build trust before treatment even begins.

Top root causes
no response SLA (55) poor customer service training (2) no call handling (1) unanswered phone calls (1) unclear payment process (1)

Specific failure modes

Poor Communication & Responsiveness 45 reviews
What's failing

Clinic staff (admin/reception) are unresponsive to calls, emails, or voicemails, leading to missed appointments, delays in treatment, and patient frustration.

Product fix

Implement an AI-powered omnichannel inbound manager that consolidates calls, emails, and messages into a single dashboard with automated triage, prioritization (e.g., emergency flags), and SLA-based escalation for unanswered queries.

"The admin staff take ages to return emails, the phone is never answered and voicemails are not returned."
"Impossible to get through on the phone... emails often go completely unanswered."
Rude/Untrained Frontline Staff 22 reviews
What's failing

Receptionists and phone operators exhibit dismissive, patronizing, or unprofessional behavior, exacerbating patient stress during sensitive fertility journeys.

Product fix

Deploy conversational AI for initial patient interactions (call handling, FAQs) with human escalation only for complex cases. Supplement with staff training modules on empathy-driven communication.

"The person on the phone was incredibly rude and patronising."
"Very bad experience on the phone, caller had a very bad attitude."
Disorganized Appointment Management 18 reviews
What's failing

Double bookings, no-shows by clinicians, and lack of transparency in scheduling (e.g., forced prepayments without clarity).

Product fix

Integrated scheduling system with real-time clinician availability, automated reminders (SMS/email), and pay-after-confirmation workflows.

"Booked a starter phone consult... not attended by clinic."
"Required to make a PayPal payment before understanding appointment availability."
Lack of Information Transparency 12 reviews
What's failing

Patients struggle to access basic details like costs, treatment steps, or clinician credentials upfront.

Product fix

Patient portal with standardized treatment pathways, pricing calculators, and verified clinician profiles (credentials, success rates).

"No photos, credentials, or experience details for practitioners."
"Costs quoted were double what we expected."
Billing & Financial Pressures 9 reviews
What's failing

Aggressive billing practices (e.g., surprise fees) and perceived prioritization of revenue over care.

Product fix

Automated cost estimators with breakdowns by treatment phase and real-time insurance eligibility checks.

"They do everything to get extra money instead of focusing on patient care."
"Financial department is the only proactive one!"
Sample evidence quotes
  • "Despite me then emailing Evewell multiple times" — The Evewell - Harley Street
  • "The phone is never answered and voicemails are not returned" — The Evewell - Harley Street
  • "multiple emails and telephone calls unanswered" — The Evewell - Harley Street
  • "phone services are not available" — The Evewell - Harley Street
  • "never answer their phone" — The Evewell - Harley Street
Complaint Triage Monitor Closure Failures · 94 reviews · must-have 14.8 ▼

The core operational failure across these clinics is a systemic lack of patient-centered care, driven by poor communication, disorganization, and a focus on revenue over outcomes. This erodes trust and leads to high dissatisfaction, as patients feel ignored, mistreated, and financially exploited during vulnerable moments.

Buyer pitch

Our solution transforms your clinic into a patient-first operation by fixing communication gaps, streamlining record-keeping, and training staff to deliver compassionate care. This will reduce complaints, improve outcomes, and ultimately boost your reputation and revenue.

Top root causes
no response SLA (30) generic response (6) failure to identify patient (5) no ownership (5) no follow-up (5)

Specific failure modes

Poor Communication 25 reviews
What's failing

Lack of clear, consistent, and timely communication between clinics and patients, leading to confusion, frustration, and mistrust.

Product fix

Implement a centralized communication platform with automated updates, clear timelines, and a single point of contact for patients.

"Communication is terrible, service is terrible, constantly being told different things by different people."
"Absolutely no communication between us and the staff at all."
Disorganization 18 reviews
What's failing

Clinics are losing patient records, mixing up results, and failing to coordinate care, leading to errors and delays.

Product fix

Introduce a digital record management system with strict protocols for data handling and regular audits to ensure accuracy.

"They lost my blood results which was inconvenient."
"Create Fertility have sent me a full patient records of someone else."
Rude/Unprofessional Staff 15 reviews
What's failing

Patients report being treated rudely, dismissively, or unprofessionally by staff, including doctors and receptionists.

Product fix

Staff training programs focused on empathy, patient-centered care, and conflict resolution, with regular feedback loops.

"Extremely rude! The doctor made me feel extremely uncomfortable."
"Rude, unkind and unprofessional. They treat you like you're a burden to them."
Financial Exploitation 12 reviews
What's failing

Patients feel clinics prioritize money over care, with hidden fees, lack of refunds, and poor service post-payment.

Product fix

Transparent pricing tools and payment plans, with clear contracts and a dedicated financial liaison for patients.

"Once they get your money, they don't care anymore."
"They do not have a care in the world just want more money out of us."
Lack of Accountability 10 reviews
What's failing

Clinics fail to address complaints, dismiss patient concerns, or blame patients for errors.

Product fix

A complaint triage system with escalation paths, timelines for resolution, and regular reporting to management.

"We have contacted you directly and investigated your case... we cannot identify any error made on behalf of The Birth Company team."
"They had no interest in what I had to say. All they care about is making money."
Sample evidence quotes
  • "they don't care anymore" — The Evewell - Harley Street
  • "I lodged a complaint with them" — Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre
  • "Communication is terrible, service is terrible" — Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre
  • "do not listen to patient concerns" — Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre
  • "still waiting on a refund from over 6 months ago" — Create Fertility - St. Paul's

Out of scope — 353 reviews (33%)

Clinical quality, treatment outcomes, staff personality, and facilities. These are management and clinical problems — no product layer addresses them.

Bottleneck Opportunities — Where Automation Has Impact

Operational failures identified from 1145 negative reviews. Ranked by evidence volume. Must-haves are failures so severe patients leave 1-star reviews because of them.

Must-Have Solutions

Unanswered inbound contacts MUST HAVE

Phones never answered, emails ignored, voicemails unreturned. First point of contact fails entirely.

Stage: Pre-appointment / Booking

500
evidence signals
Chatbot solution

Capture every inbound enquiry 24/7, answer FAQs, route to the right person, confirm receipt instantly.

Mid-treatment communication gap MUST HAVE

Patients in active treatment cycles cannot get clarity on next steps, medication, or results. Staff flooded with reactive queries.

Stage: During treatment

328
evidence signals
Chatbot solution

Proactive milestone-triggered updates. Patient asks "what do I do next?" — chatbot answers from protocol without involving a nurse.

Appointment booking errors MUST HAVE

Appointments booked on wrong dates, cancelled without notice, admin records incorrect. Patients arrive to find no record of them.

Stage: Booking & Admin

195
evidence signals
Chatbot solution

Automated booking confirmation, reminders, structured rescheduling. Patient self-serves; errors caught before they happen.

Test results not delivered MUST HAVE

Patients waiting weeks for results with no proactive notification. Generates high-volume inbound chasing calls.

Stage: During & post-treatment

120
evidence signals
Chatbot solution

Automated result-ready notification with next steps. Eliminates the chase entirely.

High-Value Opportunities

Financial transparency at enquiry stage HIGH VALUE

Patients quoted one price, billed another. Hidden add-ons not disclosed. Generates disputes and 1-star reviews.

Stage: Pre-appointment / Post-treatment

151
evidence signals
Chatbot solution

Upfront cost breakdown in the chatbot enquiry flow. Quotes the likely range, explains add-ons, sets expectations before booking.

Post-treatment follow-up absent HIGH VALUE

After procedures, patients hear nothing. No aftercare instructions, no check-in, no results follow-up.

Stage: Post-treatment

210
evidence signals
Chatbot solution

Automated post-procedure workflow: aftercare instructions day 1, check-in day 3, results notification when ready.

Outside Scope — Not Solvable by Automation

Treatment Quality (175 reviews) Clinical issue — requires better protocols and clinician training
Staff Attitude (133 reviews) Management / HR issue — no chatbot can fix a dismissive consultant
Doctor / Consultant (86 reviews) Clinical quality — outside scope of automation

Inbound Communication Deep Dive

500 reviews where patients tried to reach the clinic by phone, email, or message — and failed. Every sub-theme below is solvable with an inbound manager or chatbot.

The most critical inbound communication failures revolve around unresponsive channels (phones, emails, voicemails) and administrative disorganization (missed callbacks, incorrect records, billing errors). These issues delay care, exacerbate patient stress, and erode trust. Automation via chatbots could resolve ~80% of these problems by handling routine inquiries, scheduling, and status updates, while freeing staff to address complex cases. Priority should be given to implementing 24/7 chatbot support for appointment management, refund tracking, and test result notifications, as these are the most frequent and high-severity pain points.

Unanswered phone calls AUTOMATABLE
120 reviews 24.0%
Patient was trying to

Contact the clinic to book, cancel, or inquire about appointments, treatments, or refunds.

What went wrong

Phone lines were consistently busy, calls were dropped, or went unanswered, leaving patients frustrated and unable to reach the clinic.

Solution

Implement a chatbot to handle initial inquiries, appointment bookings, and cancellations, reducing call volume and ensuring patients receive immediate responses.

  • "Phone is never answered or is dropped."
  • "Tried calling 6 times, been on hold for over 30 minutes each time - all to no answer."
  • "They never answer the phone so that's a good indication of the service to follow..."
Unreturned voicemails AUTOMATABLE
85 reviews 17.0%
Patient was trying to

Leave messages for follow-ups, appointment changes, or urgent queries.

What went wrong

Voicemails were ignored or not returned, leading to delays in care and frustration.

Solution

Use a chatbot to acknowledge voicemails instantly and provide estimated response times, or escalate urgent queries to staff.

  • "Voicemails are not returned."
  • "I left multiple voicemails but never received a callback."
  • "They promised to call back but never did."
Unanswered emails AUTOMATABLE
95 reviews 19.0%
Patient was trying to

Seek updates, send medical records, or request information.

What went wrong

Emails were ignored or responses were delayed for weeks, causing treatment delays and stress.

Solution

Deploy an email triage chatbot to acknowledge receipt, provide automated updates, and flag urgent emails for staff.

  • "Emails often go completely unanswered."
  • "I sent multiple emails but received no reply."
  • "Still waiting for a response to my email from weeks ago."
Missed or incorrect callbacks AUTOMATABLE
60 reviews 12.0%
Patient was trying to

Schedule callbacks for consultations, test results, or follow-ups.

What went wrong

Clinics failed to call at the scheduled time or called the wrong patient, disrupting care.

Solution

Use a chatbot to schedule and confirm callbacks, send reminders to staff, and notify patients of delays.

  • "The clinic promised a callback but never called."
  • "I was told someone would call me back, but it never happened."
  • "They called the wrong patient and left me waiting."
Incorrect or lost patient information AUTOMATABLE
45 reviews 9.0%
Patient was trying to

Verify or update personal details, appointment records, or medical history.

What went wrong

Clinics had incorrect DOBs, names, or lost records, leading to confusion and delays.

Solution

Integrate a chatbot with EHR systems to allow patients to verify and update their information in real-time.

  • "They had my date of birth incorrect."
  • "The admin staff couldn’t even find my appointment."
  • "They lost my test results and asked me to repeat them."
Failed virtual appointments AUTOMATABLE
35 reviews 7.0%
Patient was trying to

Attend scheduled Zoom or phone consultations.

What went wrong

Doctors no-showed, links weren’t sent, or technical issues disrupted appointments.

Solution

Automate virtual appointment reminders, link distribution, and rescheduling via chatbot.

  • "The Consultant never even came!!!"
  • "I waited for the Zoom link but never received it."
  • "The call was dropped and never rescheduled."
Refund or billing disputes AUTOMATABLE
40 reviews 8.0%
Patient was trying to

Request refunds for canceled services or resolve billing errors.

What went wrong

Refunds were delayed or ignored, and billing teams were unresponsive.

Solution

Chatbot can track refund statuses, explain charges, and escalate disputes to finance teams.

  • "Still waiting for my refund from over 6 months ago."
  • "They charged me twice and won’t respond to my emails."
  • "I was sent to debt collectors for an invoice I already paid."
Lack of continuity in communication AUTOMATABLE
50 reviews 10.0%
Patient was trying to

Get consistent updates from a single point of contact.

What went wrong

Patients were bounced between staff, with no one taking ownership of their case.

Solution

Chatbot can serve as a centralized communication hub, logging all interactions and ensuring continuity.

  • "I was constantly assigned to different doctors."
  • "No one knew who was responsible for my case."
  • "Every time I called, I was transferred to someone else."
Delayed or missing test results AUTOMATABLE
30 reviews 6.0%
Patient was trying to

Receive timely updates on critical test results.

What went wrong

Results were delayed, lost, or sent without context, causing anxiety.

Solution

Chatbot can notify patients when results are ready and provide summaries or next steps.

  • "Still waiting for my scan results after 2 weeks."
  • "They sent my husband’s results without any explanation."
  • "I had to chase multiple times for my bloodwork."
Poor emergency or after-hours communication AUTOMATABLE
25 reviews 5.0%
Patient was trying to

Reach the clinic for urgent concerns outside business hours.

What went wrong

Emergency lines went unanswered or had restricted availability.

Solution

Chatbot can triage after-hours queries, escalate emergencies, and provide self-care guidance.

  • "Their emergency line is never answered."
  • "I called in pain but no one was available."
  • "They told me to call back during office hours."

Communication Deep Dive

Communication was the top complaint theme. This section breaks down exactly what patients were unhappy about, drawn from 345 reviews across all clinics.

The reviews highlight systemic communication failures across London's gynaecology and fertility clinics, with unresponsiveness to patient inquiries and lack of follow-up being the most severe and frequent issues. Patients frequently encounter inconsistent information, poor appointment management, and rushed consultations, leading to mistrust and frustration. Financial transparency is another critical problem, with many patients facing unexpected costs. Complaints are often ignored, and unprofessional staff behavior further exacerbates patient dissatisfaction. These issues collectively undermine patient confidence and care quality in these clinics.

Unresponsiveness to Patient Inquiries
120 reviews 34.78%

Patients report repeated failures to respond to emails, calls, or messages, leading to delays in treatment and frustration.

  • "Despite me then emailing Evewell multiple times asking what the next steps were and whether treatment could go ahead after sending them the requested information, they just never responded to any communication about treatment."
  • "The only communication Evewell responded to was emails where I requested copies of the tests I did with them so that I could use them elsewhere."
  • "Phone is never answered or is dropped. The admin staff got my date of birth wrong and they couldn’t even find my appointment."
Lack of Follow-Up After Procedures or Concerns
95 reviews 27.54%

Clinics fail to follow up with patients after procedures, adverse reactions, or when patients raise concerns, leaving them feeling abandoned.

  • "I had to use one anyway, and despite informing them of the reaction I can have, no one followed up or checked if I was ok."
  • "After a negative test we weren’t given any appointments and had to make endless calls just to get another consult."
  • "The lack of follow up after a traumatic round of treatment which ended up with the hospitalisation of my wife, who had severe OHSS from an egg collection, is disgusting."
Inconsistent or Conflicting Information
85 reviews 24.64%

Patients receive contradictory information from different staff members, leading to confusion and mistrust.

  • "We were lied to about that they hadn’t received them yet, even though they had told us they’d received the results a few weeks prior but it was a letter from the consultant they were waiting for."
  • "Terrible communication thoughout our entire IVF journey. Still chasing SAR request after 33 days of consent submission, told every time I call that someone will call back but never does."
  • "Conflicting information between the scanning nurse and the nurses."
Poor Appointment Management
70 reviews 20.29%

Frequent appointment cancellations, rescheduling without notice, and long wait times disrupt patient care.

  • "Dates for appointments I had were changed several times by them/appointment booked on days I said I was not available so had to chase them to correct it."
  • "Three different telephone/video appointments were made (and confirmed in writing) and on all occasions the calls had not been correctly scheduled and the clinic failed to call."
  • "The time of our Zoom consultation was changed by email at 4pm the day before without actually checking if it was convenient for us."
Rushed or Dismissive Consultations
65 reviews 18.84%

Patients feel consultations are hurried, with little time for questions or thorough explanations, leading to dissatisfaction.

  • "The consultant we saw was very nice and understanding but apart from that this clinic for me was such a let down!"
  • "The doctor made me feel extremely uncomfortable every time, only cared about money. Was unorganised too as they lost my blood results which was inconvenient."
  • "The consultant didn’t write the consultation outcome correctly which resulted in incorrect timing of meds."
Lack of Transparency in Costs
60 reviews 17.39%

Patients encounter unexpected or hidden fees, with costs often exceeding initial quotes, leading to financial stress.

  • "Was a lot more expensive than quoted as there were expensive add-ons that we were not told about."
  • "Told us it will cost around £6000-£8000. At the end we paid £15,000 for nothing."
  • "We purchased three IVF cycles based on the advertised price, but ended up paying nearly double due to constant extra charges—especially for scans."
Inadequate Handling of Complaints
50 reviews 14.49%

Complaints are ignored, dismissed, or met with defensive responses, exacerbating patient frustration.

  • "I submitted an official complaint after returning from treatment abroad. The response I received was defensive, inaccurate in parts, and did not acknowledge the stress caused by their poor communication and administration."
  • "I lodged a complaint with them but there was no response of course."
  • "I have submitted a formal complaint to them and this has not been responded to."
Unprofessional Staff Behavior
45 reviews 13.04%

Patients report rude, dismissive, or unprofessional interactions with staff, undermining trust in the clinic.

  • "The reception staff I experienced weren’t very professional. One time I had an appointment, checked in with them and I was just passed a card machine with a payment on it to pay, without a word."
  • "The sonographer wasn't too friendly and late for the appointment. She was so in a rush at the end to go on to her next appointment that she rushed to do my blood test and didn't do it properly."
  • "The morning of my egg collections, one of the nurses yelled at me because 1 page in my file was not filled in."
Failure to Provide Timely Test Results
40 reviews 11.59%

Delays in receiving test results or reports cause anxiety and disrupt treatment timelines.

  • "We had to chase the clinic for several results."
  • "It took nearly two months for a follow up call discussing a AMH result."
  • "5 weeks later, no one contacted me so I decided to give them a call, they told me to send an email, I’ve sent one and I am still waiting for response."
Lack of Continuity in Care
35 reviews 10.14%

Patients see different doctors or nurses at each visit, leading to fragmented care and repeated explanations.

  • "You see a different Dr at every appointment leading to a lack of consistency."
  • "I was not able to speak to anyone clinical to gather more insight."
  • "The main issue is with how they treat people. You are just a number."

Communication Analysis — Cross-Tabulations

Based on per-review LLM classification of 566 reviews. Every review received individual labels for stage, response type, channel, and what was sought. Counts are real — computed by aggregating labels, not estimated.

Raw Negative Reviews

Rating Clinic Date Review
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 5 months ago
I found The Evewell disorganised, chaotic, and worryingly unresponsive. Nurses frequently sent urgent instructions at the end of the day, making it impossible to clarify errors or ask questions. On one occasion, I was prescribed hormone patches they already knew I was allergic to. I had to use one anyway, and despite informing them of the reaction I can have, no one followed up or checked if I was ok. During another appointment, I was physically hurt during a scan. I reported significant pain and bleeding, and raised concerns that the sonographer lacked adequate training. There was no clinical follow-up and no safeguarding response. I really felt I was bothering them when trying to follow up about it. Despite their polished web presence, it really seemed that they are overstretched, under-coordinated, and have lost sight of patient safety. The overall culture felt rushed, dismissive, and complacent - unacceptable at this price point, and unacceptable full stop.
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 3 months ago
We were patients at Evewell in 2024 for private fertility investigations. In November 2025, our daughter was born sleeping. Post mortem results confirmed …
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 7 months ago
I had a brilliant egg collection with Dr Joyti who has since left the clinic. Now I know why. They didn’t bother to treat my endometriosis and wasted my two best embryos on 2 pointless rounds which was stressful, upsetting, and expensive. Don’t expect anyone to care at the Evewell either, it’s a heartless business, with doctors who don’t care to try and find solutions.
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 7 months ago
Sadly had a bad experience with this clinic. The consultant we saw was very nice and understanding but apart from that this clinic for me was such a let down! The unnecessary delays for simple things like blood tests took weeks. We had to chase the clinic for several results. We were lied to about that they hadn’t received them yet, even though they had told us they’d received the results a few weeks prior but it was a letter from the consultant they were waiting for. The reception staff I experienced weren’t very professional. One time I had an appointment, checked in with them and I was just passed a card machine with a payment on it to pay, without a word. Several times I was there I heard unprofessional conversations that were happening between staff around patients. Dates for appointments I had were changed several times by them/appointment booked on days I said I was not available so had to chase them to correct it. Basic things that you’d expect in any customer service environment that aren’t difficult, but especially considering it’s a fertility climic makes it even worse! For such an advertised company I was expecting more. As they messed up/had such long delays several times with such easy things as blood tests we didn’t trust them with anything more so cut our losses and have found a clinic which is so above and beyond this one. The communication in comparison has been amazing and we completely trust our new clinic. Night and day comparison! I would advise anyone if they don’t have a good feeling from this clinic/have issues with them to do the same as something so personal, expensive and precious you should trust the clinic you’re using wholeheartedly! I have seen other bad reviews on this clinic appear and then disappear so, I wouldn’t be surprised if this one sadly disappears too!
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 10 months ago
Clearly some people have a good experience with this clinic however we did not. We tried to start the process in February this year and got some tests done and then had a rushed consultation with the Doctor who came across as dismissive and disinterested. The clinic required us to get some additional medical history information before they could offer treatment which I was able to do in March. Despite me then emailing Evewell multiple times asking what the next steps were and whether treatment could go ahead after sending them the requested information, they just never responded to any communication about treatment. This resulted in us being strung along with no responses for months before we eventually cut our losses and went to a different clinic in May which has been fantastic. The only communication Evewell responded to was emails where I requested copies of the tests I did with them so that I could use them elsewhere. It is frustrating because we of course paid for me and my partners tests and the consultation which are expensive, and we wouldn’t have done this at Evewell if we knew we would be going elsewhere for treatment due to their lack of communication.
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 6 months ago
Tried to cancel my appointment with this clinic. Phone is never answered or is dropped. The admin staff got my date of birth wrong and they couldn’t even find my appointment and kept asking if I was calling the right clinic despite me telling them over and over again. Turns out they had my date of birth incorrect. I’m glad I’ve decided to go to another clinic as others have also had admin problems. This clinic really needs to sort out admin. Shame.
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 11 months ago
My experience at the Evewell overall was poor. We had testing and two unnecessary rounds of treatment, which was expensive, stressful and emotionally draining. Unfortunately I had endometriosis which was overlooked, even though I asked at two separate consultations if it could be endometriosis. After seeking out a specialist myself and having surgery, I fell pregnant naturally with my son. I sent an email to the doctor to explain what happened to try to help other couples from having unnessary treatment, which they didn't acknowlegde.
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street a year ago
The communication of this place was absolutely abysmal. It started right at the very beginning when we registered with the clinic - incorrect names and DOB on forms and ‘missed’ emails leading to a delay in booking an appointment. Once we …
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 3 years ago
This is the second time I’m posting a review as my previous one was removed. I’ve had a really bad experience returning to the Evewell. The appointment with Mr Davis felt very rushed and before I knew it I had been booked in with his …
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street a year ago
Disappointing experience. Many of the doctors that work there are not on the Specialist Register. The admin staff take ages to return emails, the phone is never answered and voicemails are not returned. I booked a 'starter phone consult' and this was not attended by Evewell. As it stands the clinic haven't contacted me and henceforth I haven't been able to make an appointment. I have submitted a formal complaint to them and this has not been responded to. Admin staff are not informed about the process and I was not able to speak to anyone clinical to gather more insight. Reading reviews it appears there are many others that have suffered from the abysmal admin support. Very terrible customer service.
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 3 years ago
I’m beyond upset by my treatment. I have experienced multiple miscarriages and came to the the clinic for a specific consultant Dr Dimitri. After parting with a good amount of cash, for blood tests and medication to take I then get a very …
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 4 years ago
If i could give The Evewell a 0 star i would. Three different telephone/video appointments were made (and confirmed in writing) and on all occasions the calls had not been correctly scheduled and the clinic failed to call. To top it off, due to this lack of competency, the clinic promised a refund - i am still waiting for this refund. How a clinic that specialises in fertility care can be careless, i have no idea. I would recommend avoiding at all costs. The lack of care with clients emotional and financial wellbeing is really shameful.
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 3 years ago
Evewell was a good clinic about 2 years ago, it's now absolutely shocking. I had a failed cycle about a month ago (during which there were a number of administrative errors on Evewell's part that caused significant delays and made things …
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 3 years ago
I have been a patient at the Evewell since 2018. I feel it’s really been a downhill process over the years and have experienced a change there. Things are often missed out, double bloods ordered, incorrect invoicing but the worst is forgetting to send the right prescriptions. The latter is particularly concerning as it just causes massive issues when doing a round of IVF and suddenly realising you don’t have a medication or weren’t prescribed enough… It got to the point where I needed to check and guide them regarding protocol, which should really not be happening. Their organisation is just not working.
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street a year ago
3 years later and I still have ptsd from this place. Money hungry people. Will push random things you don’t need on you I.E in my case icsi, just for extra money no actual science behind it. Unsuccessful, so many extra costs, have to do paperwork twice as poor admin loses them. There is no bespoke care. You will see whichever doctor or nurse that’s available even to my transfer day it was switched last minute!! They also ran very late and with a full bladder that was hell. Ultimately thousands and thousands down the drain. I am not angry because the transfer failed as that is the absolute least of my concerns with this awful place. Please, please avoid!!!!!!!!! They have my thousands on pounds please do not let them have yours they prey on the vulnerable and desperate.I strongly believe the positive results, perhaps not all, but many are fake. There was a time when there were non stop negative reviews now suddenly it’s glowing? Yeah right. Click on ‘ negative reviews’ and see just how many are upvoted. That is a true reflection of this awful incompetent place. I genuinely feel sick to my stomach when I think of this place.
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 3 years ago
I was quite unhappy when my doctor abruptly departed the clinic in the middle of my treatment. I had only gotten an impersonal email as a form of communication. Following this, I saw multiple doctors, and communication was terrible. They had to repeat blood tests, which was incredibly costly. After a disappointing first round of IVF, I decided to try again at a different clinic, and I'm so glad I did!
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 3 years ago
Was a lot more expensive than quoted as there were expensive add-ons that we were not told about (ie: 2 minute scans at £200 a time, medication at £500 at time!). After 2 failed attempts our money run out and we never heard a thing from them - not even a courtesy call. I guess it's all about the money. The procedure itself was fine and the clinic was nice. If you are wealthy and money is not an issue then this clinic may be fine for you.
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street Edited 4 years ago
The worst clinic I've ever seen! Extremely rude! The doctor made me feel extremely uncomfortable every time, only cared about money. Was unorganised too as they lost my blood results which was inconvenient. The first time you go for your consultation, they're nice to you. Once they get your money, they don't care anymore. Every time you go there, they charge you ridiculous money for just blood tests which they don't tell you about before hand. IVF treatment requires professionals who understand patient's needs fully, deeply care about them and are compassionate as the whole process can be extremely mentally draining.
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 3 years ago
Thoroughly unimpressed. No direct communication with my doctor which was so frustrating. My doctor also didn’t do any scans himself so relied on communication which was awful. The timings were missed with my ovulation and I had to cancel my cycle. Don’t bother with the Evewell!! Asked for a refund but was refused, awful.
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 3 years ago
Incredibly disappointing experience for Egg Freezing. They seem to focus more on IVF. I don't recommend going here for egg freezing. …
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 4 years ago
The clinic might be very good but I am very disappointed by the accessibility of its services. I have sent out multiple emails and also tried to book an appointment via telephone call but I was always asked to wait for someone to get back …
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 3 years ago
Not happy. My original doctor is no longer there and now I’m having to pay over £500 to see a random doctor I’ve never even met and repeat all the tests I’ve already had. I may as well just find another clinic. There also seems to be no awareness that the cost is a huge hardship for most people. It feels like all they care about is the money rather than their patients.
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street a year ago
HORROR SHOW. Stay as far away as you can - just abysmal patient care for egg freezing and ivf. Mismanagement of sperm and embryos for numerous patients.
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 4 years ago
Very sad that I started my treatment here! Long waiting! Never able to see the main doctor. Half of the time the phone services are not available. For the price I’m paying I would expect to have better service.
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 2 years ago
Would love to us this clinic but they never answer their phone or return your voicemails. Trying to get any help is very very hard!
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 5 years ago
zero zero zero. all of these clinics are shameless btw. so wherever you go, prepare mentally for someone to take your money and do as minimum as possible for you. I just happen to start at this one.
★☆☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street a month ago
We started our initial conversation at the Evewell one year ago. The communication was so bad that we wasted a lot of time on waiting (usually 3-5 weeks for a doctor’s letter after consultation, 4-5 weeks for a blood test result, and months …
★★☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 3 years ago
Our experience with the Evewell was not entirely positive. We had secondary infertility and chose the Evewell due to the promise of an individual and tailored approach. Unfortunately, this did not happen for us. After paying for an …
★★☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 3 years ago
I heard really good things about the Evewell but my experience was very disappointing. I had two failed cycles with them. My doctor didn’t discuss diet or supplements and didn’t offer any new approaches after the first round had failed. …
★★☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 3 years ago
I decided on the Evewell because I was looking for a clinic that would offer one to one care. As they are one of the most expensive clinics and they promised this I had high hopes. After my first appointment I immediately bounced to another doctor. I ended up seeing several doctors and it was very frustrating as they weren’t communicating with each other. They even ordered double labs at my expense and gave conflicting recommendations. The PAs I dealt with were very disorganised and overall it was really not the experience I was hoping for. I stopped my treatment at Evewell as I just didn’t feel comfortable. So glad I made that decision because I found a clinic that is much better and way less expensive.
★★☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 3 years ago
I was referred by a friend, unfortunately my experience was disappointing I travelled to this clinic, front desk staff were great, I found it to be disorganized they lost my blood work it delayed my procedure didn’t offer compensation or …
★★☆☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 3 weeks ago
I was a patient at The Evewell in 2021. It has taken me a long time to write this review because I wanted to move …
★★★☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street 2 years ago
We had an unsuccessful round of IVF at the Evewell in July 2022. IVF clinics are really in the business of selling hope. When people are spending upwards of £10k on a round of IVF there should be far more rigour and independent analysis of the 'success results' that a clinic claims. We sort treatment due to having a low AMH. Our NHS consultant gave us a 3% chance of success. My advice after having 2 unsuccessful rounds (1st with the ARGC) and 2 miscarriages is to try and relax! Keep yourself healthy and try to be positive. We went on holiday and shortly after coming home we got naturally pregnant and now have a beautiful 9 week old baby!
★★★☆☆ The Evewell - Harley Street Edited 3 years ago
There is a serious problem with their patient communication. My doctor left mid-treatment and there was no communication to me and this change. I inadvertently find out about the departure while I was sorting out their terrible record …
★☆☆☆☆ Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre 7 months ago
I chose to have my fertility treatment with ARGC because their published statistics looked incredible. Unfortunately, my experience did not reflect the same standard. The clinic can be quite difficult to navigate at first, though over time you do find your feet. My first visit was for immune testing — I had even called twice beforehand to confirm the details. However, when I arrived, I was mistakenly treated as if I was there for a monitoring cycle. After some confusion and back-and-forth, I was redirected to another clinic for the correct tests. When my results came back showing elevated NK cells, I was understandably upset. Dr. Nawal was kind, clear, and supportive, explaining the next steps thoroughly. I later decided to proceed with a TSI (timed intercourse) round with Letrozole. While the doctors I encountered were excellent — knowledgeable, empathetic, and concise — the communication with the nursing and administrative teams was consistently poor. Messages and emails often went unanswered, sometimes for days, which during treatment can feel like an eternity. I frequently had to repeat information that was already in my file and chase updates that should have been proactively shared. During my cycle, there were repeated issues with clarity about next steps, medication requirements, and blood testing. On one occasion, I was contacted after leaving the clinic to return and give more blood due to a sample issue. Later, I discovered I had been given insufficient progesterone pessaries for my two-week wait, despite the team knowing I would be abroad. When I reached out for help, the responses were dismissive and lacked accountability. I eventually submitted an official complaint after returning from treatment abroad. The response I received was defensive, inaccurate in parts, and did not acknowledge the stress caused by their poor communication and administration. When my treatment ultimately did not work, I still reached out with openness to continue. Sadly, my final interaction — a phone call with the head nurse — was met with hostility and no compassion, despite just having shared the news that my treatment had failed. The doctors at ARGC are undeniably skilled, and I have full respect for their medical expertise. However, the administrative, nursing, and patient care teams seriously undermine the clinic’s reputation. The lack of communication, empathy, and accountability made what was already an emotionally and financially challenging process far more stressful than it needed to be. If you are considering ARGC, I would advise going in with caution — their doctors are excellent, but their support systems for patients fall significantly short of the standard you would expect from a leading fertility clinic.
★☆☆☆☆ Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre 6 months ago
I am sad to leave this review after many years at ARGC. I will start by saying that the admin staff and nurses are amazing. Unfortunately the doctors are so disorganised and frantic that no one knows who you are ever, and this leads to serious problems. ARGC put my health completely at risk (then joked about it) and I would really urge everyone to avoid the clinic for this reason. I lodged a complaint with them but there was no response of course
★☆☆☆☆ Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre 6 months ago
My experience of IVF treatment with ARGC was disappointing. While the clinic is known for high success rates, I now believe this may be partly due to their aggressive approach rather than careful practices like PGT-A testing—such as …
★☆☆☆☆ Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre 4 months ago
Awful - there is very poor communication and management of patients' mental health. It is an enterprise designed to make a profit from women and family's desperation and misery.
★☆☆☆☆ Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre a year ago
Do dot use this clinic for IVF! Their treatments are extreme and dangerous. My wife was recommended unnecessary procedures at the wrong time which were evidently only being suggested so that could charge us more money. She was not warned of …
★☆☆☆☆ Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre Edited a year ago
ARGC Malpractice Risked My Sister’s Life: Unfortunately, my sister received treatment at ARGC for an in vitro fertilization procedure. Despite the protocols indicating that implantation should be avoided if there are signs of ovarian hyper-stimulation, the clinic advised her to proceed with the implantation of two embryos. As a result, she developed ascites, renal failure, and severe electrolyte imbalances, putting her life at risk. From the onset of the first symptoms, including difficulty breathing, abdominal swelling, nausea, and intolerance to food, my sister visited the clinic multiple times. However, no additional tests were performed, and her condition was downplayed. She was simply advised to increase fluid intake. Fortunately, my sister went to A&E, where she was attended by the critical care team. They confirmed that her condition was life-threatening. The hospital later confirmed that she was the only patient they had treated with ovarian hyperstimulation syndrome (OHSS) who had embryos implanted. As no reputable clinic would recommend implantation when the ovaries are hyperstimulated. Currently, she is hospitalized in critical condition due to complications arising from malpractice at ARGC, which failed to apply proper preventive measures and dismissed her initial symptoms, allowing her condition to worsen and putting her life in danger. Please carefully consider your health and well-being before choosing ARGC for your fertility treatment.
★☆☆☆☆ Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre a year ago
Would leave a zero for the service and patient care if I could. They are involved in a purely money making enterprise with no thought or consideration for the women and families that come to them. Their so called service borders on …
★☆☆☆☆ Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre a year ago
Simply a negative experience through and through. Communication and environment are terrible, egg collection room looks very dodgy and not hygenic, getting information during scans is impossible- they almost can’t be bothered to talk to you, arrogance and entitlement of the doctors starting with Dr Taranissi is unbelievable.. Such high cost for such poor experience and results. It simply is not worth it. Although they have the reputation of ‘delivering results’ I think it is either an old or exaggerated story… Noone was able to provide any data.. Don’t waste your time and money and think that the clinic being on Harley St makes it good.
★☆☆☆☆ Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre a year ago
Have been researching IVF options in London for a family member - who will definitely not be using ARGC. Reasons to avoid: CEO Dr Taranassi is worth £30m+ but the clinic cuts costs by using eCourier, a company that pays workers less than minimum wage. (Don't assume savings are passed on - a single blood test costs £2,200 at ARGC.)
★☆☆☆☆ Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre a year ago
We had a demanding but overall acceptable experience all the way through egg collection. The problem is after having 10 eggs and 10 embryos fertilized on day 1, there was no check between day 1 and day 5. We were told on day 7 that we had none left. Nobody could explain to us what went wrong - just to try another cycle. Personally, it felt like we were constantly pushed to spend more on tests, and since we didn’t plan to transfer, perhaps they thought we were a bad stat anyway. Time and money wasted. More importantly, it took my husband and I four months to recover from that experience and now need to start over somewhere else.
★☆☆☆☆ Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre a year ago
I have am looking into fertility clinics at the moment, and was quite disgusted to see that ARGC uses Ecourier to transport essential medical samples. I can only assume that you are unaware that ECourier is pays their staff below minimum …
★☆☆☆☆ Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre 2 years ago
I'm really very surprised by any of the positive reviews I've read on this site. Our experience at ARGC was so awful. Someone wrote here that it feels like they are selling hope. That's exactly how we found it. The place is hugely overcrowded and so so shabby. We were ushered in from one test to the next with little or no explantation of what it was all for. Each time we went through the door we paid more money and we didn't know why. After the first consultation we didn't have any connection with the doctor apart from one not very pleasant scan where I asked him what my results meant. He didn't look at me and said that I had a very low chance of getting pregnant and that was it. I was devastated and left the room. After having spent over £1000 on bloodtests over several weeks we went in to start the treatment. On this day we found out that they needed another £5000 for medication on top of the £5000 we knew about. We hadn't been told about this and when we complained they said we should have asked about how much it was going to cost - which we had done repeatedly. The price list we had been given was very obtuse and hadn't made this remotely clear. I was devastated. We didn't have this money set aside. It felt like our chance of having a baby was taken away from us and we'd thrown away the money we'd spent so far. Some of the staff were so uncaring. You felt like you were wasting their time with a question. I felt like they just wanted our money. There was no human connection. Yes they get good results for people who are able to pay for it. But that can't be all there is. You should feel cared for and comforted and kept in the light when going through something as potentially as stressful as IVF. But the good news is we found a clinic in the Czech Republic - in Prague called UNICA. The clinic was clean and calm with a wonderful zen like atmosphere. The doctors available to talk and tell you about your treatment at every step. The and receptionists who know your name and ask how you are. Above all you have a dedicated co-ordinator who answers any questions you have, looks after you, walks in with you before the operation and there for you at the end. She tells you what drugs you are taking and why. She gives you a little pep talk when you need it and reminds you to be realistic when you need that too. In short it was the best care we could have hoped for. For this exceptional treatment, scans and all medication, flights, accommodation, food, concerts in the most beautiful of cities and a holiday of a lifetime the whole thing came to £5000 - about £7000 less than we would have paid at ARGC. We don't yet know whether we are pregnant but the experience was magical and I'd urge anyone thinking about doing IVF to explore options abroad.
★☆☆☆☆ Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre 3 years ago
The lack of follow up after a traumatic round of treatment which ended up with the hospitalisation of my wife, who had severe OHSS from an egg collection, is disgusting. Complete negligence from the staff at the time - they should have …
★☆☆☆☆ Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre 4 years ago
ARGC is one of the worst clinics in the U.K. I froze my eggs with them in 2016, got 19 in total, I thought I had preserved my fertility. However in 2020, when I wanted to use my frozen eggs, Dr Sapna continued to insist we should do a fresh cycle, which made no sense to given we I had eggs saved from when I was much younger. Normally When you thaw eggs, survival rate is 90%- ARGC lost 17/19eggs while thawing them. I have take two opinions since, one from a clinic in the US and one at St Mary’s hospital, both confirmed that their lab must be sub standard and embryologist are not trained properly given our outcome. We requested a full investigation, which came back with “we followed protocol on the day”. After such a disastrous outcome- Dr Taranassi never spoke with us or emailed us ( even though we requested it). Please do yourself a favour and pick a clinic with modern lab like Care Fertility/CRGH or anyone else really. Absolutely AVOID ARGC- substandard is the only description.
★☆☆☆☆ Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre 2 years ago
Paid them a deposit last year for an appointment that never happened due to lack of information (not) communicated by staff at ARGC. Was told I'd get a refund but am still waiting on the money. Very disappointing.

★☆☆☆☆ Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre 4 years ago
I abhorred my experience there: - Paper printed medical records - Dated decor, blue carpet and dusty fake flowers - Conflicting information between the scanning nurse and the nurses - Super rushed atmosphere at the time when you as a patient are the most vulnerable - Zero to none consultant time - Zero to non consideration for the patient time and wallet - They will insist you have milk while you are in stimulation phase, I am vegetarian and follow a lactose free diet. There is no scientific ground for recommending this. Would not go back there even if I was gifted another chance...

★★☆☆☆ Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre 7 months ago
Disappointing. The wait times and bedside manner are just as disappointing as the NHS and when I am spending hundreds of pounds for a service I expect better. Most disappointingly, the doctor did not introduce herself nor say hello, when I walked in she just ordered me to undress and get on the chair. I get that the practice busy but empathy and conversational skills is apart of the job and not using them is not how you make someone feel comfortable before an internal pelvic scan. I have also been emailing for a number of months requesting a copy of my ultrasound report to which I have had no response. I am hoping this review will escalate a response from your company.
★★☆☆☆ Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre 7 months ago
I am still waiting for my treatment and might change my feedback later, but I have to tell that if a donor is required, you need to deal with “the lab”, which seems like a separate set up with little communication with nurses and doctors. They answer emails one a week, don’t communicate well about what’s required, and possible delaying my treatment with no concern whatsoever. Any enquiry is answered again one week later. Any respectable clinic can’t have a lab operating like this!
★★☆☆☆ Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre a year ago
Terrible experience, despite high cost the clinic is extremely disorganised (my file couldn’t be found several times they had booked me in), terrible communication (nurses on the same day providing different information), lack of clarity on the full process and costs. There were consistent delays and you feel rushed at every step. It is managed like a production line. ★★☆☆☆ Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre 5 years ago

It is a stressful treatment and the busy, rushed atmosphere at ARGC makes the whole experience miserable.
★★☆☆☆ Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre Edited 6 years ago
The main issue is with how they treat people. You are just a number. You wait 30 min to 1 hour for a scan, they don’t try to speed things up or optimise, they don’t care. You never get privacy when speaking about your situation, it’s always in front of 5 other people waiting by the front desk. The morning of my egg collections, one of the nurses yelled at me because 1 page in my file was not filled in. The first day I collected the meds, no one had told me I would get a large bag full of meds which I had to take with me to a conference in Canary Wharf. March 2020 update: the IVF with ARGC did not work, the process was hard physically and mentally. I then fell pregnant naturally 1 year later and now have a healthy and happy baby! So hopefully there is light at the end of the tunnel for those of you researching IVF.
★★☆☆☆ Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre 6 years ago
