★★★☆☆ Assisted Reproduction & Gynaecology Centre 2 years ago
We had an unsuccessful round of IVF at the ARGC in Jan 2022. IVF clinics are really in the business of selling hope. When people are spending upwards of £10k on a round of IVF there should be far more rigour and independent analysis of the 'success results' that a clinic claims. We sort treatment due to having a low AMH. Our NHS consultant gave us a 3% chance of success. My advice after having 2 unsuccessful rounds (2nd with the Evewell) and 2 miscarriages is to try and relax! Keep yourself healthy and try to be positive. We went on holiday and shortly after coming home we got naturally pregnant and now have a beautiful 9 week old baby!
★☆☆☆☆ Create Fertility - St. Paul's Edited 3 months ago
Trying to build a family through fertility treatment is already exhausting and heartbreaking. What we didn’t expect was to also have to manage a clinic. Our experience at Create Fertility (St Paul’s, London) has been marked by poor communication and a lack of coordination. The clinic’s “patient coordinator” model hasn’t functioned as a support system for us—there have been long stretches with no proactive contact, no clear ownership of next steps, and uncertainty about who is responsible for our case. Instead of feeling guided, we’ve often felt we were chasing information and stitching the process together ourselves. Appointments have also felt poorly managed, with delays that add even more strain to days that are already emotionally loaded. When you need answers quickly, getting hold of the clinic can be difficult, and responses are not consistently timely. If you are pursuing the egg donor route that involves working with IVI Madrid, be aware that coordination between clinics may fall heavily on you as the patient. The contrast has been noticeable: the Madrid-side transfer process has been efficient, and the blood draw staff in London have been consistently good. Unfortunately, the overall London-side support has not matched what this kind of journey demands. This review is to help future patients know what to expect and to encourage Create to improve the basic standards of communication, continuity, and care. UPDATE: I emailed a complaint in about the lack of care I'm receiving with hopes that it would help progress our treatment. Instead, I'm told that the the complaint needs to be resolved BEFORE my treatment can resume!! So my treatment hinges on Create fixing poor internal processes that have been on going for years now. I feel that I am being penalized as a patient for raising a concern. Despite raising a complaint, I have yet to hear from anyone for days now. Time is ticking away and we're not closer to having the family we dreamed of.
★☆☆☆☆ Create Fertility - St. Paul's 2 months ago
I would give negative 100 if I could, back in 2024, I was 36 year-old (turining 37) when I under went a full IVF cycle with ABC IVF/Create Fertility. Their one2one conselling session costed me almost £200 per hour and I was not given much information nor support, their service was rushed, and the week when I had my egg retrival surgery, their building was out of power for 3 days. Ended up failed with my non-refundable cycle, they blamed me for being too old and could unlikely never have my own biological child ever. I felt publicly humiliated when the embryologist told me all of these, and so I had formally complained this clinic to HFEA. Then, I had a mental breakdown and suffered 1 month of hospitalisation and further 6 months of recovery. During this time, I had my current boyfriend who encouraged me to try conceive naturally with him. I got pregnant after first try, now I have our lovely daughter in my arms. This clinic is a highly commercialised and they can falsely make you feel good about yourself or have wrong perception about your fertility, so you put your money down to their services. However, their embryologists were incredibly unprofessional and nasty when things aren't going smoothly. BE VERY CAREFUL with choosing your clinic and services if you want to go through full IVFs.
★☆☆☆☆ Create Fertility - St. Paul's 7 months ago
I have been a patient at Create Fertility since September 2024, and I am now in the process of moving my embryos to another clinic. I feel it’s important to share my experience so that others are fully informed of the poor communication and …
★☆☆☆☆ Create Fertility - St. Paul's 7 months ago
An “experienced nurse” did the blood test and used her fingers to wipe my blood instead of the cloth and tape, resulting in the bruise lasting for more than 10 days. Seriously concern around hygiene and professionalism. If you want to experience the same level of service, feel free to sign on the 3-cycle contract and get you £20k locked in. No refund after 6 weeks of cancellation which blocked me from exploring other clinics in such a time sensitive situation.

★☆☆☆☆ Create Fertility - St. Paul's 4 months ago
Very poor service with hidden charges that they don't mention on their website such as having to pay to travel to Spain and PAYG medications for donors. I left there wondering if they are scammers so will be contacting the authorities.
★☆☆☆☆ Create Fertility - St. Paul's a year ago
It took me a year to recover from the way I was treated in this clinic because of my age and the results. I had a good promising first consultation. However,I am aware and informed that the fertility tests good results can be good but does not mean 100% success.i had 10 eggs retrieved and 7 of good grades with 6 of best grades. I Was updated the first 3 days of embryos then afterward received an email that disappeared before I read it on a weekend. Contact the clinic to find out what it was about and was told I will be call the next day or 2days. Anxious to know how my 6 good embryos I was informed of on Thursday and Friday were doing. When I call in afternoon, I was told that no I am will received the call on Tuesday instead. Call again on Tuesday, I was told I will received a call on Wednesday. On Wednesday I received a call from an ambriologist I never spoken before telling me what do you want us to do with your one embryo that is left, conduct the biopsy test done on it. Not even explained to me what happened to the other embryos which were according to the information received on Friday were all growing well with good amount of cells as expected. When I asked the question, the person.without any consideration of my feelings just said, they dud not survive the biopsy due to your age. Since then,I did not speak again to either a consultant and the embryologist I have been spoken to before. The person was so ear close and no emphaty or care at all when speaking to me about it. I know I made payment for all ivf procedure from start to the end. I did not have any implantation for which I was asked to pay. They made me me pay for all drugs even progesterone that was supposed to be used when I already pregnant before hands. I was denied any kind of re-emboursement for all procedure I did not have. I paid even for embryos conservation for a year which did not even happened. I think my care after my embryos got probably accidentally destroyed by someone at that clinic without informing me and missing the dates of my update on the progress was really really unfair. To have the report of anything else to forward to my GP became another big deal on emails back and forward were I was turned around because they were asking me pay for the report after destroying my embryos.I said that because before the destruction of my embryos I was given every scan and consultant report without even asking for it. I did find it hard to believed. That one private care should that worst compared to the public care only because they know that care is not accessible to everyone in the public sector.

★☆☆☆☆ Create Fertility - St. Paul's 6 years ago
I had a very poor experience after visiting CREATE (St Paul's). The Dr that assessed me had clearly not reviewed my notes and seemed to be questioning what I was telling her. Her manners were rude like she would interrupt you (not a big deal) .She then said she had to scan me to assess my situation. After a long while she said my case was complex, she could not see properly my organs blaming my severe endometriosis. She mentioned that in order for them to tell me how they could help I would need a further scan and other tests that she did not explain. I mentioned I would need to discuss with my husband as she believed I would have to choose egg donation. She then instructed me to go to the reception and book my scan and to take give the receptionists my notes. What was my surprise that despite me having already paid for the initial consultation plus the scan, they were expecting me to pay for another scan just because that Dr "couldn't see due to the severity of my condition". They also wanted me to pay for other tests that I clearly said I would need to first talk to my husband. Of course, I did not pay, I excused myself and repeated to staff I would first need to talk to my husband. Had I paid what they wanted to that initial consultation would have cost me more than 1000 pounds! and they would just see if they could help. Some days later they got in touch with me saying she had discussed with other Drs how they could help and they wanted me to book another consultation to discuss. (Why they were looking for more money in advance then?). I decided not to continue any dealings with them. I decided to go for a clinic abroad who were able to help me at the firs appointment and scan I had with them. A real world of difference and treatment. This other clinic made me feel like a human and not an ATM. I would recommend people to stay away.
★☆☆☆☆ Create Fertility - St. Paul's 3 years ago
Avoid avoid avoid! Since going to another clinic it's made me realise just how bad Create were. Felt very much like just another number to them. Different doctors all the time, always running late. phone lines constantly busy. Very little support and understanding given. Miss-leading prices. IVF and infertility is hard enough, do yourself a favour and pick a different clinic!
★☆☆☆☆ Create Fertility - St. Paul's 2 years ago
If you can find a different clinic, please choose that instead of CREATE. I have had the worst experience with the clinic at St.Paul's. It was all about money and still waiting to receive the call about the refund.
★☆☆☆☆ Create Fertility - St. Paul's 4 years ago
Had some blood tests here recently. The actual test was not a pleasant experience. They have a corridor queuing system which was a bit like a factory. It wasn't explained to me so a couple of people got ahead of me. It felt so impersonal and took a lot longer than I expected as I was originally given an appointment time. Worst of all I was told the results would probably be with me the next day. It's now 6 days later. I have chased for the results now 5 times now by phone and email. I've not received anything other than 'we've passed your enquiry on' on the phone and no reply at all by email. There's been no acknowledgement of the bad service, not even one 'oh I'm sorry about that'. I paid £365 for these tests and have nothing to show for it yet.

★☆☆☆☆ Create Fertility - St. Paul's 3 weeks ago
⭐️ 1 Star I would strongly urge anyone considering CREATE Fertility to think very carefully before proceeding. Our experience has been characterised by poor communication, a complete lack of coordination between departments, and a level of patient care that has steadily deteriorated over time. We were led to believe we would be proceeding with a frozen embryo transfer this cycle, took time off work for appointments, and only at the last minute were told that basic prerequisite steps had not even been arranged. These were things that should have been identified and coordinated well in advance. The result was an entirely avoidable delay to treatment. What has been most shocking is the complete inability to get hold of our assigned patient coordinator. Despite repeated emails, phone calls, formal complaints, and requests for escalation, weeks have passed with virtually no meaningful communication. Multiple staff members acknowledged internally that responses were overdue and that management had been “chased”, yet still nobody contacted us. The repeated explanation given to us has been that the clinic is “exceptionally busy”. Frankly, if that is the case then the clinic has clearly taken on more patients than it can adequately support and manage appropriately. As private patients undergoing fertility treatment, we do not believe it is unreasonable to expect basic communication, coordination, and continuity of care. Every department appears to operate in isolation, with responsibility constantly shifting elsewhere while no one takes ownership. The role of a patient coordinator should surely be to bridge those gaps, yet in our experience that coordination has been entirely absent. Sadly, this sits within wider concerns we have had regarding clinical decision-making and informed consent. We were advised to freeze one embryo at day 3, while the remaining embryos continued in culture but ultimately did not progress to blastocyst stage. This frozen embryo is now our one and only embryo. Looking back, we do not feel we were provided with sufficiently balanced or patient-specific information to make a truly informed decision regarding freezing at day 3 versus continuing culture to day 5. We were later advised there is no clear evidence base demonstrating improved outcomes with this approach, yet the implications of the decision — emotionally, financially, and practically — were never properly explained to us. Most concerningly, during a follow-up appointment after egg collection, the consultant openly acknowledged that the clinic receives “lots of complaints” regarding this practice and stated they “might be changing” it. Hearing that after the fact only reinforced our feeling that concerns around this approach were already well known internally, yet we were not properly informed beforehand. As non-medical patients, you place enormous trust in a fertility clinic to guide you through hugely emotional and complex decisions. Instead, we have been left dealing with months of uncertainty surrounding the viability of our only embryo, while continuing to pay storage fees and repeatedly chase for basic communication. At times, the clinic has felt less like a healthcare provider focused on patient care and more like a profit-driven operation, where communication and support rapidly decline once patients are within the system. The repeated delays, lack of accountability, ongoing storage fees, and constant need for us to chase updates have only reinforced that feeling. At this stage, we feel completely powerless. CREATE currently has our one and only embryo in storage, while we struggle simply to get responses or move treatment forward. We are now actively exploring transferring our embryo to another clinic because we no longer have confidence in the level of care, communication, or organisation being provided. Fertility treatment is emotionally exhausting enough without feeling ignored, unsupported, and passed between departments. We would not recommend this clinic.

We purchased three IVF cycles based on the advertised price, but ended up paying nearly double due to constant extra charges—especially for scans. Communication and care were very poor. We received no real guidance, and then were told it was “too late.” We felt rushed and dismissed throughout, as if they just wanted to take our money and move on. The professionalism and empathy—especially from the owner—were extremely disappointing. After more than 10 IVF cycles elsewhere, this has been our worst experience by far. What seems cheap at first becomes much more expensive with hidden extras. Avoid this clinic.

For a clinic that supposedly specialises in the over 40s, it is beyond poor that they do not do more to encourage uptake of PGTA. This is one of our regrets after two wasted years of transfers via this clinic. Initially if you have relatively few embryos it is tempting to bypass it, but this is a false saving as it can cost you in so many other ways. They are the ones who should know that, but never had any advice from them on that point. Doctors and facilities fine though patient coordination variable. Prices (relatively speaking) reasonable. But you are on a conveyor belt and get standard treatment, no attention to male vitamins and you could well end up wondering whether there are investigations they haven't tried.

★★☆☆☆ Create Fertility - St. Paul's Edited 4 years ago
I'd like to express that I feel disappointed finding out about the procedure Create takes when deciding/offering to freeze an embryo on day 3. When we received the call to ask as if we would like to freeze that embryo, we were told that it was a good quality. Later one, we found out that this embryo would still have to go to a previous and additional process of thawing before being transfer in which there is a possibility it could be lost, i.e. it cannot have a good quality to be transfer. During one of the last calls, I had with a nurse, I was told that this is normal procedure of Create. To decide to freeze embryos on day 3. I was also told that other clinics do not do this anymore, while Create still does. I felt disappointed when given this information as this can be easily interpreted as being given higher hopes. This procedure, I was told, is done to prevent or to make sure there is one embryo frozen. However, this is not 100% accurate as at that moment, we as a couple, or other couples, do not know that there is an additional step required for the embryo to be transferred. In other words, I feel like I have two embryos frozen (chances for a potential pregnancy) and one maybe. As you can imagine, this feels disheartening considering the high financial investment and effort put into this procedure. Furthermore, the day 3 embryo, after three days of cultivation, it did not survive and was discarded. The quality was not good enough to freeze again. We keep asking ourselves why wasn't it cultivated from the beginning further? We have ask Create and we still get no answer. We've been told that this was in perfect quality to freeze and it was done just in case none of the other would survive. This creates uncertainty. I interpret is as a way to say add an extra 'successful' frozen embryo as a result of the treatment when in reality it isn't! Therefore, I highly suggest to couples to let their embryos develop until day 5 or until they are in great quality for direct transfer. Regarding the communication, I agree with other comments here. The communication in English of some foreign professionals is poor. I am a foreigner as well, working in a foreign country; however, I make the effort or have had courses to help me achieve a better performance. The fixed line has had technical issues, but the main problem was their language skills. If these people are communicating such sensitive information to couples, some actions could be taken by Create (i.e. training) to improve. Regarding the service in St Paul's, all has been well. Just some slight delays in early morning appointments 5-10 min late. The team does stay in touch, calls/emails to follow-up on the procedure, and the staff is very friendly/amicable. My chose of stars is related to the disappointment of the guidance for freezing embryos. It would be better to be more transparent and open for this matter with the couples. Update 14.12.21: After this review, we received a call from the lead consultant. We must say that our comments were always taking into consideration and we did receive some additional feedback before. However, in some cases, the extra info provided, did not make things better. After this new and last call on Nov. 22, my husband and I felt satisfied with the explanations that were given. Nevertheless, my comments and rating above remain. Reviews are meant to help the company improve and to share experiences with future customers. I've have stated the good and the bad, and I hope that this helps couples to inform themselves well about this part of the process and also Create to improve their customer service. Communication is key, and this was part of the problem, technically (with the lines) and the way is was delivered (communications skills/fluency in the language)
★★☆☆☆ Create Fertility - St. Paul's 5 years ago
We had failed IVF. We had 2 transfers and both came negative. The reason is that the staff does not even have the common sense to do semen analysis before the treatment. They did blood tests of my wife but no semen analysis for me. Later I came to know that they want to charge extra £1000 for ICSI by saying the sperm motility is not good. Those who are going to this clinic, please make sure you have done all the tests otherwise you will end up losing the money or unsuccessful IVF. I still dontunderstand how can a fertility clinic start treatment without doing semen analysis.

Example is over stimulation and excess of medication sent out when you may not be able to use and you cannot send back. Please do your research its important and not just on recomendation.
★★☆☆☆ Create Fertility - St. Paul's Edited 4 years ago
Long unnecessary waiting times. Many of the workers can't speak clearly enunciated English. You can sense the disorganisation. Invoicing seems a focus of the service rather than ensuring a smooth efficient process that respects the time of clients. Underground laboratorial setting has no natural light and feels overly “clinical”. Location is very good and ideally placed in central London.. Out of principle, it's worth saying that this business model is exploitative and takes full advantage of desperate people looking to conceive. It's a money extraction racket backed by government statutes that will set people back thousands for an outcome that isn't even guaranteed. By them making home DIY artificial insemination less legally protected, the gov is funnelling people into this disgusting cash cow industry and taking their cut through taxes. Also IVF has a much higher rate of a child emerging with significant issues. This is because IVF selects imperfect “lazy” sperm to fertilize an egg. Nature, as usual, does things better. In response to their comment on this review; I am on the system, but I've chosen to post this review anonymously.
★★☆☆☆ Create Fertility - St. Paul's 4 weeks ago
I received treatment with Create St Paul’s in June of 2025 and since moving clinics wanted to leave a review of my experience. I’ll start by saying that all of the healthcare professionals I encountered there were very kind, considerate and extremely supportive in helping me through my first experience of IVF. After a lot of research we decided to go with Create. A huge deciding factor in this was its location with it having a clinic in St Albans as well as central London. I have a fairly hectic work schedule so this was important to minimise stress around apts. Unfortunately throughout the process we only had two apt. in St Albans one of which was cancelled so I would not take this location as much to do with the clinic. The difficulty we had with create was around the clinic being very unorganised and lacking in personalised care. You see a different Dr at every appointment leading to a lack of consistency. The biggest issue for us was the patient coordinators. The idea is you are assigned someone who supports you throughout the process who helps with all of the admin side. Our patient coordinator changed 4 times and were completely unorganised to the point where I often had to follow up and chase up admin and apts. This added a lot more stress into the process, managing everything alongside work and I felt I couldn’t trust them to be on top of everything. Following our unsuccessful results the doctors did a review and said this was a case of ‘luck’ and they recommended we go again with the same protocol. Knowing what I know now, I am absolutely baffled with this response as there were a whole host of easy diagnostic tests we could have done and things we could have tweaked moving forward. We have since found a different clinic where we had drastically different positive results. Looking back at the experience with create, I feel they are a very run of the mill IVF clinic that won’t be able to go above a very basic IVF protocol. They offer treatment for NHS and private patients however, I feel we very much got an NHS experience but at the price of a private clinic.
★★☆☆☆ Create Fertility - St. Paul's a year ago

Wishing you all the best in the next steps of your journey, CREATE Fertility

★☆☆☆☆ Avenues 4 months ago
Very disappointed in the communication and level of service provided. I had read and heard good reviews from the clinic and wanted to start my fertility jouney with Avenues. The first appointment with Dr Vivienne Hall was good and I felt very hopeful. Following this appointment it then took nearly two months for a follow up call discussing a AMH result (which could be done with an at home test). Avenues use an App for communication and appointment which seems really efficient. However, it became a painful and unpleasant process. The system to book appointments meant that they booked the wrong time, when I called to change it, the changes were never done. It was a painful process for booking a 30 minute follow up call and therefore I didn't have the opportunity to book any further tests. I've been trying for nearly two years and I was looking forward to beginning with a clinic where I felt heard and could get some answers but the issues in communication have been extremely disappointing and I would not recommend this clinic.
★☆☆☆☆ Avenues Edited 3 months ago
The entire process has been unnecessarily stressful due to a lack of transparency in communication and an inability to address straightforward requests. Additionally, the use of overly casual language, such as ending sensitive emails/messages with 'kisses,' feels inappropriate and unprofessional given the absence of a personal relationship. Overall, this has not been a positive experience. Update Feb 2026 - thank you for the response over a year later. These issues occurred after you separated from the partner. Up until then I had a good experience and planned on progressing with Avenue.
★☆☆☆☆ Avenues Edited a year ago
Clinic lacks transparency, accountability and management. They failed to make me aware that they freeze FOUR eggs per straw and that you will have you pay for additional straws used. They convinently told me this AFTER paying for an initial consultation of £210. I have had consultation with 3 clinics and have asked embryologists if they ever freeze fours eggs in a single straw, and the answer has been a resounding NO - 1 is the practice and 2 is the absolute maximum per straw. I tried raising the fact that I had been uninformed of these things and wouldn't have gone with their clinic if I was informed of their practice of freezing four eggs per straw, but it was brushed aside. I have also experienced a repeated lack of follow-up with the clinic on a few things. To date (entering September), I have not received the letter from my consultation I had back in July despite asking for it twice. I have always had clinics send this out promptly following my consultations, a day after and at the most 5 days. Reply: Following my consultation on 18 July 2024, I reached out on 26 July 2024 on the app informing your staffs that I am waiting to be contacted for costing before I proceeded, where I also asked for the summary of my consultation and for Dr Fatum to answer a quick question relating to my treatment plan. Come 2 August 2024, I still do not have an answer to my question nor the summary of my consultation to this very day (nearing end Sep). I explicitly asked if the clinic could first let me know the answer to my question so that I'll be better informed about how many cycles I'll be proceeding with (and then go over the costs). Once again, I do not hear back and I reach out 6 August 2024, asking for an update. Your clinic had decided not to publish anywhere in your pricing list or communicated at any point BEFORE my consultation that a maximum of four straws are used in your treatment and that any further straws are chargeable (and that it freezes FOUR eggs per straw). This was communicated to me AFTER my initial consultation, and in vain, I had to get my husband to take a day off from work to drive down from Leeds to London. I explained on 12 August 2024 on the app, that I was uninformed of these and would not have considered your clinic if I had been aware that your clinic freezes multiple eggs per straw and that it was charging for any more than four straws used to freeze a maximum of four eggs per straw. This is not something I can accept. I wrote you 5 September 2024 that given that these pieces of information was withheld from me before I had to spend on a consultation in vain, I asked for a refund of the cost of my consultation and reimbursement for my travel cost to attend that appointment. I expressed that I am no longer interested in receiving the summary of my consultation (which I have yet to receive to date, close to three months later) nor pursuing treatment with your clinic for lapses in transparency, accountability and management. I expressed that I wished to move on from this disappointing experience with your clinic. Once again, your clinic hasn't cared to respond to my email. Given how your clinic has repeatedly and blatantly ignored my emails, I will be escalating this to HEFA and providing my honest feedback of my experiences with your clinic.
★☆☆☆☆ Avenues 7 months ago
Unfortunately very unprofessional and unsafe clinic. Please don’t risk it with them.
★☆☆☆☆ Avenues a month ago
After care was horrible - they left us in the ditch after the first cycle. After a negative test we weren’t given any appointments and had to make endless calls just to get another consult. By that time we had already missed the period for another transfer. Everything before was fine, great service. But as soon as a negative test happens, we felt abandoned
★☆☆☆☆ Avenues 2 months ago
DO NOT USE THIS CLINIC. They took a £499 deposit from me for treatment, then they cancelled the appointment with less than a day’s notice and have failed to refund the money in spite of me asking repeatedly in writing and on the phone over the course of a month. Starting court proceedings to recover the lost money this week. Utterly unacceptable.
★☆☆☆☆ Avenues 2 months ago
I would have liked to give 2 stars, but based on our overall experience, I’ve gone with 1. …
★☆☆☆☆ Avenues 2 months ago
Predatory and deeply troubling experience. From start to finish, the entire process felt geared towards fear-mongering and …
★★☆☆☆ Avenues a year ago
The actual clinic and the staff were amazing, I have nothing but praise for them and would happily give a 5 star. It is a shame i cannot separate my review for the consultant and the clinic. We had one round of IVF and for a forward thinking clinic that uses AI and an app, they need to ensure their consultants use the app for all medication updates and appointment updates (as IVF veterans will know there are many medications at different doses). There is no point advertising that there is an app, or live feeds for embryo development if they are not used or available for EVERY patient (transparency is very important) . They also need to ensure they partner with consultants that have time to dedicate to IVF patients as everything is time critical and this is very important, as at a regular IVF clinic the staff are available for contact as soon as a patient is at day 1 of their period. It is extremely important to be allowed a safe space to be allowed to ask questions, we did not feel we were given this by the consultant we used. She did not react well to questions, and the one time I did get emotional (mainly because of how I was being treated) she told me I needed to get help for mental health issues. The receptionist used by the consultant is not contactable by phone, only responds via email, as does the consultant. If you are the kind if patient that needs to be able to contact someone by phone then AVOID. We appreciate the tests requested by our consultant and the recommendation for a hysteroscopy. We often had to chase up results and were told they had already been sent (we had no record and nothing in our junk mail). We continued our treatment with the same consultant as we had already paid over £2000 for the medications (lot of which were unnecessary add-ons). We appreciate all of the help and advice we received from the clinic and once they were involved things improved dramatically, the rooms were really nice and it has the potential to be an amazing clinic if working with right consultants. Also high praise for the embryologists at Avenues they tried everything they could to ensure we had an embryo to transfer (a day 3 6 cell) their communication was excellent. We have since moved to a new clinic/consultant and have had much better results so far (day 5 blastocyst) and less a aggressive treatment protocol.
★★☆☆☆ Avenues 4 weeks ago
I don’t know why fertility clinics hide costs in their policies. This is unfair and it’s already a difficult journey for us. On the day I arrived with my husband, I was rushed into the consultation room. They didn’t explicitly mention that …
★☆☆☆☆ The Birth Company London, HCA a year ago
I had a very disappointing experience at The Birth Company under HCA Healthcare UK today (27Feb2025), specifically due to the unprofessional and rude behavior of Specialist Sonographer Afsaneh Tehrani. This was my first pregnancy, a deeply emotional and special moment, yet she treated me in an unwelcoming and dismissive manner. When I asked if the sound we heard was my baby’s heartbeat, she responded in a condescending way: “Your heart is up there” (pointing at my chest), “that’s the baby’s heartbeat, yeah!” This was completely unnecessary and unprofessional. A first-time mother asking a simple question should never be made to feel belittled. On top of that, she didn’t even acknowledge my pregnancy with a simple “congratulations,” which is basic courtesy in any prenatal setting. She was also clearly in a hurry, rushing through the appointment as if she just wanted to get it over with. At one point, she even asked if I had finished changing yet, making me feel pressured and uncomfortable instead of supported during such an important moment. Additionally, she spoke very aggressively to my husband, telling him to only take pictures without her in the frame. While we understand staff may have preferences about photos, the way she communicated this was unnecessarily harsh and unpleasant, adding to the overall negative experience. To be clear, the reception and administrative staff were kind and professional, and my issue is solely with Afsaneh Tehrani. I am well aware of the exceptional quality of care in HCA Healthcare, as I will be delivering at The Portland Hospital. Unfortunately, this experience fell far below those standards. I strongly do not recommend Afsaneh Tehrani and hope The Birth Company ensures their patients receive the respect and compassion they deserve during such a sensitive and emotional time.
★☆☆☆☆ The Birth Company London, HCA 5 years ago
My husband and I went for an early reassurance scan here and it turned out to be nothing but. We found the sonographer to be quite rude, abrupt and her delivery of the results was very confusing. Leaving us worried with no further guidance …

I went for a Harmony test at exactly 10 weeks. Almost a week later I received a call saying that my results were inconclusive and they need to repeat the test. Went to give another sample, had another scan just to receive again the same no …
★★☆☆☆ The Birth Company London, HCA 6 years ago
We are were not sure which NIPT to go for and a lovely receptionist told us to discuss with the technician. We found the technician to be rude and she also gave us incorrect information. During the scan my husband was taking a video of the screen on the wall (not neither I or the technician could possibly be included in the view) and we were told very rudely that she would rather that we do not record. We have been to other scanning centres and its never been an issue! As said we are just taking a video of the fetus and not anyone else or thr room! Also the same thing could have been conveyed in a polite manner. While explaining the difference between safe, panorama and harmony we were told the panorama results take 7-10 days. I queried this as the website says otherwise. However was told no its 7-10 so we went ahead with Panorama. We wanted the results before the NHS 12 weeks nuchal test and did not receive the results in the 7-10 days and before the NHS test. I would not use this place again. Maybe it was just our luck to get the wrong technician. 2 stars for the easy booking process and the receptionist.
★★★☆☆ The Birth Company London, HCA a year ago
I have done 7-8 scans here during my two pregnancies which all went fine. Easy booking, fast service, on time, pleasant staff. However, I had a senior sonographer appointment few weeks ago where I asked specifically for transvaginal scan which provides the most accurate picture for my state. She dismissed me by saying that she sees everything on the stomach scan. Few weeks later when I did transvaginal scan it turned out the things are very different. It is possible they may have changed in the meantime but also likely I wasn't checked well. I came specifically as NHS didn't want to do transvaginal at that point but being rejected at private clinic is a very poor practice.
★★★☆☆ The Birth Company London, HCA a year ago
Pro: Area clean and front desk staffs were friendly Scan was quite detailed and the screen was huge ! Report came back quick. Con: The sonographer wasn't too friendly and late for the appointment. She was so in a rush at the end to go on to her next appointment that she rushed to do my blood test and didn't do it properly. First time on my right arm wasn't successful she wiggled the needle inside my arm for awhile but still failed. She then needs to redo it on my left arm. ( I have been having blood test at the hospital numerous times in these past few weeks and is always a success). The wiggled she did on my right arm was a little discomfort. Both of my arms ended up bruised. I called before and was told the sonographer will explain to me the difference between each NIPT package before making a choice. But because she was so in a rush, we didn't have a thorough explanation and had to make a decision in that second. One other thing is for some reason people keep knocking on the door while I was having a scan and blood test which was quite disturbing.
★★★☆☆ The Birth Company London, HCA 6 years ago
We went for the Bronze Package that includes 3 scans . I must say we were very happy with the first 2 ladies that did our scans . They were super nice and caring , they would ask us if we wanted more photos or videos of the baby and they …
★☆☆☆☆ London Gynaecology - Moorgate 4 months ago
I have previously attended London Gynaecology and received good service, which makes this experience particularly disappointing. During a recent emergency, the standard of care was extremely poor. The ultrasound machine was not working initially, which I was patient and understanding about. However, further issues followed. I requested copies of my scan images, which were important due to the emergency, yet it took several days to receive them and only after chasing multiple times. There was no sense of urgency or clear communication. I also asked for blood tests to be carried out, which were refused, despite the NHS hospital later confirming they were appropriate and offering to do them. Most concerning of all, I was misdiagnosed, which was later confirmed by the NHS hospital. During an already stressful and time-sensitive situation, the lack of urgency, poor coordination, and incorrect diagnosis were unacceptable. I will not be attending this clinic again and would not recommend it based on my experience.
★☆☆☆☆ London Gynaecology - Moorgate 4 months ago
My partner was left extremely distressed after her scan due to a clear misdiagnosis. She was devastated and spent days in tears as a result. When you pay for a private service, you expect accuracy, professionalism, and expertise — none of …
★☆☆☆☆ London Gynaecology - Moorgate 9 months ago
Really disappointed with my experience here. Poor customer service and lack of transparency on pricing. When you pay a premium for a service, I would have expected better.
★☆☆☆☆ London Gynaecology - Moorgate Edited a year ago
Why are you declining my numerous calls to ask for an update on this charge??? Receptionists charged my insurance for a HPV vaccine package consisting 3 doses. I signed the document at the beginning of my appointment highlighting my insurance coverage. They asked me to pay (210 additional pounds) stating the consultant I saw is not covered under my insurance, despite them cancelling and rescheduling my appointment at the last minute - changing my consultant from one who is insured to one who isn't. Instead of rightfully refunding me, they told me they will contact my insurance to check if they can cover the mistake the receptionist made. So they have been paid for the vaccine coverage, and also random additional costs which they would rather keep than refunding. They cancelled my appointment last minute, and none of the many receptionists knew what to do! I was told my jab would be done by a nurse, and even the 'consultant' who isn't insured didn't ask me any questions/discussed anything, just gave me the jab and it was done. Weird how I have been charged for my injections and also, a 210 pound mistake that they have owed up to, but rather would profit and ask the insurance to cover their bums as opposed to just refunding me. Money hungry place that has so many receptionists but none of them know what to do.
★☆☆☆☆ London Gynaecology - Moorgate a year ago
Whilst this clinic may be good at dealing with other / straightforward gynaecological matters, I found the consultant I saw to be disappointing. She was truly compassionate in terms of the physical pain and emotional distress I was in which was great, but was lacking in medical knowledge around things I would expect any gynaecologist to be well versed in. She was unable to diagnose a textbook case of vulvodynia, looked up on her computer how to deal with recurrent thrush, and when given a full history of my symptoms and concerns, said multiple times that it's a 'mystery' - not comforting words to hear from a specialist you've paid substantial money to see. Ultimately, after the tests, the mystery remained, the explanation was insufficient, and the treatment suggested was not suitable and did not work. She did not get to the bottom of the issue. I eventually got an answer and solution from the NHS a few months later from a gynaecologist that knew her stuff. Regarding the clinic itself, the only nice thing I can say is that the waiting room was nice and well equipped. Aside from this, waiting times were terrible - especially given this is a private clinic. Communication about delays was poor and on one occasion, I travelled in, waited a long time, only for the consultant to then tell me that the test results I'd come in to discuss had not even been received and we had nothing to discuss. This was known prior to my appointment and should have been communicated. They then said they would not charge for that appointment, but still did. Overall, I would not recommend this clinic. Other consultants here might be good but even the waiting times and terrible communication is enough to put me off. There are plenty of better private clinics to go in London.

★☆☆☆☆ The Fertility Academy 2 years ago
I initially contacted The Fertility & Gynaecology Academy due to a misunderstanding with the contact information I found online. Despite this mix-up, the admin staff was eager to assist, strongly recommending Dr. Amin Gorgy as an exceptional option. Encouraged by their enthusiasm, and after discussing it with my husband, we decided to book an appointment for clinical advice regarding our fertility concerns. Our appointment began later than planned, highlighting an initial disregard for patient time. Punctuality is crucial to us, and this delay was not a positive start. During the consultation with Dr. Gorgy, it became evident that there was a lack of empathy towards our situation. The doctor discussed Intrauterine Insemination, emphasising its low success rate, and quoted the treatment at £1,500 to £2,000, which is considerably higher than the £1,100 listed on their website. Similarly, for IVF treatment, he quoted £6,000, significantly above the website's prices of £3,800 for IVF and £4,750 for an IVF-One Cycle Package. This pattern of quoting higher prices than those listed led us to suspect an intentional strategy to increase patient expenditure, undermining our trust in the clinic's transparency and honesty. Furthermore, when my husband requested a written report of our assessment, Dr. Gorgy informed us that the clinic only provides verbal feedback, denying us any tangible documentation of our consultation. This approach felt dismissive and left us without essential reference material. My husband was advised to get hormone tests, and the finance team advised us to use their in-house lab for these tests. Before finalizing any decisions, we carefully reviewed the costs involved. In the end, we were confronted with a billing summary of £760, comprising £280 for the consultation and an unexpectedly high £480 for the blood tests. This was in stark contrast to the anticipated costs, especially since the basic hormone blood tests were listed at £70 on their website. Yet, we were quoted £240 under the "Hormone Profile" category, with an additional two tests at £120 each. Given these discrepancies and the lack of clear justification for the extra charges, we decided not to proceed with the blood tests, sensing something fishy in their billing practices. This decision was driven by the dramatic difference in expected versus actual billing, which raised significant concerns about the clinic's transparency and integrity. It appeared as though there was a deliberate attempt to manipulate billing categories for financial gain, indicating a profoundly troubling approach to financial practices. Our interaction with the admin staff and the accounting team, especially concerning the lab tests, further compounded our decision not to pursue treatment at this clinic. The perceived enthusiasm of the admin staff felt insincere upon leaving, and the pressuring tactics of the accounting team regarding the lab tests were particularly off-putting. In conclusion, our visit to The Fertility & Gynaecology Academy was disappointing. We encountered a lack of empathy, flexibility, and a respect for our autonomy and time. The inconsistent pricing and the refusal to provide written assessments, coupled with a concerning billing experience, diminished our trust in the clinic. We felt viewed not as individuals in need of support but as potential sources of increased revenue. Based on our experience, we cannot recommend The Fertility & Gynaecology Academy. We will seek assistance elsewhere, where our health and well-being are prioritised, and transparency is a given.
★☆☆☆☆ The Fertility Academy 5 years ago
Me and my partner had a video consultation with the doctor. (She had several treatments done at the clinic before, so the doctor knows her medical history.) Our agenda of the consultation was to discuss results of tests that were recommended by the doctor. Not to mention that each consolation with the doctor (each time you need to address any question to the doctor) is extremely expensive 170+ pounds. When such a high premium is paid you expect to get a corresponding service. 50% of scheduled 30 min zoom talk both audio and video just froze - we could not hear anything. During the rest of the time we were able to formulate a few questions. Almost none of the questions were answered. Definitely nothing specific, no reasoning at all. When a person claims to be a specialist in a certain field, immunology in this case, he/she should be able to interpret both clinical history of a patient and results of tests and and at least try to connects the dots - to provide some sort of reasoning, which makes him believe that a certain medication is of a benefit. I didn't get a feeling that a person I was talking to had good knowledge of immunology. It was rather: try this medication because (we can provide it) and in general it works better than other medications - very business oriented. One of test was LAD - and after some hours of researching and reading replies (that are publicity available) from other doctors to patients with similar test results and clinical history, I'm almost 100% that the doctor didn't even highlight result that were of relevance, but instead pointed to a completely different direction. Luckily for people working in this field there are not so many medications to chose from , and therefore even people with a limited knowledge of the domain can "successfully" operate in this field.
★☆☆☆☆ The Fertility Academy 2 years ago
I had a very unfortunate experience of coming across this clinic and its doctors. After i came to terms with the lack of the actual result ; My later regret was combined with both anger for the waste of money but mostly for being given a false hope by what can only be described a totally unsympathetic and robotic doctor (along with his mismanagement and misadvise on my situation). 3 unsuccessful IVF cycles and money down the drain (where I was charged for the 3rd cycle that involved nothing but one scan and one "supposed egg extraction" ) I later learnt that they could have just advised against the last IVF cycle completely in order not to waste my money and not to give me the false hope..... We couldn't help it but my husband and I both felt somewhat the "extortion approach" but alas also knew that we were partially to blame for trusting ourselves into the incompetence of these doctors. My advise to other women out there, do your research promptly and thoroughly before committing to your IFV team....I will add though that some of the less senior members of staff at the clinic were pleasant and understanding....so justly.. i wont say much negative about them...
★☆☆☆☆ The Fertility Academy Edited 3 years ago
Really disappointing & distressing experience with Dr. Gorgys clinic- both the service & staff, from start to finish. I travelled from another country to attend an appointment last August 2022. They missed some of the bloods on the day - which can happen but then zero help or support to sort the issue - I was left to arrange the missed bloods in my home country with a third party that they deal with - it was very stressful all round. Spent months trying to get information from FGA around what bloods were carried out on me. 7 months later it is still going on. Am waiting on a partial refund & again zero help. I could write pages on what happened. Anyway, in a nutshell, genuinely shocked at how they carry out (or dont) their business. I would not recommend this clinic on any level. Update 12/05/2023 in response to Dr. Gorgys Clinic Reply This response is interesting, given what happened, hence the update - the issue happened in August 2022 and I received ZERO help or support from from Dr. Gorgys Clinc to resolve the problem at that time - as stated in my original review - I was left to sort bloods in my home country & set everything up with no support from Dr. Gorgys clinic for the whole entire process. This, despite the fact that, again, as referenced in my original review, we traveled to the UK and paid for all services with Dr. Gorgys clinic and just because you outsource part of your services, I, as YOUR customer, was left to deal with this outsourced company directly. Shocking & disappointing. In addition, your reference to “have been communicating with you ever since to resolve the issue” - you did not communicate to resolve the above issue where we were left for weeks trying to sort the mess & issue with bloods. What you did communicate on was the minor issue of a partial refund, at a much later stage, when we had been left to sort the actual problems on our own. In addition, the apology you reference as made was - an apology on behalf of the 3rd party - I have no record of where Dr. Gorgys clinic took responsibility or apologised for anything themselves.
★☆☆☆☆ The Fertility Academy 3 years ago
We were supposed to have a first consultation with them. We arrived and was left to wait in the communal hallway for over 20mins even though we repeatedly knocked and could see two staff on the other side of the door. When they eventually let us in the man was unapologetic and nonchalantly pointed to the waiting area and chucked the registration forms for us to fill in. I was already stressed and anxious as this would be our third time trying. I was tearful and frustrated with the poor attitude so my husband and I left. The doctor rang an hour later to apolgise about the poor treatment but the damage and first impression was already done. To top it off they actually charged me for the terrible encounter and I have been trying to get a refund ever since! Word of advice hire staff who are empathetic as fertility is such a delicate emotive subject. I have selected another clinic's services.
★☆☆☆☆ The Fertility Academy Edited 4 years ago
BE CAREFUL! I donated my eggs in this Clinic over a year ago. They are very unprofessional and rude. I ended up in A&E with OHSS because of them and my life was at risk. I'm in process of reporting this clinic and especially Dr. G who has over stimulated my eggs and tried to get away with it when I started to feel unwell and ended up in the hospital. And okay maybe they helped women to get pregnant and have their dream baby but the way they treat us - donors is shocking and had a big impact on my physical and mental health. I will never forgive you.
★☆☆☆☆ The Fertility Academy 6 years ago
I have had two treatments of IUI with Dr. Gorgy after someone I knew had a number of treatments with him. Unfortunately, we don't exactly share the same experience and I can relate to the other reviews that the whole experience is very money driven. Dr. Gorgy takes very little into account your past history and hardly writes down any notes or remembers anything from previous appointments. This is something i was prepped for by my friend who attended the clinic, so I knew to write a diary for each appointment. I had one cycle of IUI with Gorgy which all went well and all to plan, however no pregnancy at no fault of the clinic. Sadly the second cycle did not go as well, where he completely missed my ovulation by a couple days. Somehow I was convinced to proceed with my IUI, and out of desperation I agreed. I felt mislead as the chances are actually known to be very small when performing IUI a couple days after ovulation which was not mentioned on the day. During this cycle I ended up hyperstimulated and suspected for an ectopic pregnancy for an event that happened prior to this IUI. I notified the nurses that I was going away around this period and no advice was given not to travel when I was due to fly 7 hours overseas. Had my pregnancy turned out to be ectopic I might have died mid flight. Unfortunately, while abroad I ended up in hospital due illness from being hyperstimuled and also underwent investigations for an ectopic pregenancy which turned out to be negative. From the experience I found it really amazing how the medical clinic treating me did not advise me not to travel when I mentioned it to them, particularly when it comes to potentially life threatening issues. Another review mentions the Dr. being late, I can vouch for this as on three occasions I arrived for an ultrasound over my two treatments with Dr. Gorgy not being there that day when promised and then and just passively sent for a blood test alone. Personally, I found the majority of the staff apart from Jeana a bit ignorant. On a number of occasions I had texted the nurses during my miscarriage and they did not reply back to my texts. Lastly, a particularly uncomfortable situation being sent into a basement for payment each session where the staff don't have the courtesy to even look you in the eye. I felt like that handing over a couple thousand was a nuisance to them. Since I've had treatment with the NHS and I can hands down say that the treatment is ten folds better. Sadly, I won't be recommending this clinic as I felt there was serious lack of care.
★☆☆☆☆ The Fertility Academy 5 years ago
Extremely poor service. First I called and ask if I can book an appointment for test and I was told “yes” and the receptionist book me for the test and even made me pay a deposit. After one hour she called me back saying she did a mistake and first I need to have a consultation and then the test... but instead of giving me an option to have my money back since she took deposit for a service they don't offer, she told me that I can still go for the test first and have a consultation later on... which it doesn't make sense but anyway I was desperate to do the test so I agreed. So I went there at 12h(my appointment ) and waited there almost 40min to find out that I will have a consultation instead of a test. I’ve paid £570 for the test and consultation and I was told that the results will be ready in 3-4weeks. 5 weeks later, no one contacted me so I decided to give them a call, they told me to send an email, I’ve sent one and I am still waiting for response.....one week.... One week for a response... response which simply could be “we are sorry but there is a delay with your test” But... NO, they are not even bothered to get back to me. So much money for such a disrespectful service, I will not continue with the rest of the tests I was offered by them as they are not reliable!
★☆☆☆☆ The Fertility Academy 6 years ago
I cannot unfortunately recommend this place. Dr Gorgy charged us £230 for a consultation, which resulted only in him providing the complete pricelist for his services, none of our history was analysed, and no specific or useful recommendations were made - instead just a recommendation that we try a bit of everything and we can decide on what to prioritise. This "consultation" could have lasted less than fifteen minutes if he'd had his way. Nothing was explained, just the technical names of tests were listed off. I insisted on asking for explanations, which were still unclear and ultimately not useful. I'd recommend avoiding this place.
★☆☆☆☆ The Fertility Academy a year ago
very disappointed after a series of expensive tests, they gave me a medication that was not suitable for me, which other doctors have confirmed, they do not explain why they are giving you the medication and what effects it has. I paid a significant amount of money for nothing . Avoid it please
