★☆☆☆☆ London Women's Clinic - London Bridge 2 months ago
My experience with LWC and their credit management team and overall process has been very poor. Overall coordination within teams is extremely poor causing me as a customer tremendous pain and mental stress.
★★★☆☆ London Women's Clinic - London Bridge 3 years ago
We've had a long journey with LWC which finally ends now with the disposal of our remaining embryos. Sadly in 2018 we lost our pregnancy. We thought at the time and still do that the medication could have been monitored better. We've often felt we were just a number and when you're paying so much for treatment you want to feel special. Hence we only give 3 stars! Good luck to everyone and hope you get your happy ending!
★☆☆☆☆ Harley Street Fertility Clinic 7 months ago
Please take the negative reviews seriously - do not go to this clinic. Writing down our experience to hopefully help couples: We are both healthy adults in our 30s who wanted to do basic checks, just to catch any issue early (if there was one). What subsequently played out at the clinic was ridiculous. First, the clinic is dirty and cramped. The moment you enter it does not feel like a premium and well run organisation. For the female internal examination, the lady doing the test was extremely rude and dismissive to my partner when she requested me to be in the room to accompany her. She said "there's no space here, you are wasting my time". Unacceptable. Once all testing was complete, things only got worse. Getting an appointment with Dr Venkat to receive the results was difficult. At the appointment she started with "Good news" but then tacked on the fact that it would be very difficult for us to conceive naturally. She cited specifics. She seemed genuine. She suggested we go straight to IVF. After her call we received follow up emails from the clinic further perpetuating this. However, when we looked at the actual test paperwork there was NO mention of the conditions she had mentioned. We went for a second opinion with a leading OBGYN who reviewed the test results, did a scan, and categorically told us that there were no issues at all and we should have no problem conceiving. She was absolutely right: 3 months later we were pregnant and we now have a healthy child. On reflection, I believe that DR Venkat (who I believe owns the clinic) and her team are purposefully pushing healthy patients to do IVF to boost their success statistics. The callousness of doing something like this for financial gain is insane, but if you read other reviews you can see a pattern. I am writing this review to encourage people to go elsewhere where they will get the care they deserve and be treated honestly.
★☆☆☆☆ Harley Street Fertility Clinic 4 months ago
My husband and I both had tests done at HSFC, which was professional and we then had a video call to go through our results. The results implied that my husband was infertile but at no point was it suggested we retake the tests or that there were any options other than IVF. We subsequently went to The Lister Hospital for a second opinion and my husband had the tests redone and it transpires that my husband was not infertile. The Dr at The Lister explained to us that results can be skewed by viruses/ dehydration etc. so you should always retake the tests. This was never suggested to us by HSFC and the call was a hard IVF with no thorough explanation of what was actually wrong with my husband's results. The Dr at The Lister asked me my results and with his advice, we were pregnant within a month and now have a healthy 6 month old. I'm a pragmatic individual but it has taken me over a year to write this review. My advice would purely be that if you get negative results, get a second opinion.
★☆☆☆☆ Harley Street Fertility Clinic 7 months ago
I would first like to state that they will respond to this saying none of it is true. I promise you this is 100% true and we just don’t want anyone else to go through the utter trauma and expense that we experienced. From our first visit we should have seen the warning signs about the clinic but from having nothing to compare it to it’s so hard to navigate this system. I was made to sob on our first visit due to the delivery of news about my situation. In hindsight I’m convinced this is a tactic used by the clinic to physiologically make you think that you really need them. I think this because as my treatment continued and I was too far in to back out I saw many other girls sobbing like I had that day. The rudeness of the staff is consistent throughout the entire clinic. Training in customer care seems to not happen at all and makes for a trying time when you are so emotionally and financially invested. The whole circumstances were made even worse by the fact that nothing in the clinic is computerised. All notes are physically written and there is only one file (very dangerous in terms of the event of a fire etc) it also means no one has any idea what’s going on. Be aware if you choose this clinic you better stay on top of your treatment as they won’t. A quick note on facilities…they are shocking. When you come to having and surgery/transfers or eggs collected good luck!! The room in the basement is AWFUL. It’s dirty and dank. There isn’t enough equipment for the amount of patients, they have to keep unplugging the heart rate monitor (without cleaning in-between) to put on each patient whilst being monitored. I was absolutely SHOCKED. The only toilet that is available to use is in the staff cloakroom full of old stinky shoes. Not the best when you just had very intimate surgery. My story took a dark turn when we came to having our embryo transfer. I was advised on many medications that I needed to take to get my body ready for this. Now bearing in my at points I was taking up to 10 - 15 different medications daily and it’s not my job to understand exactly what every one of those does. You are putting your trust in the Drs at this stage. In my case Dr Venkat. I was without knowing what it was put onto a high dosage of prednisone (a very strong steroid). I understand that I may have needed this to make my treatment successful and I’m not opposed to that. I just had any issue with the fact that not at any point did anyone explain the side effects and possibilities from taking this. It’s very very likely to cause very high blood sugar levels. Which in my case it did and wasn’t discovered until I was at hospital. Not once was I advised this could be an issue. They didn’t advise me to monitor my sugar levels or let my GP know that I was taking this so they could monitor it. Nor did they check my sugar levels before prescribing this drug to me!! The hospital (Kings College London) was absolutely SHOCKED at this! They said this is absolutely medically negligent. For the first 3 months of my pregnancy I was walking around with sky high glucose levels which puts you in grave danger of a miscarriage. When I complained to the clinic rather than handling this situation like adults Dr Venkat just threw her toys out of the pram and refused to treat me further leaving me in a very frightening situation at 3 months pregnant with nowhere else to go. They also plain face lied that I had been made aware of the situation. I honestly couldn’t believe it. This is why we are currently seeking advice to take this further. But in the meantime I implore anyone looking to seek treatment, PLEASE GO ELSEWHERE. Don’t make the same mistake that we did. There is so much more to say but it’s impossible to say it all here. It was 1.5 years of trauma. I’d be more than happy to contact anyone who needs further advice or who would like to join forces in stopping this clinic from further treating people like this. Please look me up on social media and I will reply. Samantha Clare Kininmonth.
★☆☆☆☆ Harley Street Fertility Clinic 9 months ago
Whilst we are now pregnant, I would advise everyone to use any other fertility clinic than Harley Street Fertility Clinic (HSFC). Please read all other 1 and 2 star reviews - we didn't and truly regret it now. All these poor reviews give a true and fair representation as to your journey with this clinic. Total lack of communication, unexpected bills every step of the way (including one bill on the day of £1950 that we weren't told about!). This clinic is solely there to earn as much money as possible out of each patient. Most concerning was that at the end of February 2025, unbeknownst to us, amongst the drugs that had been instructed to take for the fertility treatment, HFSC included a steroid - Prednisone. HSFC did not highlight that this was a steroid or the reason why it was prescribed; it was included with the other supplements we were instructed to take for the fertility treatment. We now aware that this steroid is extremely likely to cause hypoglycemia especially in the size of doses given. This led to my partner being hyperglycemic for 3 months, and not finding this out until the first midwife appointment. This puts the person at a high risk of miscarriage and could have been very detrimental to either Mother or Babies health. We highlighted the above to HSFC in the form of an official complaint, and we're simply told all medical guidelines were met during their investigation. All of the other fertility clinics have better reviews than HSFC - my advise would be to take note of that.
★☆☆☆☆ Harley Street Fertility Clinic Edited a year ago
Highly advise against using this clinic. Please read the multitude of 1 and 2 star reviews. The reviews and ratings are a true reflection of their complete lack of professionalism. Please also notice that the most recent “positive” reviews are vague and have the exact same number of reactions - which, if you read to the end of the review, is clearly an attempt to course correct their negative reviews. We had rescheduled our appointment with the clinic and upon arriving, not only was the receptionist rude saying we were late for our appointment which turned out to be a scheduling error on their part as we had email proof, but she then proceeded to give an excuse saying she had called us - which she would have called 30 minutes after the time they had scheduled us for. After realizing their error, she just said to go “empty your bladder” without specifying which of us or even where the restroom is. She then tells us that we need to wait an additional 45 minutes for our appointment because the doctor and her manager are in a meeting as a result of their scheduling error. When I told her this was there fault, she goes back to the “I tried to call you” even though that would have been 30 minutes after what they had scheduled in error, so another demonstration of their incompetence. All in all, a waste of time. Please run away from this clinic - I regret having even done the consultation here since we’re now locked in to doing the rest of the testing. If you are someone that has a schedule or other commitments to abide to that you need to schedule around, this is not the right clinic for you. Pick any other clinic but this one. Amending this review following our last and final call with Dr Venkat to review our results. She showed up 10 minutes late to our virtual appointment and said she is only human and the other couple were upset and continued to ask questions which is why she was late. This is another example of how the clinic does not respect the patients’ time - if they anticipate couples will have questions, they should schedule longer appointments and not put the onus for the delay on the next patient. Not only that, but they did not even have my husband’s results to review on the call which demonstrates the level of disorganization. After we waited for someone to bring the results up to review, we expressed that we would no longer be patients at this clinic. Dr Venkat asked why and when we begun to explain our experience, she raised her voice at us and refused to let us speak. She said we caused enough damage to her clinic and that’s why they have had no customers in 2 weeks. When we tried to speak, she continued to cut us off. When we said it was inappropriate that she was yelling at us or to say that we caused damage to our clinic, she proceeded to say she was not yelling and that she didn’t say we caused damage, but our 1 star review did, which meant she was clearly already aware of our experience. The clinic’s manager had previously called us to try to see how we could remedy the experience and not post a 1 star review, but I do not feel that it would be appropriate to any other person to not be aware of the terrible experience we had prior to investing in any type of fertility treatment. This clinic is extremely unprofessional. As much as I’d like to think it was just the receptionist and scheduling that went awry, the primary doctor herself proved that the clinic is systemically unprofessional. Ultimately, it is your choice where you choose to invest your money and time for your fertility consultations and treatment. I cannot, in my good conscience, recommend this clinic to anyone. We will be sharing our feedback to HFEA following this last, and thankfully final, experience.
★☆☆☆☆ Harley Street Fertility Clinic Edited a year ago
I don’t think words or a 1* review will ever be enough to truly discourage anyone thinking of undergoing any fertility services from HSFC to please DON’T DO it. They put myself and my husband through 5 months of nothing but stress, disappointment and heartache that I will never get back, let alone £1000s. My final straw was when I ended up having to see an emergency GP followed by an urgent referral to a gynaecologist through the NHS and being told my treatment and advice from the clinic was completely negligent and my condition was caused by them! Not a single nurse or consultant bothered putting my care first and instead prioritised giving me advice that only favoured me hopefully getting pregnant and getting their success rates up. They then had the cheek to ask me if I wanted to go in and PAY to be examined by their consultant which blew my mind as this was something that should have happened before I had to receive NHS treatment. Add this to the months of no communication from nurses, having to chase multiple times to get hold of anyone, not being given enough medication in cycles (which I realised and flagged myself) resulting in me having to find a local pharmacist to accept an emailed prescription from them in order to get hold of what I needed in time, turning up to appointments (which took me 2 hours to get to) and being told the nurse didn’t book me in, travelling home (for 2 hours) after an appointment and being called 4 hours later to say please come back you need more medication, to turning up to collect medication and being told you only need 1 dose but we sell it in doses of 4 so you need to pay x4 the amount of what you actually need and just throw the rest away. The nurses have no compassion whatsoever and every time you go in or call you speak to someone new. There is nothing personal or supportive about your experience with them. The list could go on and on and considering I used this clinic to help me conceive after being unsuccessful, they caused me more stress than I ever could have imagined and I never want anyone else to experience it. Whatever response they reply to this please ignore. I asked for compensation and they said they felt they were “responsive to all concerns raised”. How they think any of the above was responsive let alone acceptable sums them up! IN RESPONSE TO YOUR REPLY HSFC: You never asked me for a letter from another medical professional and if you had I would have sent it to you, especially if this was what was needed as "part of the investigation into my complaint." You also never once told me that an examination by one of your consultants would be at no cost. But to be honest, this is all absolutely NOT the point. The point of my review and complaint against you was that this was something you should have done when I came to the clinic initially with heavy bleeding, not offered to me AFTER I had already done via the NHS because your clinic wouldn't. I am complaining about the lack of duty of care you gave me when you should have, not afterwards. So your point is completely void. I would love to know what appropriate corrective actions you took because the service didn't change until the day I walked away from the clinic? Regarding your last point, I absolutely can provide you with a report from the Gynaecology Consultant I saw stating what it was and that it was caused by what you were giving me. But what would I even gain from doing that? You've already told me you won't be compensating me so what is the point? I'm also not quite sure what you mean by we want to clarify that some of the claims in your review do not align with our records? I wrote you an email listing every single complaint in it which you came back to me to say you won't be doing anything about? So that's also completely false!
★☆☆☆☆ Harley Street Fertility Clinic a year ago
I am absolutely appalled by the disgraceful and negligent treatment my sister endured at this clinic. I was present for many of her phone interactions with the staff, and the experience has been nothing short of a nightmare. To make matters worse, the clinic responded to her recent review with blatant lies about her treatment and claims, so I’m writing this to set the record straight and bring some honesty to a place that clearly lacks it. This clinic has failed, on every level, to show the basic professionalism and compassion that anyone would expect from a facility handling such delicate, personal issues. They seem more interested in racking up ‘successful’ pregnancy numbers and lining their pockets than caring for the actual well-being of their patients. My sister was told to keep ‘trying’ despite consistent bleeding - advice that any responsible clinic would recognise as a cause of concern. It was only after an emergency GP appointment that we learned just how reckless and harmful their approach had been and that the ‘treatments’ the told her to keep taking were actually causing her symptoms and a mini procedure was needed. The lack of basic care and concern is honestly beyond words. I want to emphasise that this is not a complaint I make lightly and it’s not usually in my nature. I’m writing this because people deserve to know the risks of entrusting their care to this clinic. I value honesty, and I want to protect others from the emotional and physical harm my sister has suffered. I plan to bring this issue to several support groups as well, to make sure no one else has to endure the same nightmare. I hope this clinic reflects on this and puts their time and energy into prioritising the lives and well-being of its patients over profit and replying to google reviews with false information. No family should ever have to go through what we have because of their reckless, careless treatment.

★☆☆☆☆ Harley Street Fertility Clinic 2 years ago
Quite unimpressed that we were quoted one price when booked in, to only be given another more expensive price come the day of our appointment. This was eventually rectified after complaining multiple times before paying but should not be the case. Staff should not be misleading patients like this or promising a price that is not accurate. It is dishonest and misleading. Not to mention when we turned up for a couple’s consultation they nearly let us leave without taking my husband’s blood or urine sample. We had to tell them it was required.
★☆☆☆☆ Harley Street Fertility Clinic 3 years ago
Like the other reviewers have said, Harley Street fertility clinic are just after your money! They don't care, unprofessional and negligent. I called and emailed several times following my first appointment, no one has responded. Like the other reviewer has said, I need to spend £6500 to continue treatment - why wasn't I told that first? £250 just to speak to the Dr? No thanks. The only way to stop such charlatans is to vote with your feet. I wish I read the reviews online first before parting with my money.
★☆☆☆☆ Harley Street Fertility Clinic 5 years ago
I'm writing this in hope other women read this review before consider using their services. I have been to another clinic after visiting this one and I can compare both. HSFC has very outdated machines. Doctors were only nice on my first consultation. On the follow up - which was a reading of my blood exam - she didn't explain anything to me. I felt like she was waiting for me to ask questions but they didn't send me my blood exam with me so I didn't even know what to ask. At the end It was rushed and the explanation very superficial. I asked if they could please send me my blood exam, and after a couple of hours I had it, so I contacted the clinic to book another call so I could better understand my results. They said they would charge ( a large sum of money I must say ) me for the follow up and I was so disappointed that I decided to go for another clinic. I preferred to pay more to do it all over again with another clinic than stay with HSFC. I think they lack ethic and professionalism. They charge the same as other clinics, however their equipment is old, their staff needs training and their doctors need to be more passionate o for what they do. I felt robbed, but at the end it was a good thing because I decided to go for another clinic and OMG, such a difference. Their equipment was SO much better, the doctors were so attemptive and thorough on their explanation, during and after procedure. So maybe It was good that I received such a bad treatment from this clinic because I am very happy to not have gone forward with Harley fertility clinic. I recommend any women that has had their first consultation with them to visit another clinic before they making a final decision.
★☆☆☆☆ Harley Street Fertility Clinic 2 years ago
A pretty traumatic experience especially for people going through a very challenging period of their life: 1) HSFC (especially the coordinators/front of house team) did not seem to know what was going on. They always required me (as the patient) to follow up even though they said they would and they kept referring me to other "colleagues", so I was bounced around from team to team with no clear answer. When I asked to speak to my Doctor (as I was hoping that the doctor would be my single source of truth), they said I would have to pay another £240 for a consultation (ridiculous given that I had already paid a fairly substantial amount to even get that far and had not received much in return!) Definitely feels very salesy and pushy. 2) I felt the medical "advice/recommendation" was misleading and scared me into pursuing a treatment with the clinic. It was only after going to a SEPARATE clinic and paying a SEPARATE consultation fee, that I realised that I was only told half the story. I'd recommend anyone who visits ANY fertility clinic to first read the book: It Starts with the Egg by Rebecca Fett. It is in the fertility clinic's interest to get you to do a treatment and so it's good to get some impartial advice beforehand so please do your research. 3) This isn't exclusive to this clinic but to almost all the fertility clinics out there - I am disappointed that the costs for treatments aren't more clearly documented, shared and most importantly regulated. Sure there's a price list which they can distribute (with large price ranges and names of complicated drug names etc) and I do appreciate that treatments will vary from person to person. BUT often, it feels like fertility clinics will only share the prices to your personalised treatment plan once you're already quite far invested into the process and so will have to pay the ad-hoc/hidden costs that they sometimes spring on you throughout the process. Furthermore, informing patients of the costs so late on does not allow patients to do proper cost-comparisons at the beginning and so creates this horrible power dynamic. I don't know what the solution to this fertility-clinic-collusion is other than maybe for there to be stricter price regulations and for clinics to create personalised treatment plans much earlier than they currently do.
★☆☆☆☆ Harley Street Fertility Clinic 4 years ago
I can give only 1 star to this clinic, it is because of one person. At first when i was told harley street was the best i felt so happy to get ib touch with them to solve my problem. So last year april i contacted them , receptionalist was very kind she gave me an appointment with dr. Geetha venkat, and charged 250 pounds which i paid when she spoke i gave my scan report from previous doctor , and explained all my problems, she was giving me hope at first saying dont worry we can offer you financial plan to pay monthly , we will cure everything and send you home happily. To be honest i was in top as im gonna get cure atleast by paying money. Then they took scan and i said i have these many fibroids to be operated. Everything was going good until that . And geetha venkat called me and told wellington hospital does the surgery but you have to pay 6500 pounds. 🙄 which i told her i been through domestic violence and as i told her earlier im financialy affected thats why at first point i asked you about paying monthly, she just said nothing else i can do either you pay full or nothing can be done. After that i tried to reach them one person from admin she was so nice , she answered and she said the same thing. Then i asked her why did the doctor gave me hope that finance can be done. But you are telling me we only offer wellington hospital which you have to pay in full. Why cant she tell this in first step before charging me £400. She didnt have any response. Only she said doctors doesnt know the exact prices which is a big joke. Finally i said you guys wasted my 3 months hoping for the best and my money and time as well and i asked her to refund my money. She said you have consulted our doctor and did scanning so nothing there to be refunded. I paid 250 for nothing to talk with dr. Geetha venket. So i asked them .. after that these people never pass my phone to anyone. They will ask my name if i say they are busy they will call you back but only once i received. No refund no treatment. Waste of time . Many people would had good experience over there. Good luck to you but i had the bad experience. Thanks a lot for receptionalist , person who did scanning for me, admin very kind lady and a black i think somalia nurse who did blood test was amazing. Except the doctor
★☆☆☆☆ Harley Street Fertility Clinic 2 years ago
Awful, unprofessional, money grabbing clinic and have to call consistently to get a response “someone will get back to you”. Absolute shambles and shameful behaviour from management who just cower at any confrontation rather than dealing with the patient. And before any person from HSFC replies to this saying “we don’t have a record of your name” - I’m using a pseudo name to protect my identity unlike you guys have done! Waste of money and time. Avoid or I promise you will be writing a similar review as they really don’t give a rats a$$ about you all they care about is your money, believe me
★☆☆☆☆ Harley Street Fertility Clinic 7 years ago
They can improve by not constantly and forcefully overselling, actually see people on time, especially as they charge a fortune the least they could do is stick to patient booking times they need to make sure their staff read the notes!!!! and not ask they patient what they were supposed to give and most importantly not employ the use of Debt Collection Agencies!!!! When a simple email or telephone call would suffice to resolve their issues, I think the GM needs to learn some personal skills and then train his team!!! definitely not worth the recommendation especially as there are many other clinics around, would avoid at all costs!!!
★☆☆☆☆ Harley Street Fertility Clinic 5 years ago
Currently extremely annoyed with this company. My partner and I are going through IVF as part of the egg share program. She has been put on medication to link her cycle with the receiving doner of her eggs. There was initially confusion with how long she should be taking her tablets (received 17 days worth, but told to take 10 by one person, and then 17 by another??). 9 days in, and my partners period has started (heavily, painful and stressful). She has been constantly calling the clinic to advise them that she has started her period, and requested to know if this is normal, and what the next steps / follow up plan is (we have been kept in the dark about this). After calling them 3 days in a row, she has been told that someone will call her back - no surprise to me, they haven't. I now have an emotional suffering partner, who is going through this delicate stressful process, with no communication and complete lack of support from the clinic. Horrific service and very disheartening to what should be a process made easier by the clinic.
★☆☆☆☆ Harley Street Fertility Clinic 7 years ago

★☆☆☆☆ Harley Street Fertility Clinic 4 years ago
Absolutely awful. Didn’t receive any response to my emails, told blood tests had expired and had to be redone. When I shared via email the date & content of the email asking when I could book in/how long these were valid for several months earlier my email & subsequent chasers were completely ignored.
★☆☆☆☆ Harley Street Fertility Clinic 2 years ago
Don’t ever buy supplements from the clinic, they sell at a price x10 times more expensive than outside without telling you the price first. Truly a rip-off clinic. I think that says a lot about their attitude towards patients.
★☆☆☆☆ Harley Street Fertility Clinic 5 years ago
I was with Harley Street Fertility clinic for a year and had undergone multiple IVF cycles. To say that it was a positive experience would be a lie. Often the majority of appointments running late and the doctors treated you more like a number rather than a person. Our first cycle I had my beta blood work and progesterone blood work done early on Saturday morning and was called an hour before close to pick up more progesterone. My husband rushed to get there (we live outside of London) and asked for the needles (he meant injections). The receptionist then handed him a SEALED bag and off he went home. When we got home, I went to take my injection and found I only had syringes in the bag. The doctor knew I was coming to get my progesterone prescription, because they had put me on a higher dosage. I then called their out of hours number and talked to Dr Venkat. She told me that she couldn't do anything for me, it was my husband's fault for asking for needles and that the nurses had asked him if he meant the medication (this was a lie). I then asked her if she could send the prescription to a chemist for me and she said no. When I asked her what would happen to my baby, she said that my beta was low anyways and that without the progesterone I could lose the baby. I cried to her on the phone, this was my first positive pregnancy test in 5 years. I begged her to not let me lose the baby because of this. She told me to not put this on her and she said 'any loss is your husband's fault'. She then hung up the phone on me. An hour later she called me back and agreed to send in a prescription. After that I did a further 2 rounds of IVF with them, I felt trapped there as I had 12 frozen embryos and didn't want to discard them. After each failed cycle, it appeared to me that the treatment provided contradicted my issues that caused my infertility. For example, I have elevated killer cells, but was given a scratch and transferred 2 embryos at a time. This would cause more killer cells to attack the cervix. When I suggested this could be why the transfer wasn't successful in my follow up with the Dr, the dr acknowledged this could have had an impact and that we could do it differently the following time. Surely the doctor would have considered this before my transfer, especially since they are the experts? After having various tests, treatments and procedures done, I felt deflated and gave up. This story has a happy ending, 3 years later without any IVF or medical support, I gave birth to a healthy baby boy. While I do not regret trying through IVF, I wish I took the time to research clinics carefully and hope sharing my experience will help people find the right clinic for them. X

Outrageous more than 1 hour late, I recommend going to another clinic

★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) 3 months ago
I am writing this review to warn others who may be considering CRGH. Unfortunately, my experience has been deeply disappointing and distressing. 1. Incorrect stimulation protocol (rookie mistake) My consultant (Jara Ben Nagi) prescribed an inappropriate stimulation dose despite having full access to my previous IVF protocol. In my earlier cycle at another clinic, I was given 375 units, which resulted in 4 blastocysts. At CRGH, I was initially prescribed 325 units, which was only increased to 375 units after 3 days, when it was already too late. This cycle resulted in just 2 blastocysts. When I challenged my consultant, she admitted she could have prescribed a higher dose from the start. Correct stimulation dosing is basic IVF care, and this mistake should not have happened. 2. Lack of transparency during egg collection Egg collection was performed by another consultant (Efsthatios Theodorou) . After the procedure, I was told that 6 mature eggs were collected, which was shocking given that 17 follicles were visible on ultrasound. When I asked how many eggs were collected in total, both consultants said “all” — yet were unable to provide a number. To this day, I still do not know how many eggs were actually retrieved. The explanation provided was confusing and inconsistent, and even the embryology team could not confirm the total number. This lack of transparency is extremely concerning, especially as my previous IVF cycle at another clinic resulted in 11 eggs collected, 10 of which were mature. 3. Poor clinical support during the implantation phase I have an underactive thyroid and take levothyroxine, with regular monitoring through my GP. Before transfer, my TSH was below 2.5. After transfer, it increased to 3.3, which can negatively affect implantation. I contacted the clinic in a state of genuine panic and shared my blood results via screenshots from my NHS app, explaining that an official GP letter would take time. I urgently asked whether my medication dose needed adjusting. A nurse called Anna refused to escalate the issue due to the format of the results. Only after I was extremely distressed — shouting and close to tears — did she agree to ask a doctor for advice and promised a same-day call. I am still waiting for that call. Why my concern and extreme worry wasn’t priority but following a procedure to the point of absurdity is? 4. My Consultant left without notification During this same call, I was told — for the first time — that my consultant had left the clinic. No one informed me of this beforehand, nor was I told who my new consultant is, despite the fact that I am still in the implantation phase. This is completely unacceptable. 5. Extremely expensive and not worth it CRGH is a very expensive clinic, and based on my experience, it is absolutely not worth the cost. Poor communication, questionable clinical decisions, lack of transparency, and inadequate patient support made an already stressful IVF journey far worse. Do not use this clinic. If you are deciding between clinics or just starting your fertility journey, I strongly recommend looking elsewhere.
★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) 5 months ago

★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) 8 months ago
1 star is for the technical teams such as nurses and embryologists who are kind, informative and professional.... the rest of the organisation is a total mess! I would NOT recommend coming here, ever. They are disorganised, dont communicate, misplace information, dont answer emails, and dont call back as promised. It adds a level of stress and emotional strain for a process that is already likely to be challenging. I have cried with frustration and distress just trying to communicate with their Finance department. The sudden closure of their city office and transition to GPS has exacerbated an already disfunctional practice. If I had choice, I would not trust this organisation with such an important process! However I've already paid upfront for treatment, so now I'm stuck. Note: this review is not a reflection of the outcome of treatment... we haven't even got that far yet! We havent even got to the 'emotional part' of treatment and I've wanted to give up and not proceed so many times already out of sheer frustration and distress working with this commercial yet unprofessional organisation.

★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) 6 months ago
The experience with this clinic has been frustrating from start to finish. Booking appointments is an absolute nightmare — there’s constant confusion about availability, and it often feels like no one communicates internally. The miscommunication between the finance and admin teams has been especially stressful, leading to repeated billing errors and unclear charges. Customer service is another major issue. It’s nearly impossible to get timely responses, and when you do, the staff often seem disorganised and unhelpful. For a clinic dealing with sensitive and important matters, the lack of professionalism is really disheartening.

★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) 10 months ago
I had a very disappointing experience at CRGH City Clinic, specifically with Dr. Javier Cordero. The ultrasound follicle tracking consultation lasted less than one minute, with no proper measurements taken — only vague and inconsistent follicle counts that were later changed without explanation. To verify, I went to another clinic the next day, where I received a thorough and professional scan with completely different results. On top of that, I paid for three blood test metrics but later discovered that only the AMH test was done — something the clinic never disclosed until I was contacted by their financial department. While CRGH eventually refunded all my payments after I raised a formal complaint, the time and trust lost cannot be recovered. Worse, no one explained how they plan to address these serious quality issues. I cannot recommend this clinic. The service I received was far below the standards expected from an accredited fertility clinic, and such carelessness can lead to serious consequences for patients seeking treatment.

★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) a year ago
The worst experience of any clinic I have been to. Challenges run from being sent multiple contradicting plans to being sent unexpected invoices the day before appointments and threatened with cancelling treatments if those invoices are not paid. From being sent private medical details of other patients to having to project manage the process myself because departments don’t speak to each other. I’ve just received a letter for payment years after using them with no idea what it’s for and with no communication for many years about anything outstanding. If you raise any concerns about their process it is never acknowledged it is their fault. Look through trust pilot rather than google for what appears to be honest reviews. Another great resource is glassdoor if you want to get the employee’s perspective.
★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) 11 months ago

★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) a year ago
If i could give 0 starts I would. This place is awful and I seriously do not recommend for anyone. It has taken me 3 years to find the courage to write this review. They do not have any pre or after care. This place has left me mentally, emotionally and even physically distraught. First, they bruised both my arms with all the trial and error blood tests they were trying to take. Second, when things don't go to plan there is no support no explanation and certainly no aftercare. Third, they only talk to you when they need an invoice paid. I asked several times to speak to someone about what happens next and everytime I was told I'd get a call back. I am still waiting 3years for a call back! I do not recommend anyone to come and waste their money or their hope here.

★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) Edited a year ago
DO NOT USE. Please women, do not use this centre. It has taken me so long to take the courage to speak out about this place. It is a battery farm. The doctors do not have expertise gynae knowledge, the after care is poor. If a woman dies during child labour and dies, but delivery of the child is successful, is their benchmark of success- as that analogy was how they saw my case, that my extraction was successful. CRGH failed to give the right medication, monitor my situation properly and I was rushed to A&E post procedure, suffered OHSS and now potentially unable to conceive or even carry a child naturally, because of the damage caused by CRGH doctors. Due to the amount I've suffered - it has involved so much care - and it has turned out that most gynae's know not to use CRGH. I've also come across other women who've suffered, but have been too traumatised to talk about it. N.B Please note that I have made lots of attempts to speak to the clinic, and that was even more traumatic- where every attempt was made to dismiss the negligence.

★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) 5 years ago
Having done lots of research into private clinics we went with CRGH based on their strong statistics. We did 2 rounds of IVF and and IUI after being told that after our 2 cycles of IVf had failed there was nothing more that could be done. The consultant himself was very nice and professional and i got the feeling that he felt let by the other staff at times. I was informed i had endometriosis by a Sonographer after a HYCosy Scan. She shrugged her shoulders when i asked what was going on and why this had only been picked up at that time and she replied she assumed i knew. Clearly it was. shock and delivered in a very unsympathetic way. I feel that this has been the main issue with my infertility and this should have been looked into before starting treatment to rule it out. No pelvic exams or questions were asked about possible problems before treatment started. Its really the doctor that should give you diagnosis and reassure you. I was told there was a chance it could be cancer and naturally panicked after being told by a nurse that i should seek an MRI scan elsewhere as my symptoms were no longer their problem. Overall the service was shabby and we lost a lot of faith and trust in how things were being run. We were faced with huge disappointments but we should have been reassured at regular stages as the process is not cheap. Having left the clinic disheartened and angry at the way things were handled we have now found another clinic which have given us hope and made us feel it is not the end of the road for our fertility.
★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) 2 years ago

★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) a year ago
Rude…. I rang up as a new patient enquiry. I required a specific cost, I was told that no one in that department was currently available to answer that question at the moment. I asked if they could call me back with the cost. I was told no and asked to ring back in 20minutes. I politely said that I had requested that they call me back when they had the information that I needed. The person on the phone went completely silent, and needless to say I have had no phone call back. I have called another clinic in London who were incredibly helpful. Seems that new patients aren’t important to this clinic.
★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) Edited 5 years ago

★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) Edited 2 years ago
Edit (27.08.2023): I'm editing in light of CRGH asking me to contact them. Don't be fooled by this token response. I tried to raise concerns a few times and they had no interest in what I had to say. All they care about is making money. Our consultant had no interest in doing what was best for us. We were kept in the dark about treatment decisions (despite us asking) and were told we couldn't speak to a doctor whenever we asked to. I'm so angry and upset. Original review: I am writing this review as someone who had a few cycles at CRGH. The clinic caused me nothing but stress.  The main problems were finance, nursing and admin. The finance department at CRGH are unprofessional, dishonest and incompetent. They caused me a significant amount of stress at a time when I was meant to be minimsing stress. A few examples of what they did: - Overcharged me many times (this was normal practice). - Regularly and aggressively harassed me for payments I had already made. - Charged me for procedures I did not have. - Quoted me the most expensive prices for tests when cheaper options were available. - Said I couldn't proceed with an appointment until I had paid money I did not even owe. - Quoted me one price and invoiced for a higher price, saying the prices had gone up. - Regularly sent me invoices late and expected payment within two days. - Never apologised for their mistakes, despite making errors regularly. They sometimes even tried to gaslight me and pretend they hadn't made a mistake. - Conveniently ignored my messages querying costs. Please please check every single item on every single invoice. Do not give finance your credit card details and do not authorise them to take payments at their will. You will regret this. CRGH are very opaque about their pricing and you end up paying A LOT more than you originally think you will.  There's always added extras that crop up. I should not have had to spend my time arguing with finance on the phone and trying to get refunds or credits applied but that is what I had to do. There seems to be no standards in the finance team at all. They had a significant negative impact on my mental health. Hardly what you need when undergoing a stressful process. Nursing staff will tell you that many patients complain about the finance team but they just wash their hands off it and do nothing to help. These nurses are regulated by the NMC. How does being complicit in this unethical practice fit with the NMC professional standards of practice? Being a nurse means behaving ethically and challenging poor practice. You will see from many reviews that the finance team's unethical practice is commonplace.  However, CRGH do nothing about it. This makes me think that overcharging is deliberate practice (in the hope that patients will not notice). Some of the nursing staff are abrupt, uncaring and rude. I have no idea why they're working in fertility given they have no empathy.  They were constantly making mistakes and never apologised or took accountability.  They are completely disorganised and I had to point out their mistakes several times (e.g. prescription errors, not knowing my treatment plan, not responding to portal messages). They also seemed uninterested in my questions and concerns. They act like patients are an inconvenience to them. Your consultant does not oversee your treatment and decisions are made by different doctors each time you have a scan. What is the point of having a named consultant if they do not oversee your treatment? It left me feeling mistrustful, in the dark and confused. This is not ok for the amount of money I paid. When I used to call up, I would invariably end up speaking to admin staff who were patronising, rude and obstructive. It's almost as if they are trying to put obstacles in the way. CRGH have no interest in accepting feedback and learning from it. Whenever I raised concerns, nothing ever changed and I just got fobbed off or gaslit. They just close ranks and make out you are the problem. This speaks volumes about what they really think of patients.
★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) 2 years ago
Do not expect to be taken care of properly. Even for medication you are the one who supposed to be knowing what doses and names your medicine is! Nurses and scan specialists are so nice and friendly they care and they are gentle, treat …
★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) Edited 4 years ago
We started treatment with CRGH with lots of hope after a failed cycle in NHS. The doctor asked us to redo lots of test (though we had done this a year back) and we did everything and spent a fortune hoping there will be some positive output. After all the investigation tests and history of tests from NHS, the doctor prepared a worst plan for Egg collection and the medicines suggested is also without any logic. The follicles were not not growing as expected and we asked the hospital to cancel the cycle as we did not expect many mature eggs (basic logic and we were correct, not sure what the doctors logic was). The doctor did not cancel the cycle and that means we paid a fortune for treatment and then as expected, there was only 2 average Embryo freezed (in NHS cycle we had 4 atleast). On the embryo transfer day, we asked for both the embryos to be transferred and the embryologists prepared only one and the excuse she gave was lame and she prepared one more in the last minute. When the doctor transferred the embryo itself, I knew this wont be successful as he itself was not confident and the blabbering he did was a proof (the NHS embryo transfer was smooth, but unfortunately miscarried). I did not even know the logic of CRGH asking us to do multiple Covid 19 test when we were not even visiting the hospital and there was no symptoms also. Anyway, we lost around 20K GBP for a BFN and what we feel is CRGH is only trying to extract money and no service at all. Update: I expected this standard template reply from the hospital, however the whole process was messed up big time and nothing was done correctly except sending invoices / debiting money on time.
★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) 10 months ago
Had a horrible experience that has left us scarred mentally and physically, as an overseas patient the neglect and lack of proper guidance and medical care was apathetic, would not recommend any overseas patient to consult this clinic, the …
★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) 5 years ago
Although I always read reviews, this is one of the first I have written. I did a huge amount of research before I chose a clinic, reading various reviews from many places. I chose CRGH because I met a consultant there who seemed professional and knowledgeable. I started the process and very soon I realised how impersonal and factory like the place was. Unfortunately they made a few mistakes with me and I started to loose trust. They accidentally changed my appointments without me knowing and changed my consultant in the middle saying I had requested it, which I had not. There were also various hidden costs, such as the price of a test does not include the results for which you have to pay another amount to receive! The finance team are very active. The consultant herself was cold and impersonal, and in one confidential phone meeting with her, which she insisted must be on loud speaker, she didn’t let me know that there were other staff in the room until I questioned it. I kept going as I had already started and didn’t want to go elsewhere in the middle. Unfortunately in the middle I was diagnosed with a condition and the consultant Dr S actually shouted at me as to why I had not told her before and why I had ‘withheld’ information. I told her that I had no idea I had this condition and it had only come up in a recent blood test. She was abrupt and challenging and more concerned about legally covering herself then acknowledging that it may be a shock to me too that I had just found out I had this condition. Though she was knowledgeable and experienced, she was cold, unfriendly and robotic and made me feel awful. I tried to speak to the manager and he was very defensive that I was questioning a Consultant and put down the phone on me, saying he did not need to speak to me if he didn’t know who I was. Suffice to say I would never go back there again. Several of the nurses there were lovely as were the friendly receptionists, but if the manager himself is so rude, then if there is an issue, there is simply no-one to speak to who cares. I wish everyone all the best in their journey and hope you find the right clinic and consultant for you.
★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) 8 years ago
Very expensive, not attentive, no one available to talk to. We started to investigate with CRGH in Nov.2017. We had all the tests …
★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) 7 years ago
I had a negative experience and they were unwilling to address any of my concerns. Problems: 1:They don’t put anything in writing for you, (no appointment times, or notes from your scans, nothing tangible) so I had to take notes myself constantly. Even emails are electronically held in their system and you can only access them temporarily, then they disappear, so it’s hard to keep track of all the info you need to know. 2: There’s an awkward reception system where they take your photograph and there’s always a camera on you and yet it’s hard to get in through the main door from the high street 3: the consultant I met with has limited social skills. He puts his foot in it often with very sensitive topics. He only reviews your notes once the appointments have begun. He gives advice off the top of his head so you’re never sure whether it’s considered medical advice or a random idea he thinks might be fun to try. 4: sometimes I would have to call or email 3 times before someone would call me back 5: it’s very expensive and the costs are not clearly revealed until the invoices arrive 6: they prescribe medication that they will tell you is accessible in all pharmacies and health shops but then you will find the medication is actually only available directly from the clinic, at triple the cost 7: I was told the final and most important procedure - the egg collection - would be under general anaesthetic and pain free. Instead they changed their minds at the last minute so I was awake and in pain throughout the surgery. They did not offer additional pain relief at any point. 8: maybe all of the above is worth it because it is a success for some people and they are able to have children through this process. I don’t know if this is true though. It felt like a scam to me, because after all this, after many expensive months of scans and appointments and injections and attempts they did not manage to collect and freeze more than one egg. So overall had a very negative experience, and unsuccessful, and they were unwilling to address any of my post- procedure concerns.
★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) Edited 4 years ago
EDIT TO ADD AS I'VE HAD A LOT OF MESSAGES ABOUT THIS REVIEW Sadly CRGH never reached out to me again even after I emailed as …
★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) 2 years ago
Would not recommend especially if you are NHS patient. Unlike St Thomas or Queen Charlotte, if you have a problem such as needing fibroids removed before ivf, you will need to be referred back to your GP and face huge wait time. Consultants are very business like. Ivf remains crude and has not advanced much in the last 20 years, consultants give too postive perspectives to clients without really outlining the risks. On PDG, they are particualy bad, they don't tell patients that there is decreased pregnancy rates with PGD especially with age and people may actually be better off trying naturally. . This establishment is nurse lead (all of whom are nice), gaining access to consultants is very difficult however, and the attitude of some of the consultant are very poor. The establishment should have feedback system for each consultant after each consultation.
★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) 2 years ago
Disgraceful. Overrated. Unprofessional. After two failed treatments (paid upfront before any procedures take place) and after 14 months when our last treatment took place, their idiotic Finance …
★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) 7 years ago
Booked in for couples fertility tests- staff is nice enough though quite dismissive, nurse was lovely. Unfortunately, this place has huge financial targets to meet & it shows. Understandably the bottom line is that patients want results, however, the brash, in-out manner employed is awful. They charge extortionate amounts and even so their manner leaves much to be desired. An assessment is just that and even though CRGH is a fertility clinic, there are various levels and options involved regarding fertility which need to be customised to each patients needs. Unfortunately CRGH fell short with this- no options, alternatives, or anything other than IVF we’re discussed. Biggest doozy though- even though results were good for both me & hubby, Dr we saw for follow-up insisted we do IVF repeatedly, gave us no alternative options and embarrassingly the ultrasound, blood tests & Dr didn’t pick up that I was already pregnant (which I didn’t know at the time)!! Missed it completely!! Even with leading questions to the Dr at follow-up who just just interested in selling her IVF treatment!! We didn’t even bother telling her, her consultation was inadequate and she had no interest other than securing an IVF booking. As a medical precessional myself I expect higher standards. Not impressed!!

★☆☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) 6 years ago
Terrible customer service. Medical procedures instill confidence and specialists are helpful and knowledgeable, but their nurses and reception are absolutely clueless. As an existing client I've been trying to schedule an appointment for ages now - they don't reply to emails or return calls. We've been overcharged /financial department is the only proactive one!/ And there's only one nurse actually capable of drawing blood - I've had my veins slaughtered a number of times before I invariably end up with their senior nurse.. I can see why they have a good success rate - they do a lot more screenings than other clinics and their specialists appear professional, but likely because of their reputation they don't feel they need to also deliver customer service so get ready for total chaos outside the doctor's office. We're already tied up with them too much to switch and have no complaints with the medical side of things but we're wondering how professional you can be medically if you can't get administration right - the impression is of chaos within the organisation and that's not trust instilling at all.

★★☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) Edited 9 months ago
I’ve been happy with the medical care I’ve received so far. The doctors and embryologists have been excellent, and I’ve felt well looked after on the clinical side. However, the administrative side of the clinic has been extremely frustrating and really detracts from the overall experience. There are frequent administrative errors that you, as the patient, end up having to chase all while managing full-time work and the stress of IVF. Previously, the old portal system worked well, and messages were responded to quickly. Phone lines were accessible, and things felt manageable. However, since the new system was introduced a month ago, things have gone significantly downhill. The portal is not user-friendly, messages sent through it are not received or responded to, and phone lines are so overwhelmed that I’ve waited 40–50 minutes multiple times without ever getting through. Emails are replied to, but often days later, which is unhelpful when you need to schedule time-sensitive scans or treatments. I’ve raised my concerns via email a couple of times, but haven’t received a meaningful response. Most recently, a nurse failed to send a time-sensitive prescription, which required multiple follow-ups from me to resolve, without so much as an acknowledgment or apology. In a recent nurse consultation I was reassured the portal is now working, but that just isn’t the case, unless they are just choosing to ignore messages that I have sent. I’m already dreading having to contact CRGH to book in for a baseline scan. How am I meant to arrange something for the next 1-2 days when no one answers the phone, I can’t contact the nurses via portal and emails take at least 48 hours for a response. It’s an impossible system to navigate. What’s most disappointing is the lack of communication. Given that the new system clearly isn’t working, there’s been no formal update or warning that portal messages are going unanswered. This level of disorganisation is simply not acceptable, especially considering the high costs of treatment. To add to this, the finance team has made errors on contracts and charges in the past, so invoices need to be checked carefully. I understand that new systems can come with teething problems, but nearly a month on, there’s still no resolution, no timeframe for fixing the issues, and no accountability. Based solely on the administrative experience, I sadly cannot recommend the clinic at this time.
★★☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) 2 years ago
The doctors were very helpful and informative when you get the chance to talk to them. It is not easy to get a hold of the doctors if you have questions, the same goes for nurses. After going to another clinic we realised how indifferent …
★★☆☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) 2 years ago
We have had a very bad experience here. Reasons being: (1) the Dr needed to repeatedly be reminded of patient name and background, (2) No full blood profiling was taken before the cycle, (3) the Dr ignored the findings of a DNA fragmentation report we requested be carried out and (4) triggered too late. The one saving grace is the nurses and embryologists are all lovely and are competent in their jobs.

★★★☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) 4 months ago
We were discharged from the clinic with mixed feelings. Negatives: My partner was sometimes treated in a way that made her feel stupid for asking simple questions, and we received conflicting information on several occasions. In one cycle, the wrong cycle’s notes were initially used, meaning we nearly skipped a cycle unnecessarily; we avoided this only through persistence. When problems arose, some staff focused on blaming colleagues rather than patient care. Telephone staff sometimes lacked basic awareness of the clinic’s own processes and did not take the time to read notes properly, which was extremely stressful when trying to get answers and book appointments at critical points in our cycles. Some nurses lacked compassion and sensitivity, leaving my partner in tears after two appointments. At certain points, senior staff admitted the clinic was overwhelmed with calls/emails, making it difficult to contact the clinic. Peaks and troughs happen, but continuing to advertise for new patients while being overwhelmed by existing ones felt like putting profit before people. Pharmasure’s delivery service is excellent (the pharmacy the clinic use), but their prices are extortionate: despite being owned by IBSA (the manufacturer of most of the prescribed drugs), we were quoted between 25–100% more than third-party suppliers for the same drugs. The process sometimes felt like a generic process rather than tailored diagnosis and treatment. Finance was too detached with invoices and chasers arriving at insensitive times. The clinic is busy and can feel like an IVF factory at times. Positives: Some nurses - especially senior staff - were outstanding: kind, compassionate, responsive, and professional. We are particularly grateful to Jessica, Anca, Sue, and Libby, who went above and beyond. The lab staff were also empathetic, communicative, and professional. After we raised service issues, the clinic took steps to put things right, and we noticed a significant service improvement towards the end of our journey. Our consultant, Dr Wael Saab (one of two Dr Saabs at the clinic), was informative, listened to our questions, and provided good care. The portal resources were helpful. We became pregnant after two rounds of IVF (albeit it is early days), so the clinic delivered the desired outcome. We had confidence in the clinic via its results and reports from friends who are in the industry who vouched for the quality of the lab. Verdict: We are grateful for the result, but there were too many avoidable service issues; I would be cautious recommending the clinic because it caused unnecessary stress at times, but we did get the desired result and I am aware that patients at competitor clinics report similar issues. Some tips for other patients: - Do pre-IVF blood tests early (as these can delay treatment). - Start supplements early (ideally ~3 months before treatment). We took Ovum (women) and Proxeed (men). - Order drugs early to avoid last minute ordering stress and cost. - Use ASDA Pharmacy for IVF drugs: they offer IVF drugs at cost price, but do allow 3–5 days from order to collection (vs Pharmasure's overnight delivery service). Pls note ASDA require you to attend in person to order and collect with signed prescriptions + prescriptions need to be for whole boxes. We felt the extra hassle was worth the cost saving. - Advocate for yourself: ask questions and escalate to senior staff if you are unsatisfied with the answers. - Expect costs of ~£2–3k+ for testing, then £8k+ per cycle including drugs (2025 prices) - don't be caught out by the, much lower, headline price for a cycle that all clinics seem to use. The headline price does not include drugs, testing, and other standard procedures which are required. - If male factor infertility is identified during initial testing, consider seeing a specialist andrologist/fertility urologist outside of the clinic - as fertility clinics generally seem to have more consultants with a gynaecology background, yet male factor infertility effects 50% of couples.
