★★★☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) a year ago
I was supposed to do a egg freezing at CRHG. I did some tests at their clinic, and had a consultation call after. Everything felt very rushed, and not very personal. They said everything looked good and that I should start with the egg freezing process. I then went to another clinic (Care Fertility) that I got recommended by a friend. They got to see my test results from CRHG, and informed me that I had PCOS which CRHG never said anything about. Care Fertility were so helpful, and personal.
★★★☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) 3 years ago
The doctors and nurses at this place are amazing, very knowledgeable and by all metrics, acheive great results in terms of supporting people with fertility and genetic issues. However, the administrative structure of CRGH leaves a lot to be desired. You never know who you are meant to talk to to get information.
★★★☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) 5 years ago
I had 3 rounds of NHS funded PGD IVF. I am sure my main consultant had my best interests at heart but she had a rather brusque manner and spoke extremely quickly all the time, as though she were always in a rush to get me out the door. In general the staff were not very approachable which saddens me as I need PGD and CRGH are very strong in this area. I wish I had had a different experience as I then could have then stayed with CRGH for these next rounds of privately funded PGD.
★★★☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) 3 years ago
The doctors and nurses really care about their patients and will go above and beyond for you. The finance team on the other hand are extremely aggressive and have no qualms whatsoever with issuing invoices at the eleventh hour and then …
★★★☆☆ CRGH (The Centre for Reproductive and Genetic Health) 7 years ago
We chose CRGH because it also conducted research. We felt that this would mean they would be more update. Unfortunately, it just meant that they had little 'bed side manner'. I'll start off by saying that our two cycles were unsuccessful. However, my negative opinion was formed during the first cycle. Communication prior to implantation was excellent. I was given a cervix relaxant several hours before implantation, there was no where comfortable to go through this, either the very small reception area or, as I chose, cured up in great pain on a chair in a corridor of the adjacent dental building. I was not offered sedation for implantation, I struggle with smear tests but implantation was horrendous. As is advised, I was expecting to lie on the trolley for a half an hour after implantation, but no, after two minutes I was sent elsewhere. Second cycle, I thought I was pregnant, and their test confirmed this but at 7 weeks had a scan and they could not see anything. Told that the better quality scan was not working. Told to go back in a couple of weeks. The feeling of being pregnant soon went. Returned to clinic to be told very coldly that nothing there. I much later learned that I had probably had a chemical pregnancy. For various reasons we chose not to do a third cycle with the final embryos. After a couple of years of paying for them to be frozen we decided that we would donate them to anyone who needed embryos for pregnancy. This was a complicated process. We had to fill in many forms, including a heartfelt note to the 'future' child/ren at 18. A couple of years after we were contacted by the clinic as they had omitted to do some essential medical tests. When we went to the clinic again for an appointment, we were kept waiting a couple of hours, we didn't even have the appointment with the named person dealing with donation, but, in a tiny room. All the forms had to be completed again. On the floor of the 'office' were patient files, showing confidential information such as name and client number. We were even left alone in the room with them. We were expected to complete the forms in that cramped 'office'. We didn't, we had to get out of there and do the forms justice. We went to the nearby shopping plaza and did over a very very long coffee. So for a couple of years, our embryos were not even available for another couple to use. Heartbreaking. Now, the regulations have changed and embryos need to be from a woman under 40 and who has had a live birth. So now, my time with CRGH is finally coming to an end, after 10 years. Would I have been happy if the IVF had been successful, yes of course. Would I have still thought that the process at CRGH was poor, yes, absolutely.
★☆☆☆☆ Fertility Plus a year ago
Medical negligence by Fertility Plus has caused harm to my wife’s health and negatively impacted our future fertility treatment. Undergoing treatment at Fertility Plus has resulted in harm to my wife’s health and wellbeing due a serious failing in patient safety and care that led to her being given 2 months of the completely wrong medication. My wife did hours of research into suitable fertility clinics and identified Fertility Plus as being the one best placed to help her given the issues we have been facing during our fertility journey. We had an initial consultation with Mr Gudi who was very positive and felt that assisted conception would be best placed to help us at this stage so recommended that my wife takes Letrozole 5mg for a total of 3 months to help with ovulation so gave the initial course of this in a box. My wife then went on to see Dr Nisha for her appointments and was given a paper bag that had strips of medication in it. Although these were meant to be more of the Letrozole, we discovered that she was actually given Levothyroxine 25mcg. This is an entirely different medication that is used to treat hypothyroidism; a condition my wife does not suffer from. This is a serious mistake as not only was she not given the medication she was recommended, but rather been taking a medication that is not needed and has subsequently affected her thyroid gland. As a doctor myself, I am fully aware of the issues taking medication that you do not need can have on the body and as well as having taken this wrongly given medication for two months; she has also experienced side effects. Her menstrual cycle has become irregular; something that she has never experienced before in her life. This not only impacts our future fertility treatment but has also led to her worrying about what other effects taking this wrongly given medication has had on her health. It has also caused her to feel more anxious during a time that has already been most challenging both mentally and physically. We are both very upset given this serious mistake that has had a detrimental effect on my wife’s health and feel completely let down as well as having no trust in Fertility Plus. After informing them of their error and our concerns we were not even offered an apology. We sincerely hope no one else suffers as we have.
★☆☆☆☆ Fertility Plus a year ago
It's taken a while for me to be able to write a review given what a terrible experience I had at Fertility Plus at the start of last year. After reading very positive, nearly perfect reviews about this fertility clinic, I'm honestly so disappointed at how out of touch and impersonal this fertility practice is. I had some unfortunate news about my fertility results (very low AMH). I told Dr Thakre, that I had previously done blood tests for infertility and that my results had varied but she was concerned about a high FHS result that was previously documented. As a result, Dr Thakre said she wanted me to get another infertility test including an FSH test to confirm her consultation that I was in perimenopause. She told me that I could get the test done at the same lab that I had done my AMH test around the corner from the clinic after finishing our face to face appointment as she would be able to confirm her diagnosis the next day by phone call. Despite asking several questions at the appointment about the diagnosis, myself and my husband didn't leave feeling like we were any less informed about our journey ahead and the level of empathy provided came across as quite fake. The next day Dr Thakre called and advised me that my FHS had skyrocketed and unfortunately as she had explained the day before that my only options for bearing children would be through a donor egg with 40-45% success or conceiving naturally (but the chances were very minimal). IVF with my own eggs was not an option. During our call I asked the doctor to provide information about the treatment options available and she had offered to book some therapy sessions for me before making a decision about a treatment plan to move ahead with donor eggs. She told me I should speak with my GP about hormone replacement therapy and to get my bone density checked due to perimenopause. She also reassured me I didn't have to make the decision right away about conceiving with a donor egg. We again asked a few questions regarding what might our journey ahead might be like, but we were again left feeling that our interaction was cold and transactional. After the call I emailed to request a copy of my blood results. They emailed me back saying I had an outstanding bill of £234 and said they received my request and would get back to me shortly. After several back and forth questions I had regarding my blood tests, the reception team finally advised that they wanted me to pay for the additional blood test before they would release my own personal test information. Once I made the payment they released my results, and gave me the Gmail contact details of a therapist to pay for counselling sessions. After both my in-person appointment, phone call and the correspondence since then, my gut instinct told me that something about the service we received wasn't right so I decided to get a second opinion. I'm thankful I trusted my gut and decided not to continue with this clinic and found somewhere that treated me less like a number and more like a human.
★☆☆☆☆ Fertility Plus 2 years ago
I gave them a 1 star, based on how I was treated by Dr Shah. I thought he was heartless and cold but very direct. I do not think he knows how to deal with women and their feelings., to him it comes across as a joke! That's just my opinion.I should have seen Dr Gudi.
★☆☆☆☆ Fertility Plus 7 years ago
Extremely poor customer service, Paid all bills on time instead of a proper due diligence they sent my account to debt collection agencies. I had to provide the payment confirmation which was made 6 months before they sent my account to debt collection. I would not recommend to use their service and will not use it again.
★☆☆☆☆ Fertility Plus 3 years ago
Went to start fertility treatment and even though asking how I could prepare, I was not recommended to go off my birth control. I paid 400£ and then had to go off birth control for 3 months and then come back again.
★☆☆☆☆ Fertility Plus 3 years ago
well, I am just calling them to start IVF treatment. Nobody answers their phone. It must be a joke. off to a very good start, eh? At this day and age, an establishment that doesn't answer their public phone has no sympathy from me. sorry.
★☆☆☆☆ Fertility Plus Edited a month ago
DONT'T DELETE - I strongly advise caution before choosing Dr. Gudi and Harley Street Fertility Plus. Dr Gudi had asked us to see andrologist end of £16,000+ dont trust the guy !!! When the treatment failed, instead of taking professional responsibility, he told me the outcome was 'between me and my God.' My success at another clinic proves this wasn't about God; it was about a lack of medical skill and proper diagnostics. They seem more focused on iron-clad consent forms to protect themselves than on actual patient success. To be quoted another £13,000+ after such a failure felt like a complete cash grab. Do not be fooled by the demeanor; look elsewhere for a clinic that prioritizes results over profit. I wrote this review more than a year ago....Please note after spending £13k and two years of treatment my husband was referred to an andrologist back in Aug 2023. I took the decision to head home because of the exorbitant costs being quoted here and contrary to what Mr Gudi suggested, there was no need which is shocking. Surprising the andrologist we were referred to were all extremely costly too. Its very disappointing and I wouldn't refer this clinic. Please save your hard earned pennis and don't follow blindly what they say. They don't know everything. They chase you down for every penny, and when you ask them questions there is no valid responses. Pls don’t delete - this place is a rip off. They tend to push negative reviews at the end and even delete at times that’s why they have a 4.9 rating We went there looking at the great reviews, but I am sad to say we were disappointed. Dr Gudi kept giving us false hope, and at the end of the process when it wasnt successful asked us to see the andrologist. Why was the IVF conducted when sperm wasn't upto the mark in the first place ? At the end of the emotional roller coaster were we spent upto £13000+ we were told "I am sorry its between you and your god". It’s not about being emotional but getting justice and value from the thousands + pounds we invested. Our case was taken very lightly.We would have expected honesty from day 1. All procedures are carried in London’s women clinic which costs £6K for two cycles, hence we aren’t sure why we paid double that amount for single cycle. Feels like being taken advantage of.
★☆☆☆☆ Fertility Plus a month ago
I had a bad experience with a negative result from Mr. Shad! I do not recommend
★☆☆☆☆ Fertility Plus 7 years ago
We are sorry to learn of the rating you gave the clinic. We don't recognise your name perhaps you intended to leave this rating for another clinic? We take great pride in our patient experience, especially as fertility treatment is often … More
★★☆☆☆ Fertility Plus 5 years ago
To be clear this review reflects my experience of trying to book, not the service. I have given up trying to book here because it feels like trying to gain access to a private club. Unfriendly service on the phone and over email, made to feel like I should be grateful that I am able to book with them. Had to chase for my initial email and for the invoice and then I can't even access the invoice because I need an account - but no explanation of how to get an account. I don't mind chasing if I feel like there is someone friendly at the end of the phone but there isn't and for me its not how I want to feel when booking such a personal service.
★★☆☆☆ Fertility Plus 2 weeks ago
We chose Fertility Plus because of their excellent reviews and paid £715 for the Couple Fertility Assessment Package after struggling to conceive for several years. While the doctors identified a possible issue, we were then referred to …
★☆☆☆☆ King's Fertility 3 months ago
Overall, I feel deeply disappointed with my experience. Throughout the entire process, I was constantly assigned to different doctors. There was no continuity of care and no single person taking clear responsibility for my case. Each time I had to re-explain my situation, which was emotionally exhausting. Communication has also been slow and inefficient. Responses often take too long, and it feels unclear who is actually overseeing or coordinating the treatment plan. In a process that is already physically and emotionally demanding, this lack of structure adds significant stress. The overall efficiency of the clinic has been low, and the outcomes have not met expectations. It leaves me feeling confused and helpless about how things are managed internally. I understand that medical treatment does not guarantee success. However, patients deserve consistency, accountability, and timely communication — especially in such a sensitive and important journey. At this stage, I feel more tired than supported.
★☆☆☆☆ King's Fertility 5 months ago
My experience with King’s Fertility was very distressing, both clinically and administratively. On the clinical side, I did not feel there was adequate shared decision-making or clear explanation of options and rationale at key points, and parts of my treatment felt rushed. I left appointments unsure what had been decided and why, which is not acceptable in time-sensitive fertility care. I felt pressured to proceed without a full discussion of alternatives and expected outcomes. Medication/protocol decisions were communicated in a way that felt last-minute and not properly explained. My concerns (including about timing/monitoring) weren’t taken seriously until I pushed repeatedly. On the administrative side, communication was inconsistent and at times contradictory. I was told one plan by clinical staff and later told something different by reception/admin, including confusion around what was possible next and what pathway/funding applied. Monitoring and scheduling also felt poorly managed, and I repeatedly had to chase to get the right follow-up. The worst moment was immediately after egg collection, while I was recovering from sedation and in pain, when booking/communication issues added unnecessary stress and felt lacking in compassion. Fertility treatment needs clarity, coordination, and empathy — unfortunately that wasn’t my experience here.
★☆☆☆☆ King's Fertility Edited 5 months ago
After two unsuccessful IVF cycles at King’s Fertility, one failed implantation and one early miscarriage, we decided to seek treatment elsewhere. Looking back, I truly regret the valuable time we lost before moving on. At our new clinic, several issues that had been missed or not properly investigated at King’s were finally identified. Something as straightforward as checking anti-TPO antibodies had been overlooked, and once discovered, it was easily managed with levothyroxine. We were also concerned about my partner’s semen analysis. The sperm count of the sample that was used for egg fertilisation was suddenly 10 times higher than any of the past tests (including one just 2 weeks before). That was very concerning, nobody could explain it. My stimulation cycle at King’s was difficult. I felt borderline overstimulated and, despite retrieving 21 oocytes, we ended up with only 4 embryos. At the time, I assumed this was normal. But at the new clinic, with 13 oocytes, we got 6 embryos. That difference made us realise things could have been handled more carefully. What stood out most was the lack of transparency and explanation from the start. The whole process felt protocol-driven rather than personalised. Important tests and options, like sperm DNA fragmentation and PGT-A, were never mentioned until we researched them ourselves and asked elsewhere. I understand that doctors may doubt whether certain tests are medically necessary, but patients should still have the option to choose and pay for additional investigations if they might improve the chances of success. Being an NHS-funded patient should not limit your ability to access or even learn about private options. Every patient deserves full information to make informed decisions. A lot of people mention the lack of continuity of care at King’s. At the time, it didn’t seem like the biggest issue for us, but at our new clinic we saw the same doctor every time, and we now understand how valuable that continuity is. It shows that it is possible. The bigger issue at King’s was feeling like we had to research everything ourselves just to advocate for the right care. I would have shared all of this directly with King’s, but they do not follow up with their patients. There was no chance to give feedback. This is especially noticeable because most clinics routinely send feedback requests and appointment reminders, which we never received from King’s. Allowing patients to share honest feedback is important if a clinic wants to improve and make sure future patients get the best possible experience. Key points for anyone considering King’s Fertility 1. Be proactive and educate yourself about your options, especially if you are an NHS patient. 2. Advocate for yourself at every stage to make sure your treatment is personalised. 3. Ask early about tests like sperm DNA fragmentation and PGT-A, not only after failures. 4. Continuity of care matters, but transparency and proper investigation matter even more. I hope this review helps other patients make informed decisions and encourages King’s Fertility to improve areas where it could really benefit future patients.
★☆☆☆☆ King's Fertility Edited 8 months ago
Overall experience: negative Positive experience with Dr Kuga and Dr Sadaf: clear communication, evidence-based discussion, and appropriate expectation-setting. In contrast, the consultation with Mr Kuhan Rajah gave a 5–10% IVF success estimate despite uncertainty about whether fibroids were cavity-distorting or actually blocking ovarian access; this felt premature without proper mapping first (e.g., saline sonohysterography, MRI, or diagnostic hysteroscopy). Options and approximate probabilities were not presented (such as “Option A ≈ X %” and “Option B ≈ Y %”) to let us make an informed financial decision; instead, the discussion felt discouraging, as if there was no chance, with limited exploration of alternatives and statistics. We would like to transfer care to another clinic. The records-release process was described as potentially taking up to a month with no guaranteed expedition, which—given time-sensitive decisions—left us feeling trapped at King’s. A close family member who is a retired gynaecologist agrees that prognosis should follow complete diagnostics and encouraged a second opinion. Not recommend. In response to kings There no step by step plan. The body language and conversation was to discourage us to go further. So I’m not sure what you say to create a step by step plan. I understand physicians need to make recommendation but without the picture how can they make recommendations. It’s been two months now since our first appointment. We don’t have any of our result that we requested. What’s the difficultly in giving people their result that they paid for!
★☆☆☆☆ King's Fertility a year ago
I think the response from King's Fertility (Jo Evans) to my Wife's negative review perfectly represents how insensitive, unprofessional and inappropriate this Fertility Clinic is towards their patients. There have been countless failures which are far too long to list within our agonising 6 months period with this Clinic. All of which, King's Fertility have taken no accountability or ownership for and have provided NO apology. Instead, deflecting it back to us and coercively trying to take advantage of our vulnerabilities by stating we are the problem. Ever since we raised a formal complaint addressing their failures and incompetence, on several occasions they've attempted to convince us to leave the Clinic which is no way to treat a Patient who's raised a complaint. They should want to acknowledge their failures and do better! I'd also like to know what the "additional support and appointments are" these don't exist, it's a false claim. Instead they brush over and ignore statements which they know are true with regards to their negligence and poor conduct. They seem to think doing this disproves our claims, but it doesn't. There is absent communication, lack of organisation, constant careless mistakes, staff are mostly rude and condescending/patronising. We've wasted 60 hours of annual leave, petrol costs not mentioning the £2.5k they kept on credit until we requested a refund. They state we weren't charged for cancellation fees (a cancellation due to their mistakes), this was only due to my Wife and I making a formal complaint refusing to pay, evidencing their negligence, otherwise they'd happily of taken our money knowing full well they shouldn't be. Furthermore, the appointment mentioned on 29th October was missed by my Wife and I as we were, again, wrongfully informed over the phone by Admin that our appointment was booked for 30th October. This Clinic provides no appointment letters, E-Mails or texts, which I imagine is due to the many booking mistakes they make on a daily basis, which they don't want the patients to evidence. When we have asked for booking confirmation we are told to "make our own note", I wonder why! I had actually called on 8th November 2024 but again due to the Clinics incompetence no notes were made on the system to reflect this, Admin said this was being looked into as the calls were recorded, I provided the date and the time and I assume the clinic was found to be in the wrong because I’ve heard nothing more on the situation. We've requested our medical records, as you're never verbally informed of scan reports or blood results, if you're lucky you'll get a "all is OK", "it's normal". The Clinic also won't provide summary reports, scans or blood results after each appointment, which is standard process in nearly every NHS Clinic. The fact we paid privately you'd think they'd afford us with a piece of paper. Upon requesting our medical records we were told this will take 28 days as allegedly they have had a high volume of requests! I imagine it's due to patients either moving clinic or the fact patients are wanting to know their results due to not being informed in their appointments. King's Fertility does have a dispensing service, but it's outsourced! Another false fabrication as a poor attempt of the Clinic trying to save their reputation. King's Fertility charge a £50 dispensing fee and won't allow you to take the prescription yourself to source the medication at the best price! You must only obtain all medication through their designated dispenser, the items are extortionately priced. Most Clinics will write the prescription and allow the Patient to rightfully have control of where they wish to obtain it whether it's via the Clinic's recommended supplier or ASDA! I find King's Fertility deeply insult our intelligence and are very disrespectful to even attempt to try point the blame to us when we can heavily evidence our valid cause to complain. 2 months later, our complaint remains not upheld. We will escalate our complaint to the appropriate authority.
★☆☆☆☆ King's Fertility Edited 6 months ago
Appalling experience at Kings. I've left some time before I complete this review, as I went elsewhere for treatment after Kings and had so much better results. Kings are not good for women like me with low amh, put me on highest dose of stims to fry my eggs, made me trigger when I had three 16 mm follicles (they were apparently worried about ovulation) which resulted in no mature eggs and obviously no fertilisation, lost £9k with no results! And there was no empathy afterwards. Went to another London clinic to embryo batch and each cycle I've had few mature eggs and good quality blasts to freeze, with them knowing to trigger me at LEAST 18mm follicles. Traumatised by my experience at Kings and would not recommend them at all! My follow up consultation after this failed expensive round was the most discouraging, giving me a 10% success rate and saying it is likely my egg quality. I can say that moving clinics was the best decision in my life as I have now been blessed with twins, using my own eggs! I would not recommend Kings at all.
★☆☆☆☆ King's Fertility a year ago
I cannot stress enough how appalling my experience with King’s Fertility Clinic, London has been. I am beyond frustrated, and after filing a formal complaint that led to no changes, I am now considering seeking legal advice for potential misconduct. In June 2024, I paid for an initial couple’s consultation with King’s Fertility Clinic, yet to this day, I have not received any actual fertility treatment. Instead, the clinic has endlessly pushed excessive diagnostic tests like bloodwork and scans, and even required me to get additional immunisations (boosters) and take iron supplements—both at extra costs. I've since learned these were unnecessary and had no relation to succeeding in a viable pregnancy. King's Fertility warned that they would refuse treatment if I didn’t comply with these recommendations (blackmailed). King's Fertility Clinic has deliberately caused avoidable delays. For instance, not returning my calls for weeks, set appointments with a 2-3 week wait, then claimed that my original blood tests were no longer valid under their policy requiring renewed blood tests every 4-6 weeks. This led to repeated blood tests at additional costs each time. Currently, King’s Fertility Clinic holds £2,500 of my money, yet none of this has gone towards the 3 cycles of IUI that I purchased. When we finally attempted the first IUI cycle, I informed the consultant that I cannot ovulate without medication and need ovulation induction (e.g. Clomid). I provided detailed medical history from my previous NHS fertility treatments to confirm this. However, the consultant insisted on monitoring me to see if I could ovulate naturally. Throughout this first cycle, I had multiple follicle tracking scans, each showing immature follicles. Despite repeatedly asking/prompting about ovulation induction at each follicle tracking scan, I was dismissed by the nurse. At the last scan, the nurse informed me that my follicles were shrinking, and the IUI cycle would be cancelled. I was then charged a £500 cancellation fee. Solely due to King's Fertility incompetence, but I had no doubt in my mind, they did this deliberately to maximise profit. Following a formal complaint, they switched my treatment plan to IVF. Yet, last month, King’s Fertility refused to begin treatment because I hadn’t had a period. They claimed I needed an “anytime” scan to determine the best time to start bleeding (an absurd statement) and insisted that I obtain progesterone tablets through them at an added cost. Despite having a prescription from my GP for Progesterone tablets to induce a bleed, they wouldn’t proceed with IVF since the medication was not issued by their clinic—another clear money-making tactic. After I had a natural period, I contacted the clinic, only to be told by Admin that they need to have the consultant call me before starting IVF. That was two weeks ago, and I have yet to hear anything. It’s as though they’re frustrated that they couldn’t extract more money from me. I wish I had taken previous reviews about this clinic more seriously. Five months of my life have been wasted, and I strongly urge others to avoid King’s Fertility Clinic. Their approach to exploiting vulnerable women desperate for a child to maximise their profits is utterly disgraceful. I'm absolutely furious and I'll be escalating my complaint.
★☆☆☆☆ King's Fertility Edited a year ago
I regret having to express my dissatisfaction with Kings Fertility. As a private patient, the client-facing and communication aspects of their service are notably very poor. Despite assurances from the assessing Dr that a follow-up appointment would take 1-2 weeks at most - this was forgotten about and never offered; and resulted in having to consistently chase for it, then it was finally offered a month later. Conflicting information from different receptionists regarding MDT meetings was frustrating, and challenging them resulted in them being insensitive, condescending and defensive. The telephone manner/etiquette of some of the receptionist is also very poor. The receptionists /admin staff will forever be the biggest downfall of the clinic - their attitude is very concerning and every interaction with them has been negative. Furthermore, despite requests, no written correspondence or clinical letters from the assessments have been received, causing delays in necessary external referrals. The lack of continuity of care also makes it challenging to build rapport or trust with their doctors. What is also concerning, is the absence of any formal ID checks. This overall experience has been disappointing and has significantly impacted my confidence in their services.
★☆☆☆☆ King's Fertility 3 years ago
Like many of the reviews our experience at the private clinic at Kings has been atrocious. The staff are rude, unhelpful and provide inaccurate information. My money was taken promptly at the start of the process but I have been unable to attain results, to decipher them and I am still awaiting a refund due to being advised to cancel one of the services I booked. The result being that we now have no context of our test results or a refund. I will be making a formal complaint and I sincerely hope that those who have to go through fertility treatment choose a clinic which is human, sympathetic and empathetic. None of which King's fertility is.
★☆☆☆☆ King's Fertility 3 years ago
Atrocious manners of receptionists at first floor. My best friend was treated with urgent care after a routine appointment and was kept overnight. I attended this clinic 2 years ago when I had my son and even through the pandemic the service was exceptionally good, these 2 lacked of compassion and professionalism and common sense. What a shame.

★☆☆☆☆ King's Fertility Edited 4 years ago
Unsatisfied treatment plan, no investigation or explanation as to why treatment failed. No after care and a sudden stop with interaction. Unprofessional.
★☆☆☆☆ King's Fertility 17 hours ago
I was recommended to this clinic from a friend but I have been disappointed from the beginning. I initially contact them for fertility testing with a view to egg freezing and was excited that they could fit me in, were less expensive than other clinics, and were local to me. Unfortunately after I booked the appointment I received no confirmation email and was concerned the booking hadn't been made. I waited until after a long weekend to call them and when I spoke to someone she told me I'd been listed as an NHS patient instead of a private one and as such hadn't received the confirmation email. I asked if I could still have the appointment and she just kept saying "you have to pay" over and over again, as if I had deliberately tried not to pay. I explained that no one had ever billed me and I'd received no invoice. She confirmed I could keep the appointment and I then asked if she could move my follow up consultation earlier as they were making me wait for a week. She became strange at this point and said she had to check things with a manager and that someone would call me back. 6 hours later I received a phone call from them offering me the same consulation appointment. The woman also threatened that they would cancel the appointment if I didn't pay, again insinuating that I had deliberately tried not to pay. I told her I had called four times trying to confirm the appointment and at no point had I ever refused to pay. I then asked her to send me a confirmation email and paid almost £400 for the tests. She didn't send the confirmation email until the next day. The scans themselves were fine and I had perfectly pleasant experience with the doctors and nurses there, but every time I tried to liaise with the admin it was awful. A week later I was supposed to have my follow up consultation and organised my work schedule around it. The doctor was then 15 minutes late calling me with no communication from anyone. I was just waiting by the phone at work. When the doctor finally called he didn't apologise for being late at all and gave me one of the most bland, yet functional consultations ever. Of course I have been in search of a clinic to carry out the egg freezing process with and have had several consulations. I would have honestly loved to use Kings but it was impossible to trust them with the logistics of organising my procedure when my initial experience was so ridiculous. I'm sure the medical side is probably fine but I don't want to spend thousands to be mucked around by a clinic like this when there are so many others out there that treat you with a lot more respect for your time. Honestly really disappointed and didn't think this would be the case after my friend recommended them.
★☆☆☆☆ King's Fertility 3 years ago

I feel completely let down by this clinic. Nurses were always great and work hard, but the admin and treatment post failed transfer has been so sub optimal. ★★☆☆☆ King's Fertility a year ago

★★★☆☆ King's Fertility 6 months ago
My experience with King’s Fertility was a mixed bag, and a few things stood out: - illogical administrative processes, like the team starting a new email thread instead of replying directly to a query I’d sent. Which made it difficult to keep track of everything and find specific information. - they do not provide consultation letters after each appointment. Which means you have no formal copy of what was discussed or the plan, unless you make your own notes. - my consultant was friendly but clearly didn’t have much faith or creativity in managing my case if I didn’t jump straight into IVF. I have low amh but everything is still functioning well. - I met a new clinician at every monitoring appointment and had to keep updating them. Some didn’t speak to me at all and others were fantastic. - nurses were very warm and supportive. - I requested my medical notes after our failed cycle and said I needed them urgently to take to an NHS appointment. It took longer than the 28 days they suggested to me and I ended up having to chase.

I’m sorry to give 3 to the clinic but I also want to give a word of advice to other patients

We are currently going through fertility treatment with Kings. Firstly I would highlight there isn’t anything wrong with the clinical side of things. However, the customer facing / communication side is very poor. There is one particular …
★★★☆☆ King's Fertility 2 years ago
Some (but not all) of the admin staff on the phone can be condescending, frustrating, misinformed and insensitive - this made a difficult and emotional process more stressful. The clinical staff were outstanding and very supportive though. You don’t get to see the same doctor each time, but all were great.
★☆☆☆☆ London Fertility Clinic 3 months ago
I do not recommend London Fertility Clinic based on my experience with their organisation, punctuality, and communication. I booked a private consultation costing £250 and experienced serious issues on two separate occasions. The first appointment was delayed by around 30 minutes. I waited infront of my laptop but did not receive the Zoom link or any update. I had to send multiple follow-up emails and also tried calling, but received no answer. Only after I chased several times did they eventually contact me to apologise. This was a clear waste of my time and felt highly unprofessional for a paid private consultation. There was no real remediation for the inconvenience caused. The second appointment was even worse. The consultation was delayed again — this time by approximately 45 minutes. I received no apology, no acknowledgement, and no communication at the time of the appointment despite following up twice by email to ask when it would start. In the end, no consultation was attended at all. I requested a full refund, and only then did I receive a response. It is unacceptable for a private clinic charging £250 per consultation to manage appointments in this way and to show such disregard for patients’ time. Appointment scheduling and time management are the responsibility of the clinic, and patients should not be left waiting without updates or clear communication. I also found it very unclear why the clinic insisted on online consultations when I repeatedly expressed a preference for a face-to-face appointment. The communication around this felt vague, and even basic details such as the clinic setup and process were not clearly explained, which added to the overall sense of disorganisation. Overall, I found the experience disorganised, unprofessional, and stressful. I would not recommend this clinic based on how my appointments were handled.
★☆☆☆☆ London Fertility Clinic a year ago
Called to enquire about insurance coverage and came across the rudest receptionist. Very bad customer service.
★☆☆☆☆ IVF Hammersmith Edited 5 months ago
They don't care about you as a patient, about your health or feelings, only about making money and acting like crooks instead of doctors. I was supposed to have a procedure done with them and luckily I had a video call with Dr Kinjal Shah Rajiv and whilst discussing some blood tests results which came back out of range, she replied "nothing to worry about" and kept on pushing me going forward and spending money with them and booking more consultations, etc. I then spoke to a competent Dr who said that she was talking nonsense and what came as out of range is very important to be treated because it's essential for my immunity. From my own experience, I warn you to stay away from these shameless individuals who only care about milking as much money as they can out of you.
★☆☆☆☆ IVF Hammersmith a year ago
Worst treatment I have ever received. After our IVF process we were left waiting 3 months for answers, despite me emailing and calling every week. Extremely unprofessional service, still no results, documents or correspondence to pass on to new clinic 9 months later and no updates on NHS app. Even PALS took over 3 2 months to respond. Diabolical service.
★☆☆☆☆ IVF Hammersmith a year ago
very bad system and you waste your time with them.
★☆☆☆☆ IVF Hammersmith Edited 2 months ago
I had my first ever IVF treatment with this clinic privately in 2024 and it completely put me off doing anymore. We were rushed through appointments with inadequate information. The whole process was overwhelming and stressful with no …
★★☆☆☆ IVF Hammersmith 10 months ago
While the medical care at Wolfson Fertility Centre may be adequate, the communication leaves much to be desired. Every time we interacted with the team, we had to repeat our story from scratch, as if there was no continuity or awareness of …
★☆☆☆☆ Aria Fertility 3 months ago
Some excellent staff, but systemic failures in care and ethics: We had a polarised experience here. While some of the team (Maartje in nursing, Ruth in admin) and specific doctors like Dr. Amanda and Dr. Anna are fantastic, the clinic’s management and diagnostic approach were deeply disappointing and frankly frightening. The positives: If you are assigned the team mentioned above, you are in good hands. The concerns (shortlist out of many): Lack of holistic investigation: Despite being 35+ and raising concerns on initial test results, our requests for further testing were dismissed. Its all put on the women when men are 50% of the equation. We felt pushed into expensive, repetitive cycles without investigating the unexplored causes. Don't rush, push to do thorough investigative work (not just surface level tests especially for both partners) before you start any rounds. Financial priority over care: After an unexpected failed round where we had clear fertilisation issues, the doctor refused to answer clarifying questions via email, insisting on a £250 appointment fee. At such a vulnerable time, this felt exploitative. Our experience at other clinics is entirely different where Doctors' answer our questions and work with us not just talk at us. Administrative negligence: Most distressingly, our embryos were moved even after we had explicitly withdrawn consent. We spent upwards of £30k and felt like a transaction rather than patients. We have since moved to a clinic that prioritises investigation over rushing rounds. Please do your research before committing and avoid at all costs if you are above 35 and don't have either of those doctors.
★☆☆☆☆ Aria Fertility 6 months ago
Absolutely terrible experience. In our view, the team seemed unprofessional and disorganized, and we never felt genuinely cared for. Because they gave my partner sparkling water beforehand, something we were later told interfered with sedation, my partner ultimately had to undergo the procedure without sedation, which was extremely painful! Due to a miscommunication with my partner's prescription, giving unclear instructions on the dosage and then attempting to correct it through an improvised email. While they were very quick to request payment, they did not show the same urgency in properly preparing us for the extraction. Whenever we tried to voice concerns, we felt dismissed and treated as though we were the problem. Never again with these people!
★☆☆☆☆ Aria Fertility 10 months ago
I had my first consultation at the clinic and was set on freezing my eggs there. Dr Louisy was lovely and very reassuring. I was very keen to quickly progress my egg freezing journey with her. I had a scan at the first consultation. This was on Wednesday 23 July 2025. I was told I would receive my egg freezing plan by the finance team. I chased for the egg freezing plan and received no response. Today is 28 July 2025. I received an invoice for £500 for my consultation and scan. I have not, to date, received my egg freezing plan. In response to the email containing the invoice, I asked when I would receive my egg freezing plan (after having already requested it the week prior). In response, I was asked “We [sic.] should we expect payment for the invoice”. I had, in fact, already paid the invoice. All of the above demonstrates to me that the clinic is more intent on receiving payment than progressing my care. I selected Aria because of the personalised experience it claimed to offer. It has not lived up to that service and I will be taking my custom elsewhere.
★☆☆☆☆ Aria Fertility 3 years ago
rubbish service from the start. I suspect those good reviews were created by themselves. I made an appointment for a consultation, but they didn't make it clear whether it was online or offline. They even didn’t send me invoice after saying it’s confirmed. I asked them if it’s really confirmed they used more than one week to respond that the booking was successful. They didn't send me an invoice until the last moment before the consultation started and then repeatedly blamed me for assuming that the consultation was online. I have never encountered such rubbish service
★☆☆☆☆ Aria Fertility 3 years ago

★★☆☆☆ Aria Fertility a year ago
My wife and I had five IVF cycles with one of the directors at Aria and ultimately felt disappointed. Although we did end up having success, it wasn’t thanks to Aria — we sought a second opinion from another clinic for our final round and only returned to Aria for the transfer because we had already paid and our embryo was stored there. We wouldn’t recommend Aria overall. While the clinic itself is lovely and the staff are kind, lab is great too, the directors are not present, have too many clients, and aren’t able to provide a personalised experience. Like many of the larger private clinics in London, they just can't offer the one-on-one care you’d expect for the price you pay.

★☆☆☆☆ Ten Harley Street Edited 6 years ago
One particular staff member makes me feel quite uncomfortable, she's of oriental decent with dyed /whitish greyish hair, don't like the way she talks to other staff members, don't like her attitude and the way she thinks she owns the place, she works behind the desk btw, very uncouth individual. Another thing, if your feeling insecure about certain facial things then these kind of clinics are probably not the best places to go, they make you feel as if your face is deformed by telling you that you need fillers in your chin, BOTOX in your nose etc, and when they take pictures of you they make sure it looks as horrific as EVER just so that you can buy more treatments, you go there for one thing then leave wanting to top yourself because you've been made to believe your looks are grotesque, their also very very overpriced, I didn't look any different to when I went in.
★☆☆☆☆ Ten Harley Street 4 years ago
I was unable to make an appointment with Dr Orel due to the arrogance and bigotry of Phyllis Ross, who was supposed to help me book an appointment and instead proved to be unprofessional and extremely unpleasant. I have sent a written …
★☆☆☆☆ Ten Harley Street 6 years ago
Consultant Clinic - Based at 8-10 Harley Street. Have to agree with all the other reviewers of Consultant Clinic. I've been to CC twice now, once for 2ml of jaw filler which was extortionately overpriced at £800. I then went back to get lip filler from Dr Melissa. I have full lips already so was looking for definition. There is NO WAY that they have achieved the results in the photos on their instagram. The lip filler cost over £400 and was unnoticeable. Now 6 months on i have a lump on one side that i'm booked in elsewhere to have dissolved. They've done a great job of marketing themselves but the instagram account is managed badly and gives a really unprofessional image. I would recommend steering clear of this company, there are plenty of good quality, professional doctors out there who will give you realistic expectations of what can or cannot be achieved
★☆☆☆☆ Ten Harley Street 7 years ago
The wait is ridiculous. I have been there a few times with different doctor's appointments throughout the years. Minimum waiting time is one or even two hours. Its completely waste of time and I would be avoiding clinics in this place in the future.

★★★☆☆ Ten Harley Street Edited a month ago
Very good practice . Seeing Dr Hu Hannal told me to change GP practices in Shropshire as not getting me meds and not helping me. But also the new GP practices didn’t want to help either, told me go back to London . Now enjoying ,living in London then isolated Shropshire I was too depressed. So never use a GP for mental health , only make y unwell and pushed me into 🏥🏥. So I’ve come back to this practice with more help then ever . Now got Phycist , health worker.
★★★☆☆ Bader Medical Institute of London a year ago
Had a good experience with Dr Bader and his team they were all very nice and looked after me very well before and after the surgery. Only reason I didn’t give 5 stars is because I gave my bracelet to one of the members of staff to keep in the safe as no jewellery is allowed on while the surgery is being performed. After my surgery I was sent home without my bracelet and when I emailed the office asking for my item back they were quite unhelpful saying there was no bracelet and there was nothing they could do about it. This was disappointing as it was an expensive piece of jewellery and I had spent a lot of money on the surgery. Apart from that I’m happy with the results and think Dr Bader did a great job.
★★★☆☆ Bader Medical Institute of London a year ago
I bought 3 laser sessions, and during the last session the doctor asked me to come back and he would do the 3. I told him it was the last one and he said I could schedule more, in front of the lady who supervises the appointments, that is, with a witness. I went on a trip, and when I got back I contacted the clinic to explain and they told me I was lying. It was absurd. This was a mistake that should not happen.
★☆☆☆☆ The Medical Chambers Kensington 3 months ago
I went to see a doctor at the Medical chambers. The doctor was great in person and diagnosed the issue very quickly. However, the treatment did not work and trying to get replies from the doctor over email was very very difficult with slow response times (about a week). It was not ideal when you have a patient concerned and needed to know how to proceed. All in all the appointment was very expensive for such poor post treatment care!
★☆☆☆☆ The Medical Chambers Kensington 10 months ago
I do not recommend Dr. Bataille or The Medical Chambers Kensington. The skin check was shockingly superficial, and the interaction dismissive. I found a serious lack of ethical care, especially around informed consent and communication. Procedures were done without clear explanation or discussion of options, and I was not involved in decisions about my treatment. The billing was confusing and non-transparent, with unexpected double charges (one for the doctor, one for the clinic - both, overpriced). When I raised concerns, the response was slow, dismissive and disrespectful towards the patient, including repeatedly mischaracterising reasons for concern. This isn’t about my diagnosis or outcome—it’s about respecting patients’ rights to clear, honest information and involvement in their care. Unacceptable.
★☆☆☆☆ The Medical Chambers Kensington a year ago
Very disappointed in the service I received at Dr Kuchai's clinic. Dr Kuchai herself was great, but the admin/finance department were all over the place. First I was invoiced hundreds of pounds extra for tests I never had, then I was also invoiced £90 for a phone call that never even happened. Finally, when I asked to renew my prescription, I was prescribed the wrong medication. I notified them of this about a week ago and there has been no update. Effectively - every step post-appointment was a blunder.
★☆☆☆☆ The Medical Chambers Kensington Edited a year ago
December 2024. Be very careful: after being a client for 10 years, I learnt last week that some invoices, dated from NINE months ago, hadn't been covered by my insurance company (not sure why, but maybe). Medical Chambers just decided to charge my card, without giving me even a month delay (when I waited 9 months to know that there was an issue). This is just a business, definitely not "client centric" as they claim to be. UPDATE Feb 2025: they now claim that the mistake is mine because they found an email dated 2023 (!!) in which I made a mistake regarding my insurance company. But this email was for one specific appointment, for which they managed to process my insurance details without any issues :-) And I double check my insurance details every single time I walk in, with their reception staff ... anyway, they obviously won't take responsibility for anything: you, as clients, will always be in the wrong.
★☆☆☆☆ The Medical Chambers Kensington 2 years ago
I have visited this place in order to see a qualified GP. I was more than happy to go private as I hoped I would save time and get better more personalised care. In reality this experience was quite awful. As their GP diagnosed that I don't have an ear infection (the problem that made me want to see a GP in the first place), but instead I may have a very serious neurological disorder and sent me to an emergency room that same evening. After waiting for hours in an emergency section of the hospital and experiencing a lot of anxiety about the possibility that she presented me with I saw the ENT specialist. As soon as the specialist looked into my ear she confirmed that I have an ear infection and should be treated with antibiotics. Why did I go private? Spend money? Get misdiagnosed, scared and still end up in a public hospital where I got much better care? My advice.. AVOID THIS PLACE
★☆☆☆☆ The Medical Chambers Kensington 3 years ago
Posting on behalf of my daughter but this surely has one of the most abysmal customer experiences for an expensive London medical chambers I’ve ever experienced in my time looking after her. Poor admin meant we had issues with our insurance …
★☆☆☆☆ The Medical Chambers Kensington Edited a year ago
I would give zero stars to Flammarion. My child diagnosed with allergy and at her report she says "we will consider an epipen in the future" she was that vague, didn't raise a concern and let us believe that we can battle an accident just with citirizine, which was totally unprofessional the least and it could lead to serious issues, if you consider that from NHS gp was told to carry 2epipens at all times. It took several emails from us requesting to send her report, she eventually sent it around 2 months later and she didn’t send it to our GP who could as a last resourse spot the omission and refer us for further investigations. Subsequently, we visited Cromwell, were prescribed on the spot with 2 epipens, and referred for blood tests. Our GP provided 2 more epipens to keep at my child's activities. Our Cromwell Dr. suggested OIT, which the sooner started, the greater the chances are for a successful outcome. She made us lose precious time. She didn't make us aware of the importance of having a fatal allergy.
★☆☆☆☆ The Medical Chambers Kensington a year ago
Very bad experience with medical chambers. My husband was in a very critical condition because of an allergy and asked for an emergency appointment. They never returned his call and Dr GARDETTE never responded. It’s not a good medical centre and MEDICARE is much better. Just don’t go there
★☆☆☆☆ The Medical Chambers Kensington 5 years ago
The short version: brilliant, kind and thorough consultant (Dr George); terrible reception/admin service. …
★☆☆☆☆ The Medical Chambers Kensington 5 years ago
If you are not even honest about the small things, how can you expect others to believe you will be honest about the big things? Let me tell you about my experience: They have a policy where they charge you £2 for a mask, which at the beginning of the crisis at least they did *not* notify patients (or, at least, me) about when booking the appointment over the phone (maybe it was in their fine print) I said at reception that I did not want to pay the fee. They offered to give me a mask for free, so I went ahead with the appointment. However, they went ahead and charged my card. I sent them an email which did not get replied to for three months. I received a reply saying that their sign outside the clinic clearly states that masks cost £2. I wrote back to the person re-explaining that I did not ask to purchase a mask but was offered one for free. However, she did not reply for a month. I called today and a lady with an uncooperative attitude picked up the phone, first saying to me her manager was on another line, then saying she was not willing to speak to me and asking me to send another email. When I stated that I had sent an email already, they said to me they were not willing to speak to me any further and said they were sorry.

★☆☆☆☆ The Medical Chambers Kensington 6 years ago

★★☆☆☆ The Medical Chambers Kensington 8 months ago
I explicitly told the staff not to use a specific credit card for my payment, and they acknowledged this instruction twice. Despite that, they used the card anyway. This mistake isn’t just inconvenient—it could have caused serious issues, such as overdrafts or problems with company accounts. Acknowledging instructions but ignoring them feels negligent and unprofessional. It really was quite a headache for me and something that was easily avoidable. I hope the practice takes better care in handling billing in the future, as attention to detail in these matters is critical.

★★☆☆☆ Kensington International Clinic - La Maison Medicale 4 years ago
I have been seeing 2 good doctors at La maison medicale for 4 years. They were doing a good job until they decided to make many changes which are not in patients' best interest: mainly online appointments for the rhumatology department, no more neurologist.. Reduced slots which makes it impossible to have an appointment. Easy to get a blood test but having an appointment to discuss the result is hard work: Why should I even have a blood test in this case? There are two secretaries: the man is very polite but the woman rushes people to take appointments, speaks to people in a rude manner. 5 discusions in one week with this person was just a disaster. La maison médicale moved from a place where you would receive great level of care to fast food version of a health centre.

Had a scan here because I didn’t want to wait for my NHS one. They completely missed the diagnosis, it all felt rushed, and they reported that there’s nothing there. My NHS scan happened 3 days later, and I had the same scan, with a doctor, and they found the diagnosis! As a doctor myself, the NHS scan was much more thorough, and they had a much higher quality scanner, so I regret wasting my time and money at this private place. I’d avoid at all cost, and not come back- even for a free scan- beware.

I recently visited the scan clinic for a Two Breast Ultrasound Scan on 28th March and was promised to receive the report within 24 hours or right after the scan, as stated on their website. Despite multiple assurances from the clinic staff that the report would be sent to me by the end of the day, I am still waiting after 72 hours. I have reached out to them both yesterday and today, and each time I was told that the report would be sent promptly, yet it has not been received. As someone who has undergone scans before and received reports promptly, this experience has been disappointing and frustrating. It is not my usual practice to leave negative reviews, but I did not expect such a delay from a reputable clinic.

Thank you for your feedback. We’re sorry to see that your experience did not fully meet your expectations. We take all feedback seriously, as it helps us review and improve the service we provide. If you would be willing to share more about your visit, we would really appreciate the opportunity to understand what happened and see how we can put things right. Please feel free to contact us at info@londonsono.com A member of our team will respond as soon as possible. Your experience matters to us, and we hope to have the opportunity to restore your confidence in our service.
★★☆☆☆ MEDii Health 3 months ago
I visited MEDii Health in London and was disappointed with the level of professionalism during my appointment. Although the clinic is registered with the CQC, the doctor I saw did not display a clear name badge or provide his GMC registration number, which made it difficult to verify his professional credentials. In the UK it is standard practice for doctors to identify themselves clearly. ￼ Additionally, I was not informed in advance that paying with a Chinese bank card would incur an extra handling fee. This fee was only revealed at the point of payment, and no detailed receipt or itemised invoice was provided. Clear and fair communication about fees is essential in private healthcare. Overall, I expected a more transparent and professional experience from a private clinic.

★☆☆☆☆ Dottore London 7 years ago
Horrible I had booked a mole mapping and acne consultation. I went in, and the doctor seemed a little nervous. He asked me which moles he wanted to see. I started pointing out a few, but he only looked at them very briefly with the device, barely getting close. The whole thing lasted two minutes, exaggerating. A small angioma on my head told me not to comb my hair. Then he handed me some flyers with sunscreen, telling me I had to wear it as if it were 30 degrees Celsius in London and the sea! Having also booked an acne consultation, I asked him about my skin, and he told me everything was fine and that maybe later on I could just get rid of the marks with laser. I should point out that my skin isn't damaged or eaten away by acne! Of course, he pulled out another flyer, telling me to choose one of the creams listed there! I already knew that Roche-Posay was good for my face, since I often go to the dermatologist in Italy, so I know how a skin mapping and checkup are performed... but I think combining an anti-wrinkle cream with an acne cream is different! The icing on the cake was that the girl wanted to charge me for the mole mapping and for the dermatological consultation that didn't happen and ended up with a flyer! I really don't recommend this dermatologist, I don't know about other doctors.

I have been to this Clinic previously to see a Dermatologist and it went really well even though I have noticed the clinic doesn't look well kept (my GP practice is much cleaner) I went a second time to see another kind of doctor and I was very disappointed, she saw me and did an ultrasound scan but she did not talk about the outcomes at all, and when I asked questions she looked very annoyed. Moreover she wrote the details for my GP to see (details that were not discussed with me at all, so I left even more confused tham before) and then they forgot to send it to my GP, apparently they did not have their contacts but still the logical thing to do after charging me £400 would be to contact me to get the contact to make sure everything was sent. I have chased them to ask if the outcomes sent to my GP were complete and I had no answer. Once they take the payment they don't care anymore. To be honest, if you cannot go to get checked in Italy, you would be better off with the NHS. I would also like to add that the consultation room of this particular doctor looked very unhygenic and not up to standard for a clinic (public or private). I would not reccomend even if some of the doctors might be good.

I had a very bad experience in this clinic. Everyone was nice and friendly but I question qualification of the doctors that work there. Ours was Barbara Marinho. I came with my daughter who was having fever and sore throat. Doctor only looked at her throat - she didnt check ears, nose, lungs, didnt measure her temperature, nothing. Never had this experience before. Cherry on the top, she prescribed antibiotics with a dosage that doesnt exist. How can a doctor to be unfamiliar what are the existing dosages of a common antibiotics as penicillin? Had to get another prescription. And it all was for 125GBP.
★☆☆☆☆ Messina Clinic 6 years ago
Unfortunately I don’t have a good experience of this clinic. Paid £90 for a first consultation and Dr Caroline didn’t even take my blood pressure. Didn’t ask me about my life, my weight, diet nothing at all. I was the one that took off my coat and showed her where the pain was coming from. Also made me feel terrible about taking medication, that was actually prescribed by an excellent doctor, that helped me cope with pain even after I told her that I only take one a day which is an amazing extremely low dosage. She seemed more concerned about herself. Not all people that take medication abuse it Dr Caroline! In the end she didn’t even prescribe an alternative to the said medication only a stronger version of Ibufren that actually gives me serious side effects. If only she took the time to ask. Waste of money and time. I wouldn’t bother.
★☆☆☆☆ Messina Clinic 5 years ago
Unfortunately, I don’t have the best experience of the clinic. The reception is the only good experience. The first consultation with Dr Flavio Messina was ok. He prescribed me medication that I could have found on the pharmacy shelves. That’s not a problem if it works, but it didn’t. There was no recommendation for a further investigation if the problem persisted. A blood test was suggested. The nurse needed three attempts. It was not comfortable. When I came back to know the result of my blood test I had to waiting more than 30 min. The receptionist had to remind Dr Flavio Messina twice that I was waiting for him and he still made jokes with another patient. He was not prepared for my consultation either. This is not what you expect when you are a paying customer and have paid everything in advance. Friendly is good, but over friendly starts to feel a bit uncomfortable.

★☆☆☆☆ Harley Street Dr 6 months ago
I strongly advise anyone considering treatment at LMA Clinic to think very carefully before committing. My experience was disappointing and extremely costly. I had Sculptra and laser treatments for acne scarring and saw no noticeable improvement. Whenever I raised concerns, I felt dismissed by staff, who kept insisting that results were visible even though nothing had changed. Instead of listening, they would bring additional staff members over to tell me everything looked fine, which felt rehearsed and unhelpful. Although the doctor is friendly and charismatic, it came across as more showmanship than genuine patient care. Pricing was inconsistent, with each session costing something different. When I wasn’t seeing results, I was encouraged to book even more treatments, despite already spending thousands of pounds. The PA and receptionist were cold in their approach, and at no stage did I feel supported. When I expressed my concerns, they were shut down rather than addressed. After my experience, and seeing that others have had similar issues, I would urge potential clients to be cautious. I regret my decision and wish I had gone elsewhere.
★☆☆☆☆ Harley Street Dr 5 months ago

Very disappointed with this clinic. We showed up for our appointment, only to be told it was canceled after we had already arrived — with no prior notice at all. The staff were rude and unprofessional, and the doctor didn’t even meet us or say hello. My mother tried to explain that her English isn’t very good, but instead of being understanding, they kept saying “yes you do” and then hung up the phone on me. To make things worse, they didn’t even reimburse us for the transportation costs after wasting our time and money. This was a completely disrespectful and unpleasant experience. I do not recommend this clinic at all!!!!!!! Shame on them and on the way they treated us!

★☆☆☆☆ Harley Street Dr 10 years ago
When my mother suggested she wanted cosmetic surgery including derma filler to regain her youthful appearance, I was concerned. Having worked in the pharmaceutical industry I was aware of problems relating to the use of derma fillers on the complexion of someone in their advancing years. She had searched online and viewed a YouTube video of Dr Ayham Al-Ayoubi at London Medical and Aesthetic Clinic who we understood had worked with some high profile faces so must be reputable. Upon consultation he said it would be no problem and although we weren't expecting it to be a cut and dry process, we also weren't expecting my mother to have such a disastrous reaction to the derma filler Sculptra. Large swellings on her cheeks - expected to distend - remained prominent after several weeks and began affecting her state of mind. When we questioned why this product was used on someone with age-related fat loss and relatively thin skin, we were told that it wouldn't be a long term problem as it would reduce over time and that further treatments were recommended to encounter the irregularity. These turned out to be just as damaging and the results were worse. This led us to believe that either Dr Al-Ayoubi was overstating his skill as a plastic technician - as this product was clearly unsuitable - or we were being taken for a ride financially. Either way the results were looking irreversible. Questioning his integrity proved futile as he wasn't about to admit liability, so here we are three years down the road and no solution or redress.

★☆☆☆☆ Outpatient 84 Harley Street a year ago
To Whom It May Concern, I am writing to file a formal complaint regarding the misconduct of a nurse named Asnaria at Harley Clinic. On November 15, 2024, Nurse Asnaria, who is of Filipino nationality, displayed inappropriate behavior towards us. She raised her voice and shouted at us, which was both unprofessional and distressing. Additionally, she refused to transfer my mother from the imaging bed to her private bed, citing the presence of family members in the room. It is important to highlight that my mother was under palliative care and in a fragile emotional and physical state. As a family, we were also in a deeply distressed state due to her condition. Nurse Asnaria showed no compassion for my mother’s fear of being alone, especially in a foreign country, nor did she consider her need for family support during such a critical time. We have reported this incident to our embassy, who emphasized that any mistreatment of their citizens is a direct offense to them. We urge the clinic to ensure that patients and their families are treated with respect, empathy, and dignity.

★☆☆☆☆ Outpatient 84 Harley Street Edited 7 years ago
I went to this clinic very excited and confident thus I wanted to spend as much as needed in order to treat my mild acne scars! When I have arrived at the clinic I only had very few spots on my forehead and clear skin on the reast of the face and chest. The doctor prescribed me products such as antibiotics, ptd and glycolic peel. The nurses are very inefficient!!! Apart from one girl which impressed me. I can't remember her name exactly, but she was new and looks Chinese and is around 35 years old. She was very friendly, efficient and she did an amazing job in terms of pad and glycolic peel. Regarding the treatment overall. The Ptd was good, glycolic okay and slightly helped my skin, the readness almost disappeared. However, when I've stopped the antibiotics they have prescribed, my skin was all covered in spots, including my chest, I looked so read I wanted to cry. Never had that acne on my face before! Horrible!!! Therefore, this suggests that the antibiotics quickly worked and cleared the acne not their treatment!!! As such, they stopped it just exactly a week before the antibiotic treatment ended so they can try to convince me to move on the next more expensive "lie" stage. Easy to figure out! Therefore, that was my last treatment at the clinic! When I have talked to them about this they have ignored the problem (shocking) instead of helping me because I've paid so much money, they surprisingly only tried to convince me to buy another treatment. So disappointing! They DON'T CARE about the costumer at all but the money!! To conclude, I'm now clear thanks to my GP, who I thanks very much for putting the effort and 0 money spent! DO NOT WAIST YOUR TIME AND MONEY AT HARLEY STREET CLINIC. THEY ARE NOT HONEST AND DO NOT CARE ABOUT YOU BUT MONEY!

★★★☆☆ Outpatient 84 Harley Street 2 years ago
Nursing staff, excellent, caring, attentive and kind. Consultant also excellent. Catering staff and housekeeping (cleaning) unfortunately the opposite to above although manager did visit me to discuss at end of my stay. Biggest complaint was changing my room without any prior explanation or apology. Cleaner came in at inappropriate times and expected me to move even though I was not well. Catering staff did not communicate well and quite a few items on the menu were not available. They did not offer to help serve the food, just "dumped and ran". I was not told at the outside that tea and coffee was included but the nurses often asked if I wanted but each time I was worried I would be charged. Poor communication at the outset as the printed material explains that charges will be made. For the cost involved of the non clinical side (accommodation and "hotel" services aspect), sadly this was a poor experience for me. Surgery successful, nurses and clinical staff excellent. Let down by the non clinical side.
