★☆☆☆☆ LIPS Healthcare 3 months ago
TOTAL SCAMMERS! On 16 February, I booked an appointment with dermatologist Dr. Geoffrey Brent for Friday the 20th. However, immediately after completing the booking, I received a phone call from the clinic informing me that the doctor was not available on the 20th and that the earliest available appointment was actually on the 23rd. It appears that the doctor’s schedule was not up to date in the clinic’s booking system. When I made the reservation, several time slots were shown as available for the 20th. However, during the phone call, I was informed that there was no availability at all on that day. As a result, the clinic cancelled my appointment. And shortly after the call , I received an email confirming the cancellation. So basically ,booking appointment, receiving call from the clinic to cancel, then receiving the cancellation email ; they all happened in less than an hour. The following day, I noticed that I had nevertheless been charged for the cancelled appointment. Since then, I have been trying to get a refund, but for some reason the clinic’s accounts/credit department has not processed it. They are withholding people’s money without any valid reason!!! It’s been a week I am still trying to get my £275!!! AVOID FROM THIS CLINIC! I will be warning everyone to avoid this clinic at all costs! If my refund is not processed within 48 hours (the legal deadline), I will begin formal action to recover my money. As a first step, I will file an official complaint through ADR. I will also report the matter to Trading Standards. If necessary, I am also fully prepared to pursue the claim through the SCC as well.
★☆☆☆☆ LIPS Healthcare 5 months ago
I had a very disappointing experience with LIPS Healthcare due to poor communication and lack of clarity throughout the process. When I first called, I was told I could receive the service and have a doctor consultation afterwards without needing to book an appointment. To be safe, I called again on the day to double check, but the staff sounded unsure and quickly booked me an appointment instead, which already caused confusion. When I arrived at the clinic, there were very few instructions and no clear explanation of what to expect. I felt confused and anxious the entire time. I was then informed that there was no doctor available on site, which completely contradicted what I had been told previously. Although I tried to remain calm and trust their professionalism, the information provided was unclear and inconsistent. When I later emailed to ask for clarification, I was told that this explanation was not included and that I would need to pay for an additional service just to have my results explained. Overall, the experience felt unprofessional, poorly managed, and extremely stressful. I would not recommend this clinic to anyone who values clear communication and reliable healthcare guidance.
★☆☆☆☆ LIPS Healthcare 4 months ago
Avoid. Misleading booking and poor patient care. I booked this appointment solely to have a small bump removed from my eyelid. I was clearly told over the phone that this would be done on the day, and I reconfirmed it on the call before attending. When I arrived, I received nothing more than a five-minute eye test, completely irrelevant to why I was there, and was sent away without treatment. When I questioned this, the Dr Maan Kasmiya simply said no and ended the appointment. I was then invoiced £165 for an appointment that provided no care, no solution, and no value. Despite sending emails explaining I had been misled at the point of booking, I received no response whatsoever, only repeated payment chasers until I was effectively bullied into paying. This was a complete waste of time and money, and the lack of communication afterward was unacceptable. Extremely disappointing experience.
★☆☆☆☆ LIPS Healthcare 6 months ago
I booked a blood test for my mother a month ago and pre-paid a £147. I then cancelled the test due to some personal reasons. I was told there was a cancellation fees of £30, but the remaining amount of £117 would be refunded. It's been a month and have not refunded me anything yet. They even took my card details two weeks ago and sent me a slip confirming that the refund was processed. I even called my bank but they said nothing came through. It does not take more than 2 weeks for refunds to get processed in the UK. They are a bunch of unprofessional scammers. I am still fighting for my refund. Please be careful. Do not pay anything beforehand.
★☆☆☆☆ LIPS Healthcare Edited 4 months ago
Recently I had a negative experience with neurologist Dr. Gratwicke. I was prescribed an expensive (£600), strong migraine medication despite having long-standing neurological hypersensitivity (my nervous system can’t tolerate even caffeine contents of a single cup of tea) and symptoms that did not clearly fit a migraine pattern. I specifically asked about the certainty of the diagnosis and was assured it was correct. After taking the medication, I experienced severe adverse effects — chest heaviness, breathing difficulties, throat constriction, and acute neurological tension — requiring an A&E visit and leaving me unable to work for a week, with lingering effects for weeks afterward. It then took 25 days to receive a response from the consultant. The reply stated that my symptoms were “quite unusual” and “might not be related,” even though shortness of breath is listed as a known side effect of the medication occurring in 1 in 100 people that requires immediate medical attention. My concerns relate to clinical judgment, a mismatch between my symptoms and the diagnosis, slow communication, and a lack of recognition of the seriousness of what happened. Clinic never responded to my subsequent customer experience email. Update after customer complaints response: they looked into it, found no breach of prescribing policy and never responded to my email asking what steps they'll take to implement changes that would: 1) improve communication around diagnostic uncertainty, 2) ensure patients with known hypersensitivity have risks discussed in a transparent, thorough, and reassuring manner and 3) ensure timely follow-ups when patients report significant side effects.
★☆☆☆☆ LIPS Healthcare 9 months ago
Booked GP visit online and paid for it online. Recieved a call later to say the time is not available, need to be rescheduled. Received the call again later to be told the slot was already gone need to be rescheduled again. Another call later asking to make a payment via phone, my baby was loudly crying and I said I can’t do it and will do at the clinic, the PA was still pushing me to pay via email. As I booked few days ago I didn’t remember I already made the payment online at the time of booking. They took payment at reception before the appointment. Two days after the visit still haven’t been sent the invoice, referrals discussed with GP or the refund. Very pushy to get the payment, very slow and chaotic with the actual service . Disappointing.
★☆☆☆☆ LIPS Healthcare 8 months ago
Unfortunately I have been let down by the consultants at this clinic. One through no fault of his own and the other through administrative incompetence. I need a PRP injection in my right arm and unfortunately the first consultant I saw is currently signed off sick and unable to perform the injection. I discovered this a month after making the appointment. I was recommended a different consultant whom I arranged an appointment with for today, 3 weeks after first contact. I have travelled to London specifically for this appointment only to be informed this morning that the alternative consultant DOESN'T DO PRP INJECTIONS!!! Why did these two consultants not confer and establish this fact three weeks ago? My time and discomfort is clearly of no interest to them whatsoever.
★☆☆☆☆ LIPS Healthcare 9 months ago
DO NOT USE THIS SERVICE! It's truly the worst I've ever used including the NHS, despite it being private. Having been told by the consultant that I was going to be put in a knee brace ASAP (it will take a week, two max, they said). I then have to chase multiple times, emailing and calling them, and 2 weeks later they say: we have no information about a knee brace being ordered, we'll give you a call by the end of this week to clarify what's going on. So that's 3 weeks just to receive a phone call! I've had a torn PCL which has meant I cannot go into work. I was told it needed to be put in a brace as soon as possible. I tell them this and they say, 'we'll give you a call by the end of this week'. It's Tuesday today... It's outrageous. I can't believe how poorly this has been handled and the lack of urgency from their side (which could have massive implications on people's long term health) is staggering. Stay away and find a clinic that takes your health seriously.
★☆☆☆☆ LIPS Healthcare 10 months ago
Arrogant, unprofessional, and utterly careless toward patients — and the list goes on. I visited this clinic less than two months ago with a minor back injury and nearly ended up being pushed toward unnecessary surgery. A word of advice to the leadership team: instead of spending on PAs and superficial frills, consider hiring competent staff who can actually diagnose and support patients properly. As a local resident, I was genuinely excited to have a clinic so close by, but the experience turned out to be profoundly disappointing. I wouldn’t recommend this place to anyone. THE WORST NEUROSURGEON AND PHYSIOTHERAPIST — yes, every letter deservedly in capitals. This community deserves far better care and far more accountability than what this clinic currently offers.
★☆☆☆☆ LIPS Healthcare a year ago
I came to buy medicine at this pharmacy when my GP told me it was the same price to buy it as it was prescribed. This pharmacy was selling the medicine at over £30 - when I questioned this, they informed me it was this price everywhere and I wasn't going to get a better deal. I trusted them and bought it. I later discovered that at EVERY pharmacy in London it is between £11-14. Furious at this I left a two star review, rating the pharmacy as overpriced. The manager of this pharmacy searched my name from the review and proceeded TO COME TO MY WORK PLACE to tell me to take it down. This was deeply uncomfortable and unprofessional - and caused friction with my management. They pressured me into taking down the post and I was left feeling embarrassed and harassed. I ended up reporting the pharmacy to the board of Pharmacies in the UK. They informed me the behaviour was unacceptable but there was nothing they could do. The whole experience was shocking. I'm only putting my review back up because I have moved jobs to one no longer within walking distance. Do not go here.
★☆☆☆☆ LIPS Healthcare a year ago
They only dispense private prescriptions which they did not state on the phone when I called. Wasted journey.
★☆☆☆☆ LIPS Healthcare a week ago
Would avoid- the way they followed up to receive payment was awful. Wouldnt respond, payment links that didnt work and threatened debt collection.
★☆☆☆☆ LIPS Healthcare 3 weeks ago
Would give 0 stars if I could. Receptionists very rude. Made me wait so long for the first appointment they couldn’t finish it so made to come back for another. Then tried to make me pay a scam charge for something they had never mentioned. …
★☆☆☆☆ LIPS Healthcare 4 weeks ago
I visited the LIPS store inside Battersea Power Station (the pharmacy/clinic space on the upper floor) and I am genuinely appalled by how they treat customers. It was very unprofessional and rude and because of that I didn’t bother wasting …
★☆☆☆☆ LIPS Healthcare a month ago
They have low range of product, almost everything I wanted is missing, do not recommend
★☆☆☆☆ LIPS Healthcare a month ago
In September, I had an appointment with Dr Lau about a cut finger. Looking back, I should have gone to a regular A&E but I was new to Battersea and didn’t know where else to go. While the customer service was nice, it was clear that this clinic is very money-focused and didn’t pay attention to treating my injury properly. They charged me around £300 for the treatment and antibiotics, which I thought was excessive. In the end, I had to go to my home country to get a proper treatment (for free!), because the one that was given to me at LIPS was very bad and could have resulted in me having lasting consequences.
★☆☆☆☆ LIPS Healthcare a month ago
I’d like to first of all say that my review does not reflect the space itself which is very clean or the people who were mostly respectful and nice BUT I have to stand up for myself as a paying individual against misinformation and misleading people when it comes to health costs. I went to LIPS for an ENT appointment. At the time of the appointment, Dr. Wong offered to perform a small procedure (I believe it was a nasal endoscopy but I’m not sure) which took about 5-10 mins. When I inquired as to the cost (as one does because private medical is very costly), he said somewhere in the £150-200 range and I said OK. At the end of the appointment, we settled the price at £495 at the Lips front desk, the initial consultation fee along with the cost of the additional procedure and I paid this the day of the appointment in July 2025 which I confirmed with the Lips team. Feb 2026 (7 MONTHS LATER) I am receiving notice of another £510 facility fee. I expressed my confusion to both Lips and Dr. Wongs team as this was never disclosed to me as an additional cost at the time of the appointment and I am only now alerted to this cost in writing 7 months after my appointment. Had I been notified of a facility fee in addition to the procedure cost I already paid of £495, I would have never agreed to the procedure or even the appointment. I feel very mislead and upset with the whole situation and will say not be returning to LIPS again as I feel they were not cost transparent at all which is so necessary when so many medical facilities can be so predatory towards scared individuals seeking help and assurance.
★☆☆☆☆ LIPS Healthcare 2 years ago
Thank you for sharing your feedback. We are sorry to hear that your experience did not meet your expectations. At LIPS Healthcare, we take all concerns seriously and are keen to understand what went wrong. Please reach out to us at +442071646114 or via email at complaints@lips.org.uk so we can address your concerns directly and work towards a resolution.
★☆☆☆☆ LIPS Healthcare 4 years ago
Thank you for sharing your feedback. We are sorry to hear that your experience did not meet your expectations. At LIPS Healthcare, we take all concerns seriously and are keen to understand what went wrong. Please reach out to us at … More
★★☆☆☆ LIPS Healthcare 2 weeks ago
BUPA Clients Beware – Ask for Costs in Advance and Check Your Emails for debt agency threats! Dr Emma was lovely and the facilities are modern and fresh, but sadly my experience with the administration and billing process was disappointing. I was told my BUPA policy would cover both the consultation and cryotherapy treatment, yet I was later charged an additional £103 without this being clearly explained beforehand. To make matters worse, I later discovered an email in my spam folder threatening debt collection action over an alleged outstanding balance of just £43.60. Given that the clinic had my home address, a formal letter would have been better. A friend of mine also had a similar experience, believing treatment would be covered by BUPA only to later discover additional costs after providing payment details. Overall, the impression was very much “take payment first, explain later,” which left me feeling uncomfortable and unable to trust the billing process.
★★☆☆☆ LIPS Healthcare 2 months ago
Actual in-person treatment and service are good and very personable, but dealing with the admin branch of this company is a nightmare. Was told that my appointments would be fully covered under insurance (AXA), only to bill me multiple times in the following months with zero clarity or explanation on what the payments are for. Can't even pay online on their portal, which has tech issues and keeps glitching. Unhappy.
★★★☆☆ LIPS Healthcare 7 months ago
Great clinic and very competent doctors, however waiting times are 30-90 mins, which is absolutely unacceptable given it’s a private practice.
★★★☆☆ LIPS Healthcare 6 months ago
Vibe above and beyond!
★☆☆☆☆ Chiswick Outpatients a year ago
Sadly I have experienced very poor handling of appointment and during appointment and even after appointment. I was meant to have three appointments back to back and the person organising these over the phone was super helpful. Once I arrived at the clinic late morning around 11:30 I have been told that one of the doctors did not come in. I asked why they could not contact me in the morning? They had no answer. Out of three appointments I ended up with one only and the person showing me to the room not only stayed in the room but did not close the curtains. I had to ask and then the doctor asked her again as well. It was very uncomfortable. After the appointment I never received a letter from the doctor with my diagnosis. I only have the HCA invoice. Trying to call them without any results for 35min- nobody picking up the phone. I feel like they were very efficient in taking my money but badly organised unfriendly and inefficient and untidy with all the rest
★☆☆☆☆ Chiswick Outpatients 3 years ago
Doctors use this place as another hub to see patients. It is also very convenient in terms of location..This could have been a hidden gem - as there is no other similar size clinic around... I am always happy with the doctors' service but not with the other personnel..Especially it is quite a challenge to get some information from the nurses or to make a booking with the other personnel. I couldn't even figure out a protocol to take my diagnostic tests!! Or, I missed the deadline of a test because of lack of information...and I had to attend again..also, I tried to reach the units few times in two different days by phone but could not reach.. They work like a GP instead of a private clinic.. Try to use the other HCA clinics...

★☆☆☆☆ Chiswick Outpatients 4 years ago
This place is a mess! I only come here for doctor appointments, never use them for any tests! The nurse did not follow correct procedures during advanced bloods works. After realising an error was made, nurse did not speak to my doctor or her supervisor. Instead, she acted through her own free will (against all training and regulations), resulting in my doctor making a complaint with the medical director. I then had to come back on another day to repeat the 2.5hr blood tests after fasting yet again. The fact that I was made to wait in the main waiting room between tests with a canula in my arm also shows little regard to health and safety (Covid). My doctor was mortified when informed of what had happened on the day. Never coming back here for tests again. New building but that's all they have going for them. Much better of using Wellington Platinum Medical Centre, never had an issue there.

★☆☆☆☆ The Regenerative Clinic 4 years ago

Understand you guys are busy, but to be 3 weeks behind on sending these out what's said to arrive within 1 hour AND not responding to emails sent, there is no excuse for. Hoping our experience getting tested at your clinic when we arrive is better than what we've seen so far.

★☆☆☆☆ The Regenerative Clinic Edited 4 years ago
UPDATE: On return I had booked my 2 day PCR test that was supposed to arrive on 13th December. It did not arrive, I am once again on the phone with customer services who are struggling to understand what has happened (again) and have no PCR test... DO NOT USE THIS COMPANY Today I upgraded to an express 4-hour test. I took this test at 9am GMT with results expected to be sent by 1pm GMT latest. At 1pm I did not receive my results. I tried to call but there was a very long wait (I was number 79 in the line), so I went back to the local clinic where I had the test in the morning where the manager and her colleague tried their best to help me. They had to call several people and I waited in the clinic over an hour and a half to get to the bottom of what had happened. Simply, my test had not been processed and if I had not gone into the clinic, I have no idea if it would have been. At this point people in my business were waiting for that information with a knock-on impact. Not only that but I had to take 3 hours out of work to deal with the issue and my results were over 2 hours late. I understand there was one other gentleman waiting to get his express test with the same issue, only he was waiting to visit his sick child in hospital and could not do so until the test was returned. I can only imagine how frustrating it was for him. I have emailed and asked for an explanation for the inexcusable experience I have had with this company as well as a full refund. I did not expect to pay almost £400 for something they could not deliver, this is misleading. All this being said, the staff in the Kingston branch could not have been better and I would like to thank them for helping however they could .

★☆☆☆☆ The Regenerative Clinic 4 years ago
My partner booked a day-2 PCR test and he was supposed to go to the clinic in Canary Wharf branch after his trip from France but upon his returned, he was tested positive and to quarantine at home for 10 days. He called the clinic to ask them to mail the PCR kit to him instead with additional cost. They said ok and after like 4 to 5 days quarantined, no package receive and he contacted them again and again and emailed but no reply. After the 10 days quarantined, he went to the clinic to pick up the PCR kit, the guy at the Canary Wharf told him to pick up one kit off the counter. He told the staff he ordered PCR kit and not ANT Kit. The staff brushed him off saying the government has changed to ANT test and there is nothing he can do. How about providing a good customer service and refund the balance to my partner. So he took the ANT kit and did the test and tried to register the result online, The Regenerative Clinic did not even register his email. This is such a bad service and it shows they only care about taking customer's money and not delivering the goods. It is as good as scamming people's money. Too bad I was not around or I would bang the table and ask for a full refund on the spot or will report to the police. Don't ever use their service!
★☆☆☆☆ The Regenerative Clinic 4 years ago
DO NOT USE- I booked a same day results PCR test & didn’t receive my results on the same day so missed my event I required the PCR test for & caused a great deal of stressed. I had to call & email many times & even though they didn’t return my test results in the correct time period I didn’t even receive a full refund
★☆☆☆☆ The Regenerative Clinic 4 years ago
I had my day 2 test done here and was supposed to receive my results within 48 hours. I heard nothing from them so decided to contact them. Someone gave me a call back 9 in the evening explaining to me that I would get it the day after. I received nothing so I contacted them again. Again someone tried to call me 10Pm this time and I chose not to answer. I emailed them and told them I wanted my results emailed to me and that I do not wish anyone to call me in the evening. Someone tried to call me the day after, still no email response. Once again I did not answer. I emailed them for every day for 8 days. The 9th day, I received a text message that said that "my results was on its way to my email box" which I found to be quite funny because if you do send an email, you receive it immediately. It's not a snail mail which you need to wait for several days. Either way, I couldn't care less at this point but I can tell you that nothing happened. I did not receive that email they were refering to after 3 days. So I basically paid for nothing.
★☆☆☆☆ The Regenerative Clinic 4 years ago
Day 2 tests didn't reach us. We enquired with the The Regenerative Clinic. They insisted that they had been delivered, gave us a tracking number and informed us that it was our responsibility to follow up with Royalmail. The check on Royalmail showed that the receipt had been signed with a mock signature + Royalmail states clearly that the sender has to enquire with them about items which were said to have been delivered but not received, not the recipient. We called the clinic again and discussed with them. Only in a third call we were offered in-clinic tests to make up for the misinformation and their attempt to make us follow up with Royalmail when it was their responsibility. Poor service + trying to mislead clients.

★☆☆☆☆ The Regenerative Clinic 5 years ago
Everyone there are amazingly friendly, and I really felt looked after. However, as someone who has stage 4 and 5 osteoarthritis in both hips, I feel that lipogems procedure probably shouldn't have been offered. There seemed to be some confusion around recovery times. At consultation I was told 3 months, a former patient who had success, told me 1-2 weeks, on the day I was told a maximum of 6 months? Doing everything to support my healing, with physio and a low inflammation diet, did cause some easing, however Im unsure if that's the procedures influence. 9months later, I'm back to looking at replacements, due to an increase of pain and lack of mobility. In addition to this the xrays (at my expense) after the procedure showed no improvement. Sadly it would seem I must fall into the 20% that don't have any success. If you have a year to waste and private insurance to foot the bill, then I'd say go for it. However if you're self funding and have quite advanced stages, then I'd say this probably isn't for you. And be mindful that it will be registered as a pre existing condition/treatment on your records, which may implicate further treatments, depending on duration and who you're with. Wanted to share some hindsights honestly so that it maybe become someone's foresight. Best wishes K
★☆☆☆☆ The Regenerative Clinic 4 years ago

★★☆☆☆ The Regenerative Clinic 4 years ago
I got a fit-to-fly PCR test at the Brighton branch and it was fine, all went well, friendly staff, etc. After the test I was handed a 'International Arrivals Day 2 Rapid Antigen Test Kit'. The person who gave me the kit said, "you have to do this test when you get back to the UK" and that was all. I left the test at home so I didn't have to carry it with me in the USA, but took a photo of the box which had a barcode with a 9 digit alphanumeric identifier under it - as I did with all of my other Covid test docs, travel insurance, etc. The night before my return flight when I checked in online, there was a notification from the airline that I had to fill in an online passenger locator form which asked for the day 2 'reference number'. When I entered the number on the box it was rejected and I couldn't complete the form. I couldn't find out why, and the next morning I emailed the Regenerative Clinic but didn't get a reply, then managed to call my wife who found the test kit, read the instructions, paid the fee of £39, and I managed to fill in the form. I wish it had been made clear that all of this had to be done before I returned to the UK. Who needs added stress at a time when travel is incredibly stressful anyway? It took hours for me to sort this out. I went to the Brighton clinic and spoke to a member of staff who apologised for the mix up, but she agreed that the process involving arrivals tests is not very clear. So why wasn't that point made when the test kit was given to me? Altogether I paid £118 for tests, plus additional phone and data costs incurred in the US but words are free to dispense - I would like to have heard more information and instruction when the test was first given to me.

★☆☆☆☆ Grosvenor Gardens Healthcare - Victoria 7 months ago
I booked in for a scan over the phone and agreed to £195 for the appointment. 2 days before, I received a call to say they couldn’t do the scan because I was booked in with a consultant who scans under 12 weeks and I’m further along. That was fine and I was happy to reschedule, but the receptionist did not inform me that the price of the rescheduled scan was £355. I’ve asked for a refund for the difference as I should have been informed and the response of the clinic has been to tell me to go away. When I emailed them to say I wasn’t happy about the fact the price wasn’t communicated to me, I also mentioned that my experience of the scan itself wasn’t good as the consultant pressed very hard on my belly and which left me with some discomfort after the scan. It’s not the end of the world but the response I received was to dismiss the feedback and tell me to go consult my GP?!!? It was super rude. I am hoping they will see this review and rethink their response…
